name: Extended Operations (ETOPS) Status
rem: (O) (M) Authorized by MCC when: 1. Extended Operations (ETOPS) beyond 120 minutes are not authorized, and 2. No specific MEL item applies. Note: MCC will determine the requirements for restricting an aircraft from ETOPS operations. Note: This Item Number is only used for deferring the ETOPS status of the aircraft to 120 minutes or less and is not to be used for deferring the status of any system or component. MCC authorization is required prior to its usage or removal.
oproc: Extended Operations (ETOPS) beyond 120 minutes – Not Authorized.
mproc: 1. Create a new log page in the aircraft logbook using the appropriate MEL Item Number (contact MCC for instructions). 2. The deferral code ETO must also be entered on the log page and the placard when deferring this item. 3. MCC authorization is required prior to its usage or removal. 4. Install a Yellow Placard in the logbook that states: AIRCRAFT IS RESTRICTED FROM ETOPS OPERATIONS BEYOND 120 MINUTES.

name: Extended Operations (ETOPS) Status
rem: (O) (M) Authorized by MCC when: 1. Extended Operations (ETOPS) beyond 180 minutes are not authorized, and 2. No specific MEL item applies. Note: MCC will determine the requirements for restricting an aircraft from ETOPS operations. Note: This Item Number is only used for deferring the ETOPS status of the aircraft to 180 minutes or less and is not to be used for deferring the status of any system or component. MCC authorization is required prior to its usage or removal.
oproc: Extended Operations (ETOPS) beyond 180 minutes – Not Authorized.
mproc: 1. Create a new log page in the aircraft logbook using the appropriate MEL Item Number (contact MCC for instructions). 2. The deferral code ETO must also be entered on the log page and the placard when deferring this item. 3. MCC authorization is required prior to its usage or removal. 4. Install a Yellow Placard in the logbook that states: AIRCRAFT IS RESTRICTED FROM ETOPS OPERATIONS BEYOND 180 MINUTES.

name: Extended Operations (ETOPS) Status
rem: (O) (M) Authorized by MCC when: 1. Extended Operations (ETOPS) beyond 60 minutes are not authorized, and 2. No specific MEL item applies. Note: MCC will determine the requirements for restricting an aircraft from ETOPS operations. Note: This Item Number is only used for deferring the ETOPS status of the aircraft to 60 minutes or less and is not to be used for deferring the status of any system or component. MCC authorization is required prior to its usage or removal.
oproc: Extended Operations (ETOPS) beyond 60 minutes – Not Authorized.
mproc: 1. Create a new log page in the aircraft logbook using the appropriate MEL Item Number (contact MCC for instructions). 2. The deferral code ETO must also be entered on the log page and the placard when deferring this item. 3. MCC authorization is required prior to its usage or removal. 4. Install a Yellow Placard in the logbook that states: AIRCRAFT IS RESTRICTED FROM ETOPS OPERATIONS BEYOND 60 MINUTES.

name: Aircraft Placards/Markings Repair Installed
rem: Item may be used to track repair provided: 1. MCC determines that placard is FAA required, 2. Install repair per TOPP 40-40-05, 3. Enter in log book repair is applied per TOPP 40-40-05, and 4. Enter in log book that repair is deferred per MEL 11-00-01. Note: If MCC determines that placard is not FAA required, use TOPP 40-40-08 (NEF).

name: Fault(s) Indicated by PACK
rem: Note: Dispatch with this maintenance status message displayed on ECAM is permitted without CFDS interrogation.

name: Fault(s) Indicated by ZONE
rem: Note: Dispatch with this maintenance status message displayed on ECAM is permitted without CFDS interrogation.

name: Fault(s) Indicated by AFT
rem: Note: Dispatch with this maintenance status message displayed on ECAM is permitted without CFDS interrogation.

name: Fault(s) Indicated by TEMP
rem: Note: Dispatch with this maintenance status message displayed on ECAM is permitted without CFDS interrogation.

name: Faults Indicated by CPC 1(2) (A321NEO Aircraft Only)
placard: STATUS MESSAGE “PACK ___ ” DISPLAYED MESSAGE “ZONE CONT” DISPLAYED STATUS MESSAGE “AFT CRG HEAT” DISPLAYED STATUS MESSAGE “TEMP CTL ____” DISPLAYED STATUS MESSAGE “CPC ____” DISPLAYED
rem: Note: Dispatch with this maintenance status message displayed on ECAM is permitted without CFDS interrogation.
ptext: STATUS MESSAGE “PACK ___ ” DISPLAYED MESSAGE “ZONE CONT” DISPLAYED STATUS MESSAGE “AFT CRG HEAT” DISPLAYED STATUS MESSAGE “TEMP CTL ____” DISPLAYED STATUS MESSAGE “CPC ____” DISPLAYED

name: Cabin Fans
rem: (M) One may be inoperative provided affected cabin fan is deactivated.
mproc: To deactivate cabin recirculation fans: 1. For the right fan, on the 122 VU panel pull and secure AIR COND/RECIRC FAN/R/SPLY circuit breaker located at W18 and AIR COND/RECIRC FAN/R/CTL circuit breaker located at W20. 2. For the left fan, on the 122VU panel pull and secure AIR COND/RECIRC FAN/L/SPLY circuit breaker located at X18 and AIR COND/RECIRC FAN/L/CTL circuit breaker located at X20

name: OFF Light
placard: FAN PB SWITCH OFF LIGHT INOP
rem: May be inoperative.
ptext: FAN PB SWITCH OFF LIGHT INOP

name: Lavatory and Galley Extraction Fan
rem: NO RELIEF APPROVED.

name: Lavatory and Galley Extraction Fan (ALL A319, A321CEO, and A321NEO A/C and A/C
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Lavatory and galley extraction fan is deactivated, 2. Cabin duct temperatures are available on ECAM, and 3. Flight duration is acceptable to the Captain and Dispatcher.
oproc: 1. Two amber crosses replace the cabin zone temperature indications on the COND SD page. 2. The INOP SYS column on the STATUS SD page may display the LAV DET message. 3. The cabin zone temperature regulation is lost: A. For the A319 and A320 aircraft the Zone Controller commands fixed FWD and AFT cabin ducts temperature to 59°F (15°C). B. For the A321 aircraft the Air Conditioning System Controller commands FWD and AFT cabin ducts temperature using the position of the FWD CABIN selector and the AFT CABIN selector on the AIR COND overhead panel. Slight effect of cabin comfort may occure. 4. Cockpit Temperature Regulation: A. For the A319 and A320 aircraft recover cockpit temperature regulation: i. Set the HOT AIR pb switch to OFF on overhead AIR COND panel. ii. Use the COCKPIT selector on overhead AIR COND panel to control flight deck temperature. B. For the A321 aircraft cockpit temperature regulation is still operative.
mproc: 1. Deactivate the extraction fan by: A. On C/B panel 122VU: pull and secure circuit breaker LAV/GALLEY VENT CTL–EXTRACT FAN–SPLY location U19. B. On C/B panel 122VU: pull and secure circuit breaker LAV/GALLEY VENT CTL–EXTRACT FAN–CTL location U21. Note: Panel 122VU is located behind the first officer on the bulkhead.

name: Blower Fan (Avionics Ventilation)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Extract Fan operates normally, 2. BLOWER pb switch is set to OVRD, 3. Both packs are operative, 4. A placard is placed on the inside of the external power panel door stating BLOWER FAN IS INOPERATIVE AND BOTH PACKS MUST BE OPERATED WHEN ELECTRICAL POWER IS APPLIED TO AIRCRAFT (see (M) procedures), and 5. VENT AVNCS SYS FAULT ECAM caution is not present. AIR CONDITIONING PACKS MUST BE OPERATED DURING GROUND OPERATIONS WITH ELECTRICAL CAUTION POWER APPLIED TO AIRCRAFT (EXCLUDING GROUND SERVICE POWER). Note: If the APU is inoperative, a high pressure cart (engine start cart) must be available to operate the packs.

name: Blower Fan (Avionics Ventilation)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Extract Fan operates normally, 2. BLOWER pb switch is set to OVRD, 3. Both packs are operative, 4. A placard is placed on the inside of the external power panel door stating BLOWER FAN IS INOPERATIVE AND BOTH PACKS MUST BE OPERATED WHEN ELECTRICAL POWER IS APPLIED TO AIRCRAFT (see (M) procedures), and 5. Air conditioning inlet valve is verified open PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required). AIR CONDITIONING PACKS MUST BE OPERATED DURING GROUND OPERATIONS WITH ELECTRICAL CAUTION POWER APPLIED TO AIRCRAFT (EXCLUDING GROUND SERVICE POWER). Note: If the APU is inoperative, a high pressure cart (engine start cart) must be available to operate the packs. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard) PAGE

name: Blower Fan (Avionics Ventilation)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Extract Fan operates normally, 2. BLOWER pb switch is set to OVRD, 3. Both packs are operative, 4. Air conditioning inlet valve is secured open, 5. A placard is placed on the inside of the external power panel door stating BLOWER FAN IS INOPERATIVE AND BOTH PACKS MUST BE OPERATED WHEN ELECTRICAL POWER IS APPLIED TO AIRCRAFT (see (M) procedures), and 6. AEVC test is performed PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required). AIR CONDITIONING PACKS MUST BE OPERATED DURING GROUND OPERATIONS WITH ELECTRICAL CAUTION POWER APPLIED TO AIRCRAFT (EXCLUDING GROUND SERVICE POWER). Note: If the APU is inoperative, a high pressure cart (engine start cart) must be available to operate the packs. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: On the ground: 1. Apply electrical power to aircraft systems. 2. Check that the air conditioning is operating with both PACKS ON. Note: If APU is inoperative, a high pressure cart must be used to run the packs. Note: Packs may be turned OFF to conduct procedures. Example: engine start, de-icing, other procedures requiring single pack operations, etc. 3. Ensure BLOWER pb switch is in the OVRD position. 4. Check that VENT AVNCS SYS FAULT and VENT EXTRACT FAULT cautions are not displayed on ECAM EWD. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Extract Fan
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Blower fan operates normally, 2. EXTRACT p.b. sw. is set at OVRD, 3. Both packs are operative, 4. VENT AVNCS SYS FAULT ECAM caution is not present, 5. A placard is placed on the inside of the external power panel door stating THE EXTRACT FAN IS INOPERATIVE AND BOTH PACKS MUST BE OPERATED WHEN ELECTRICAL POWER IS APPLIED TO AIRCRAFT (see (M) procedure), and 6. Outside air temperature (OAT) and time on ground with electrical power on are limited as follows: OAT < 38°C – No limit OAT 39–45°C – 3 hours OAT 46–50°C – 2 hours OAT 51–54°C – 35 min. Note: If the APU is inoperative, a high pressure cart (engine start cart) must be available to operate the packs.

name: Extract Fan
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Blower Fan operates normally, 2. EXTRACT p.b. sw. is set at OVRD, 3. Both packs are operative, 4. Air conditioning inlet valve is verified open PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required), 5. A placard is placed on the inside of the external power panel door stating THE EXTRACT FAN IS INOPERATIVE AND BOTH PACKS MUST BE OPERATED WHEN ELECTRICAL POWER IS APPLIED TO AIRCRAFT (see (M) procedure), and 6. Outside air temperature (OAT) and time on ground with electrical power on are limited as follows: OAT < 38°C – No limit OAT 39–45°C – 3 hours OAT 46–50°C – 2 hours OAT 51–54°C – 35 min. Note: If the APU is inoperative, a high pressure cart (engine start cart) must be available to operate the packs. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard) PAGE

name: Extract Fan
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Blower Fan operates normally, 2. EXTRACT p.b. sw. is set at OVRD, 3. Both packs are operative, 4. Air conditioning inlet valve is secured open, 5. AEVC test is performed PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required), 6. A placard is placed on the inside of the external power panel door stating THE EXTRACT FAN IS INOPERATIVE AND BOTH PACKS MUST BE OPERATED WHEN ELECTRICAL POWER IS APPLIED TO AIRCRAFT (see (M) procedure), and 7. Outside air temperature (OAT) and time on ground with electrical power on are limited as follows: OAT < 38°C – No limit OAT 39–45°C – 3 hours OAT 46–50°C – 2 hours OAT 51–54°C – 35 min. Note: If the APU is inoperative, a high pressure cart (engine start cart) must be available to operate the packs. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: 1. On the ground: A. Apply electrical power to aircraft systems. B. Check that the air conditioning is operating with both PACKS ON. Note: If APU is inoperative, a high pressure cart must be used to run the packs. Note: Packs may be turned OFF to conduct procedures such as engine start and de-icing. C. Set EXTRACT pb-sw to OVRD. D. Clear VENT EXTRACT FAULT caution displayed on ECAM EWD by pressing ECAM CLR pb. E. Check that VENT AVNCS SYS FAULT caution is not displayed on ECAM EWD. F. Ensure time on ground with electrical power on does not exceed specified limits. 2. After engine start: A. Verify that C/B on panel 121VU EIS/HORN/SPLY location P09 is closed unless horn is deactivated and MEL 23–42–01 is applied. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Skin Exchanger Inlet Bypass Valve
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Valve is secured closed, and 2. AEVC test is performed PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required) Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: 1. After the valve has been secured closed, clear VENT AVNCS SYS FAULT caution displayed on ECAM EWD by pressing ECAM CLR pb. Note: VENT AVNCS SYS FAULT will be displayed after landing.
mproc: 1. Deactivate the SKIN EXCHANGER INLET BYPASS VALVE by performing AMM Task 21-26-00-040-001. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE: 2. Perform operational test of the Avionics Equipment Ventilation (AEVC) using CFDS AIR COND system test via MCDU: Task 21–26–00–710–001: A. Make sure that all the fault messages shown are related to the fault of the skin exchanger inlet-bypass-valve. If other messages are shown, refer to the related MEL valve item. Figure 21-3: Skin Exchanger Bypass Valve CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Skin Air Outlet Valve
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Internal flap (small door) is manually secured to open position, 2. Extract valve (skin air outlet valve) is displayed in partially open position (amber or green) on ECAM CAB PRESS page, 3. Both packs are operative, 4. Skin exchanger isolation valve is secured open, and 5. AEVC test is performed PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required). Note: When ground De–icing procedures are required, contact Maintenance to close the Internal Flap of Skin Air Outlet Valve during De–icing. Flap must be opened after De–icing. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: 1. After the internal flap (small door) has been secured open, clear VENT AVNCS SYS FAULT caution displayed on ECAM EWD by pressing ECAM CLR pb. 2. If VENT SKIN VALVE FAULT is displayed on ECAM EWD, follow the ECAM procedure. Note: ECAM CAB PRESS page will indicate the door at 45 degrees open and amber or green. Note: VENT AVNCS SYS FAULT will be displayed after landing. 3. When ground De–icing procedures are required, contact Maintenance to close the Internal Flap of Skin Air Outlet Valve during De–icing. Flap must be opened after De–icing. Note: Avionics smoke may not be perceptible from the flight deck due to opening of the internal flap of the valve. In case of ECAM warning AVIONICS SMOKE, apply associated ECAM procedure even if smoke is not perceptible.
mproc: 1. To deactivate the Skin Air Outlet Valve perform AMM Task 21-26-00-040-002. 2. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE A. Complete an operational test of the AEVC by performing AMM Task 21-26-00-710-001. Link to Learning Video CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Skin Air Inlet Valve
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Valve is secured closed, 2. AEVC test is performed PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required), 3. Both packs are operative. and 4. Air conditioning inlet valve is verified open PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required). Note: If the APU is inoperative, a high pressure cart (engine start cart) must be available to operate the packs. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Skin Air Inlet Valve
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Valve is secured closed, 2. Deactivate the air conditioning inlet valve in open position, 3. Both packs are operative, and 4. AEVC test is performed PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required). Note: If the APU is inoperative, a high pressure cart (engine start cart) must be available to operate the packs. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: 1. On the ground: A. Apply electrical power to aircraft systems. B. Operate APU BLEED and both air conditioning PACKS ON to provide avionics cooling. Note: If APU is inoperative, a high pressure cart must used to run the packs. Note: Packs may be turned OFF to conduct procedures such as engine start and de-icing. C. Set EXTRACT pb switch to OVRD. D. Check on ECAM CAB PRESS page that the skin air inlet valve is closed. E. Clear VENT AVNCS SYS FAULT caution displayed on ECAM EWD by pressing ECAM CLR pb. 2. After takeoff: A. Set EXTRACT pb switch to AUTO. 3. After landing: A. Set EXTRACT pb switch to OVRD. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Skin Exchanger Isolation Valve (Valve Inoperative Open)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative in open position provided AEVC test is performed PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required). Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Skin Exchanger Isolation Valve (Valve Secured Open)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Valve is secured open, and 2. AEVC test is performed PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required). Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: 1. Clear VENT AVNCS SYS FAULT caution if displayed on ECAM EWD by pressing ECAM CLR pb.
mproc: 1. To verify the skin exchanger isolation valve is in the open position perform AMM Task 21-26-00-040-008. 2. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE: A. Perform operational test of the Avionics Equipment Ventilation (AEVC) using CFDS AIR COND system test AMM Task 21–26–00–710–001: i. Check that all fault messages shown are related to the fault of the skin exchanger isolation valve. If other messages are shown, refer to the related MEL item. 21-26-06B 1. To deactivate the skin exchanger isolation valve perform AMM Task 21-26-00-040-016. 2. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE: A. Perform operational test of the Avionics Equipment Ventilation (AEVC) using CFDS AIR COND system test AMM Task 21–26–00–710–001: i. Check that all fault messages shown are related to the fault of the skin exchanger isolation valve. If other messages are shown, refer to the related MEL item.

name: Skin Exchanger Outlet Bypass Valve
rem: May be inoperative.

name: Air Conditioning Inlet Valve (Avionics Ventilation)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Valve is secured open, 2. Both packs are operative, and 3. AEVC test is performed PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required). Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: 1. After the valve has been secured open, clear VENT AVNCS SYS FAULT caution displayed on ECAM EWD by pressing ECAM CLR pb. Note: Packs may be turned OFF to conduct procedures such as engine start and de-icing.
mproc: 1. To deactivate the air conditioning inlet valve perform AMM Task 21-26-00-040-003. 2. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE: A. Perform operational test of the Avionics Equipment Ventilation (AEVC) using CFDS AIR COND system test via AMM Task 21–26–00–710–001: i. Check that all fault messages shown are related to the fault of the air conditioning inlet valve. If other messages are shown, refer to the related MEL item. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Ventilation Filters
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. Blower and extract fans operate normally, 2. EXTRACT pb switch is set at OVRD, 3. Both packs are operative, and 4. VENT AVNCS SYS FAULT ECAM caution is not present. Note: If the APU is inoperative, a high pressure cart (engine start cart) must be available to operate the packs.

name: Ventilation Filters
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Blower and extract fans operate normally, 2. EXTRACT pb switch is set at OVRD, 3. Both packs are operative, and 4. Air conditioning inlet valve is verified open PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required). Note: If the APU is inoperative, a high pressure cart (engine start cart) must be available to operate the packs. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Ventilation Filters
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Blower and extract fans operate normally, 2. EXTRACT pb switch is set at OVRD, 3. Both packs are operative, 4. Deactivate the Air conditioning inlet valve in the open position per MEL 21-26-08, and 5. AEVC test is performed PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required). Note: If the APU is inoperative, a high pressure cart (engine start cart) must be available to operate the packs. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Avionic Equipment Ventilation Computer (AEVC)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Extract fan is verified to operate normally PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required), 2. BLOWER and EXTRACT pb switches are set to OVRD, 3. Air conditioning inlet valve and extract valve (skin air outlet valve) are verified in the proper position PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required), 4. Both packs are operative, 5. Skin air inlet valve is secured closed, 6. Weather Radar is considered inoperative and deferred per 34-41-01A, and 7. CFDS LAST LEG REPORT and Class 3 Faults is read and faults shown are documented in the Log Book. Note: If the APU is inoperative, a high pressure cart (engine start cart) must be available to operate the packs. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: 1. On the ground: A. Apply electrical power to aircraft systems. B. Operate APU BLEED and both air conditioning PACKS ON to provide avionics cooling. Note: If APU is inoperative, a high pressure cart must used to run the packs. Note: Packs may be turned OFF to conduct procedures such as engine start and de-icing. C. Set BLOWER and EXTRACT pb switches to OVRD. Note: The ground horn may be activated. If so, horn may be silenced by pulling associated C/B on panel 121VU EIS/HORN/SPLY location P09. C/B must be closed again after first engine start. If horn is triggered after landing when engines are shut down, it can be silenced again. In this case, the ADIRS and APU must be switched off before leaving the aircraft. D. Clear VENT AVNCS SYS FAULT caution displayed on ECAM EWD by pressing ECAM CLR pb. 2. In flight: A. In case of pack failure, cabin altitude may reach about 9,700 ft (CAB PR EXCESS CAB ALT caution may be displayed on ECAM EWD). CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: BLOWER FAULT Caution on
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Blower fan is verified operative PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required), 2. VENT AVNCS SYS FAULT caution is available on ECAM, and 3. EXTRACT FAULT caution is not present on ECAM. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: BLOWER FAULT Caution on
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided blower fan is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 21-26-01A, 21-26-01B or 21-26-01C as applicable.
oproc: 1. Disregard VENT BLOWER FAULT caution if displayed on ECAM EWD. 2. Disregard BLOWER pb-sw FAULT light illuminated on VENTILATION overhead panel. Note: Ground horn may be activated. If so, horn may be silenced by pulling C/B on panel 121VU EIS/HORN/ SPLY location P09. C/B must be closed again after engine start. If horn is triggered after landing when engines are shut down, it can be silenced again. In this case, the ADIRS must be switched OFF before leaving the aircraft. 21-26-11B 1. Disregard VENT BLOWER FAULT caution if displayed on ECAM EWD.
mproc: PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE: 1. To verify the blower fan is operating normally perform AMM Task 21-26-00-040-010. 21-26-11B 1. Defer Blower Fan inoperative MEL Item 21-26-01A, 21-26-01B or 21-26-01C as applicable.

name: EXTRACT FAULT Caution on
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Extract fan is verified to operate normally PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required), 2. VENT AVNCS SYS FAULT caution is available on ECAM, and 3. BLOWER FAULT Caution is not present on ECAM. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: EXTRACT FAULT Caution on
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided extract fan is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 21-26-02A, MEL 21-26-02B or MEL 21-26-02C as applicable.
oproc: 1. Disregard VENT EXTRACT FAULT caution if displayed on ECAM EWD. 21-26-12B 1. Disregard VENT EXTRACT FAULT caution if displayed on ECAM EWD.
mproc: 1. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE: A. To verify the extract fan is operating normally perform AMM Task 21-26-00-040-011. 21-26-12B 1. Defer Extract Fan inoperative per MEL Item 21-26-02A, MEL 21-26-02B or MEL 21-26-02C as applicable.

name: FAULT Light EXTRACT pb Switch
rem: May be inoperative.

name: FAULT Light
rem: May be inoperative.

name: ECAM CAB PRESS Indications
rem: INLET and EXTRACT indications may be Inoperative.

name: Avionics Extract/Blower Vibration Monitor Unit
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided AVNCS VENT System operates normally.
oproc: 1. Check that the VENT AVNCS SYS FAULT and VENT EXTRACT FAULT alerts are not displayed on the EWD.

name: Aft Cargo Extraction Fan
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided no live animals or temperature sensitive cargo are carried in the Aft Cargo compartment.
oproc: 1. Place AFT ISOL VALVE pb-sw to OFF. 2. Ensure no live animals or temperature sensitive cargo are carried in the Aft Cargo compartment. Note: Temperature sensitive cargo are materials sensitive to important temperature variations or sensitive to lack of ventilation.

name: Aft Cargo Isolation Valves
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. Both valves are closed, 2. AFT ISOL VALVE FAULT Light operates normally, and 3. No live animals or temperature sensitive cargo are carried in the Aft Cargo compartment. Note: If AFT ISOL VALVE FAULT light illuminates, both isolation valves must be secured closed. Apply MEL Item 21–28–05B.

name: Aft Cargo Isolation Valves
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Both valves are secured closed, and 2. No live animals or temperature sensitive cargo are carried in the Aft Cargo compartment.
oproc: 1. Set the ANN LT switch to TEST and check that the AFT ISOL VALVE FAULT light illuminates, then set the ANN LT switch to BRT or DIM. 2. Set AFT ISOL VALVE pb switch to OFF and check that associated FAULT light is not illuminated. Note: If FAULT light illuminates, the isolation valves must be secured closed. Contact MCC. 3. Ensure no live animals or temperature sensitive cargo are carried in the Aft Cargo compartment. Note: Temperature sensitive cargo are materials sensitive to important temperature variations or sensitive to lack of ventilation. 21-28-05B 1. Set AFT ISOL VALVE pb switch to OFF. 2. Ensure no live animals or temperature sensitive cargo are carried in the Aft Cargo compartment. Note: Temperature sensitive cargo are materials sensitive to important temperature variations or sensitive to lack of ventilation.
mproc: 1. To secure both Aft Cargo Isolation Valves perform AMM Task 21-28-00-040-002.

name: Aft Cargo AFT ISOL VALVE Fault Light
rem: May be inoperative.

name: Automatic Cabin Pressure Control Systems
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. Flight is conducted in an unpressurized configuration, and 2. Extended over water flight is prohibited.

name: Automatic Cabin Pressure Control System No.1 (For all A319, A320, A321CEO, and A321NEO aircraft except Ships
rem: (O) (M) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS), No.1 system may be inoperative provided: 1. CPC 1 is deactivated, 2. Manual Mode is verified to operate normally, 3. Cabin pressure indications are available on ECAM CAB PRESS page in manual mode, 4. Both FCU channels operate normally, 5. System 2 operates normally, and 6. Repairs are made within 3 flight days.

name: Automatic Cabin Pressure Control System No.2 (For all A319, A320, A321CEO, and A321NEO aircraft except Ships
placard: PRESSURE CONTROL SYSTEMS INOP PRESSURE CONTROL No. 1 INOP PRESSURE CONTROL No. 2 INOP
rem: (O) (M) No. 2 only may be inoperative provided: 1. CPC 2 is deactivated, 2. Manual mode is verified to operate normally, 3. Cabin pressure indications are available on ECAM CAB PRESS page in the manual mode, 4. Both FCU channels operate normally, and 5. System 1 operates normally.
oproc: 1. Limit altitude to 10,000 feet for passenger flights. 2. Non-passenger flights above 10,000 feet require flight crews to be on oxygen. 3. MODE SEL – MAN. 4. Check that the ECAM CAB PRESS page shows the outflow valve in the open position. Note: CAB ALT FT indication pulses when cabin altitude is at or above 8800 feet and is red when cabin altitude is at or above 9550 feet (refer to DSC-21-20-40 – ECAM CAB PRESS Page). Note: If cabin altitude goes above 11,300 feet the cabin lights and all cabin signs come on regardless of switch position (refer to DSC-33-40-10 – Overhead Panel). Note: Cabin oxygen mask doors open automatically if the cabin altitude exceeds 14,000 feet (refer to DSC-35-30-10 – Operation). CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE
ptext: PRESSURE CONTROL SYSTEMS INOP PRESSURE CONTROL No. 1 INOP PRESSURE CONTROL No. 2 INOP

name: Manual Cabin Pressure Control System (Outflow Valve MAN Channel, MAN V/ S CTL Sel)
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided both auto cabin pressure control systems are verified to operate normally PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required). Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
mproc: 1. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE A. Verify both cabin pressure control systems are operating normally by performing AMM Task 21-31-00-040-002.

name: Safety Valves
rem: (M) One may be inoperative closed provided: 1. Both automatic cabin pressure control systems operate normally, and 2. The three outflow valve motors operate normally.
mproc: 1. Perform verification test per AMM Task 21-31-00-040-001.

name: AUTO Function
placard: ELEVATION SELECTION SYSTEM AUTO FUNCTION INOP
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided LDG ELEV is set to destination field elevation altitude. Note: This MEL is appropriate when CAB PR LDG ELEV FAULT message is observed and/or the LDG ELEV selector is set to AUTO and the landing field elevation of the FMGS is not available.
oproc: Before Takeoff 1. Set LDG ELEV selector on CABIN PRESS panel to destination airport altitude. 2. Check on ECAM CAB PRESS page that LDG ELEV MAN is displayed. Note: The landing field elevation of the destination airport may not be correctly displayed on the ECAM CAB PRESS page. It is automatically corrected after first engine start.
ptext: ELEVATION SELECTION SYSTEM AUTO FUNCTION INOP

name: MODE SEL FAULT Light
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided SYS 1 and SYS 2 indications on ECAM CAB PRESS page are available.
oproc: 1. If system 1 active (SYS 1 displayed on ECAM CAB PRESS page): A. ON C/B panel 49VU: pull circuit breaker C/B AIR COND/CAB/PRESS/CTL1 location D09. B. Check that SYS 2 is displayed on ECAM CAB PRESS page. C. ON C/B panel 49VU: close circuit breaker C/B AIR COND/CAB/PRESS/CTL1 location D09. 2. If system 2 active (SYS 2 displayed on ECAM CAB PRESS page): A. ON C/B panel 49VU: pull circuit breaker C/B AIR COND/CAB/PRESS/CTL/2 location Y22. B. Check that SYS 1 is displayed on ECAM CAB PRESS page. C. ON C/B panel 49VU: close circuit breaker C/B AIR COND/CAB/PRESS/CTL/2 location Y22.

name: Safety Valves Position Indication on ECAM CAB PRESS Page
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided both safety valves are visually verified closed.
mproc: 1. Verify that both safety valves are closed by performing AMM Task 21-31-00-040-004.

name: SAFETY VALVE OPEN Caution on ECAM
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided both safety valves are visually verified closed.
mproc: 1. Verify that the valves are closed by performing AMM Task 21-31-00-040-005.

name: Aft Cargo Compartment Heating System
rem: (O) Associated hot air pressure regulating valve or trim air valve may be inoperative provided: 1. The heating system is switched OFF, 2. Operative valve is verified closed on ECAM, and 3. No live animals or temperature sensitive cargo are carried in the Aft Cargo compartment.

name: Aft Cargo Compartment Heating System
rem: (O) (M) Associated hot air pressure regulating valve and trim air valve may be inoperative provided: 1. One of these valves is secured closed, and 2. No live animals or temperature sensitive cargo are carried in the Aft Cargo compartment.
oproc: 1. CARGO HEAT HOT AIR – OFF. 2. Check on ECAM COND page that the operative valve (either HOT AIR pressure regulating valve or trim air valve) is indicated closed. 3. Ensure no live animals or temperature sensitive cargo are carried in the Aft Cargo compartment. Note: Temperature sensitive cargo are materials sensitive to important temperature variations or sensitive to lack of ventilation. 21-43-02B 1. CARGO HEAT HOT AIR – OFF. 2. Ensure no live animals or temperature sensitive cargo are carried in the Aft Cargo compartment. Note: Temperature sensitive cargo are materials sensitive to important temperature variations or sensitive to lack of ventilation.
mproc: 1. Deactivate AFT Cargo Compartment – Heating Trim – Air Valve per AMM Task 21-43-00-040-002 or AFT Cargo Compartment – Heating Pressure – Regulating Valve per AMM Task 21-43-00-040-003. Link to Learning Video

name: Pack Flow Control Valves
rem: (M) One may be inoperative provided: 1. Valve is secured closed, and 2. Associated air conditioning pack is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 21-52-01A, 21-52-01B or 21-52-01C as applicable. Theater Restrictions May Apply
mproc: 1. Secure the associated Pack Flow Control Valve closed by performing AMM Task 21-51-00-040-001. 2. Defer the associated air conditioning pack inoperative per MEL 21-52-01A, 21-52-01B or 21-52-01C as applicable.

name: Pack Flow Selections
rem: LO and/or NORM may be inoperative.

name: Pack Flow Selections
placard: PACK FLOW SELECTION INOP PACK FLOW SELECTION INOP
rem: ECON FLOW may be inoperative.
ptext: PACK FLOW SELECTION INOP PACK FLOW SELECTION INOP

name: FAULT Lights
rem: May be inoperative.

name: OFF Lights
placard: ___ PACK FAULT LIGHT INOP PB SWITCH OFF LIGHT INOP
rem: May be inoperative.
ptext: ___ PACK FAULT LIGHT INOP PB SWITCH OFF LIGHT INOP

name: Pack Flow Sensor (A321CEO and A321NEO)
rem: (O) One may be inoperative provided the HOT AIR p.b. switch is selected OFF.
oproc: 1. AIR COND HOT AIR pb-sw placed to OFF. Note: The hot air pressure regulating valve automatically closes and the Flow Control Valve on the affected side operates in pneumatic backup mode. Note: Unequal passengers distribution may cause high temperature in the rear cabin when the hot air pressure regulating valve is closed. Note: The temperature in the cockpit may be low when the hot air pressure regulating valve is closed combined with single pack operation.

name: Packs
rem: (O) One may be inoperative provided: 1. Airplane remains at or below FL310, 2. Upon initial MEL application, MCC shall review the 7 day history, including Post Flight Reports, of the operative pack and pneumatic bleed system associated with the operative pack to ensure no known conditions exist that could affect proper operation of the pressurization system, 3. COND ZONE REGUL FAULT caution is not displayed on ECAM, 4. Affected pack pb switch is set at OFF, 5. Associated pack flow control valve is verified closed on ECAM BLEED page, 6. PACK FLOW selector is set to HI, 7. All Cockpit/Cabin Trim Air Valves operate normally, and 8. Avionics equipment ventilation system operates normally. Note: The above pack valve verification requirement does not apply if pack valve has been secured closed with MEL Item 21–51–01 applied Theater Restrictions May Apply

name: Packs
rem: (O) One may be inoperative provided: 1. Airplane remains at or below FL370, 2. Upon initial MEL application, MCC shall review the 7 day history, including Post Flight Reports, of the operative pack and pneumatic bleed system associated with the operative pack to ensure no known conditions exist that could affect proper operation of the pressurization system, 3. COND ZONE REGUL FAULT caution is not displayed on ECAM, 4. Affected pack pb switch is set at OFF, 5. Associated pack flow control valve is verified closed on ECAM BLEED page, 6. PACK FLOW selector is set to HI, 7. Avionics equipment ventilation system operates normally, 8. Speedbrakes operate normally, and 9. All Cockpit/Cabin Trim Air Valves operate normally. Note: The above pack valve verification requirement does not apply if pack valve has been secured closed with MEL Item 21–51–01 applied. Note: Speedbrakes are comprised of spoilers 2, 3, and 4. Theater Restrictions May Apply PAGE

name: Packs
rem: (O) One may be inoperative provided: 1. Upon initial MEL application, MCC shall review the 7 day history, including Post Flight Reports, of the operative pack and pneumatic bleed system associated with the operative pack to ensure no known conditions exist that could affect proper operation of the pressurization system, 2. COND ZONE REGUL FAULT caution is not displayed on ECAM, 3. Affected pack pb switch is set at OFF, 4. Associated pack flow control valve is verified closed on ECAM BLEED page, 5. PACK FLOW selector is set to HI, 6. Avionics equipment ventilation system operates normally, 7. Speedbrakes operate normally, and 8. All Cockpit/Cabin Trim Air Valves operate normally. Note: The above pack valve verification requirement does not apply if pack valve has been secured closed with MEL Item 21–51–01 applied. Note: Speedbrakes are comprised of spoilers 2, 3, and 4. Theater Restrictions May Apply

name: Packs
rem: (O) (M) One may be inoperative provided: 1. Airplane remains at or below FL310, 2. Upon initial MEL application, MCC shall review the 7 day history, including Post Flight Reports, of the operative pack and pneumatic bleed system associated with the operative pack to ensure no known conditions exist that could affect proper operation of the pressurization system, 3. AIR PACK 1(2), REGUL FAULT, AIR COND CTL 1(2)-A FAULT, and AIR COND CTL 1(2)-B FAULT cautions associated with the operative side are not displayed on ECAM, 4. Affected pack p.b. switch is set to OFF, 5. ECON FLOW p.b. switch is set to OFF, 6. Avionics equipment ventilation system operates normally, and 7. Pack flow control valve is secured closed. Theater Restrictions May Apply PAGE

name: Packs
rem: (O) (M) One may be inoperative provided: 1. Upon initial MEL application, MCC shall review the 7 day history, including Post Flight Reports, of the operative pack and pneumatic bleed system associated with the operative pack to ensure no known conditions exist that could affect proper operation of the pressurization system, 2. AIR PACK 1(2), REGUL FAULT, AIR COND CTL 1(2)-A FAULT, and AIR COND CTL 1(2)-B FAULT cautions associated with the operative side are not displayed on ECAM, 3. Affected pack p.b. switch is set to OFF, 4. ECON FLOW p.b. switch is set to OFF, 5. Avionics equipment ventilation system operates normally, 6. Speedbrakes operate normally, and 7. Pack flow control valve is secured closed. Note: Speedbrakes are comprised of spoilers 2, 3, and 4. Theater Restrictions May Apply

name: Packs
rem: (O) (M) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS), one may be inoperative provided: 1. Airplane remains at or below FL310, 2. Upon initial MEL application, MCC shall review the 7 day history, including Post Flight Reports, of the operative pack and pneumatic bleed system associated with the operative pack to ensure no known conditions exist that could affect proper operation of the pressurization system, 3. AIR PACK 1(2), REGUL FAULT, AIR COND CTL 1(2)-A FAULT, and AIR COND CTL 1(2)-B FAULT cautions associated with the operative side are not displayed on ECAM, 4. Affected pack p.b. switch is set to OFF, 5. PACK FLOW selector is set to HI, 6. Avionics equipment ventilation system operates normally, and 7. Pack flow control valve is secured closed. Theater Restrictions May Apply PAGE

name: Packs
rem: (O) (M) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS), one may be inoperative provided: 1. Upon initial MEL application, MCC shall review the 7 day history, including Post Flight Reports, of the operative pack and pneumatic bleed system associated with the operative pack to ensure no known conditions exist that could affect proper operation of the pressurization system, 2. AIR PACK 1(2), REGUL FAULT, AIR COND CTL 1(2)-A FAULT, and AIR COND CTL 1(2)-B FAULT cautions associated with the operative side are not displayed on ECAM, 3. Affected pack p.b. switch is set to OFF, 4. PACK FLOW selector is set to HI, 5. Avionics equipment ventilation system operates normally, 6. Speedbrakes operate normally, and 7. Pack flow control valve is secured closed. Note: Speedbrakes are comprised of spoilers 2, 3, and 4. Theater Restrictions May Apply

name: Air Cycle Machines (ACM)
rem: (O) One pack may be operated on heat exchanger cooling only provided: 1. Affected pack’s compressor outlet temperature indication is available on ECAM BLEED page, 2. TAT indication is available, 3. Corresponding pack controller operates normally, 4. Affected pack is not used until airborne, 5. TAT is at or below 12°C, (54°F), and 6. Avionics equipment ventilation system operates normally. Note: FL 310 or below must be maintained if normal operating pack fails.

name: Air Cycle Machines (ACM) (A321CEO and A321NEO)
rem: (O) One pack may be operated on heat exchanger cooling only provided: 1. Affected pack’s compressor outlet temperature indication is available on ECAM BLEED page, 2. TAT indication is available, 3. AIR COND CTL 1(2)-A FAULT and AIR COND CTL 1(2)-B FAULT cautions associated with the operative side are not displayed on ECAM, 4. Affected pack is not used until airborne, 5. TAT is at or below 12°C, (54°F), and 6. Avionics equipment ventilation system operates normally. Note: FL 310 or below must be maintained if normal operating pack fails. PAGE

name: Air Cond Pack Ram Air Inlet Flaps (OPEN)
rem: (M) May be inoperative in the open position. Note: Disregard AIR PACK 1(2) REGUL FAULT message if displayed on ECAM.

name: Air Cond Pack Ram Inlet Flaps (CLOSED)
rem: (O) (M) One may be inoperative in the closed position provided the associated pack is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 21-52-01A, 21-52-01B, 21-52-01C, 21-52-01D, 21-52-01E, 21-52-01F or 21-52-01G as applicable. Theater Restrictions May Apply
oproc: 1. Comply with (O) procedures for MEL 21–52–01A, 21-52-01B, 21-52-01C, 21-52-01D, 21-52-01E, 21-52-01F, or 21-52-01G as applicable.
mproc: 1. Deactivate air inlet flap in the open position per AMM Task 21-61-00-040-002. 21-52-02B 1. Defer associated pack per MEL 21-52-01A, 21-52-01B, 21-52-01C, 21-52-01D, 21-52-01E, 21-52-01F or 21-52-01G as applicable.

name: Air Cond Pack Ram Air Outlet Flaps (OPEN)
rem: (M) May be inoperative in the open position provided backlash is verified within limits. Note: Disregard AIR PACK 1(2) REGUL FAULT message if displayed on ECAM.

name: Air Cond Pack Ram Air Outlet Flaps (CLOSED)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative in the closed position provided the associated pack is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 21-52-01A, 21-52-01B, 21-52-01C, 21-52-01D, or 21-52-01E as applicable. Theater Restrictions May Apply
oproc: 1. Comply with MEL 21-52-01A, 21-52-01B, 21-52-01C, 21-52-01D, or 21-52-01E (O) procedure as applicable.
mproc: 1. Deactivate Air Cond Pack Ram Air Outlet Flap in the OPEN position and verify backlash per AMM Task 21-61-00-040-003. 21-52-03B 1. Deferred associated pack per MEL 21-52-01A, 21-52-01B, 21-52-01C, 21-52-01D, or 21-52-01E as applicable.

name: ECAM Emergency RAM Air Inlet Indication (BLEED SD Page)
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided Ram Air Inlet system is verified to operate normally.
mproc: 1. Verify normal operation of the Emergency Ram Air Inlet System by performing AMM Task 21-55-00-040-001.

name: Pack Temperature Control Primary Channels
placard: CONTROL – PACK CONTROL PRIMARY CHANNEL INOP
rem: One may be inoperative. Note: If the primary channel is failed, pack flow is fixed at the value reached at time of failure. If primary and secondary channels are failed, the pack outlet temperature is controlled by the anti–ice valve to a nominal value of 15°C (59°F). Note: Disregard AIR PACK 1(2) REGUL FAULT message if displayed on ECAM.
ptext: CONTROL – PACK CONTROL PRIMARY CHANNEL INOP

name: Turbine Bypass Valves
rem: One may be inoperative. Note: Disregard AIR PACK 1(2) REGUL FAULT message if displayed on ECAM.

name: Primary Channel
rem: May be inoperative provided: 1. HOT AIR pb switch on AIR COND overhead panel is selected to OFF, and 2. Hot air pressure regulating valve is verified closed on ECAM COND page. Note: The message ALTN MODE will appear at top of the ECAM COND page.

name: Secondary Channel
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided the minimum idle on ground function is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 73-20-03A or 73-20-03B as applicable.

name: Primary and Secondary Channels
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Hot Air Pressure Regulating Valve is secured closed, and 2. Minimum idle on ground function is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 73-20-03A or 73-20-03B as applicable. Note: The message PACK REG will appear at top of the ECAM COND page.
oproc: 1. Comply with MEL 73-20-03A or 73-20-03B (O) procedures, as applicable. 21-63-01C 1. Select HOT AIR pb on AIR COND overhead panel– OFF. 2. Comply with MEL 73-20-03A or 73-20-03B (O) procedure, as applicable.
mproc: Defer Minimum Idle on Ground inoperative per MEL 73-20-03A or 73-20-03B, as applicable. 21-63-01C 1. Secure the Hot Air Pressure Regulating Valve closed by performing AMM Task 21-63-00-040-004. 2. Defer Minimum Idle on Ground inoperative per MEL 73-20-03A or 73-20-03B, as applicable

name: Cockpit/Cabin Trim Air Valves
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Affected valve(s) is secured closed, and 2. Both packs operate normally. Note: Unequal passenger distribution may cause high or low temperature in the rear cabin. Adjust the cabin temperature selectors to reach the best compromise temperature between the forward and aft zones. Note: The temperature in the cockpit may be low when the hot air pressure regulating valve is closed combined with single pack operation.

name: Cockpit/Cabin Trim Air Valves
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. HOT AIR pb switch on AIR COND overhead panel is selected OFF, 2. Hot air pressure regulating valve is verified closed on ECAM COND page, and 3. Both packs operate normally. Note: Unequal passenger distribution may cause high or low temperature in the rear cabin. Adjust the cabin temperature selectors to reach the best compromise temperature between the forward and aft zones. Note: The temperature in the cockpit may be low when the hot air pressure regulating valve is closed combined with single pack operation.

name: Cockpit/Cabin Trim Air Valves (A321CEO and A321NEO)
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided associated valve(s) is confirmed closed on ECAM COND page PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Cockpit/Cabin Trim Air Valves (A321CEO and A321NEO)
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided hot air pressure regulating valve is confirmed operative on ECAM COND page PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Cockpit/Cabin Trim Air Valves (A321CEO and A321NEO)
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided the hot air pressure regulating valve is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 21-63-03C, MEL 21-63-03D, or 21-63-03E as applicable. Note: Flight Crew Placard is not authorized if the hot air pressure regulator is deferred per 21-63-03E. PAGE

name: Hot Air Pressure Regulating Valve
rem: (O) May be inoperative in the closed position provided: 1. HOT AIR pb switch on AIR COND overhead panel is selected OFF, and 2. Hot air pressure regulating valve is verified closed on ECAM COND page. Note: Unequal passenger distribution may cause high or low temperature in the rear cabin. Adjust the cabin temperature selectors to reach the best compromise temperature between the forward and aft zones.

name: Hot Air Pressure Regulating Valve
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided valve is secured closed. Note: Unequal passenger distribution may cause high or low temperature in the rear cabin. Adjust the cabin temperature selectors to reach the best compromise temperature between the forward and aft zones.

name: Hot Air Pressure Regulating Valve (A321CEO and A321NEO)
rem: (O) May be inoperative in the closed position provided: 1. HOT AIR pb switch on AIR COND overhead panel is selected OFF, and 2. Hot air pressure regulating valve is verified closed on ECAM COND page. Note: Unequal passenger distribution may cause high or low temperature in the rear cabin. Adjust the cabin temperature selectors to reach the best compromise temperature between the forward and aft zones.

name: Hot Air Pressure Regulating Valve (A321CEO and A321NEO)
rem: (O) May be inoperative in the open position provided: 1. HOT AIR pb switch on AIR COND overhead panel is selected OFF, and 2. All trim air valves are verified closed on ECAM COND page. Note: Unequal passenger distribution may cause high or low temperature in the rear cabin. Adjust the cabin temperature selectors to reach the best compromise temperature between the forward and aft zones. PAGE

name: Hot Air Pressure Regulating Valve (A321CEO and A321NEO)
placard: HOT AIR PRV INOP HOT AIR PRV INOP HOT AIR PRV INOP HOT AIR PRV INOP HOT AIR PRV INOP
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided valve is secured closed. Note: Unequal passenger distribution may cause high or low temperature in the rear cabin. Adjust the cabin temperature selectors to reach the best compromise temperature between the forward and aft zones.
oproc: 1. HOT AIR pb switch on AIR COND overhead panel is selected OFF. 2. On the COND SD page, check that the hot air pressure regulating valve is closed amber. 21-63-03B, 21-63-03E 1. HOT AIR pb switch on AIR COND overhead panel is selected OFF. 21-63-03D 1. HOT AIR pb switch on AIR COND overhead panel is selected OFF. 2. On the COND SD page, check that all trim air valves are checked CLOSED. Note: On the COND SD page, the hot air pressure regulating valve is displayed amber open.
mproc: 1. Secure hot air pressure regulating valve closed by performing AMM Task 21-63-00-040-002.
ptext: HOT AIR PRV INOP HOT AIR PRV INOP HOT AIR PRV INOP HOT AIR PRV INOP HOT AIR PRV INOP

name: Cockpit Heater
rem: (O) One or both may be inoperative.
oproc: 1. Place the associated heater switch to the OFF position. Figure 21-15: Air Cond Panel

name: LLM Autoland Status (Lower Landing Minimums)
rem: (M) Aircraft restricted from autopilot coupled Autoland due to chronic problem, or no Autoland in 30 days. 1. This item number is only used for deferring the Autoland status of the aircraft and MCC authorization is required prior to its usage or removal. 2. Practice Autolands are permitted if authorized by MCC.
mproc: 1. Create a new log page in the aircraft logbook using MEL Item Number 22-00-00L (contact MCC for instructions). 2. The deferral code LLM must also be entered on the log page and the placard when deferring this item. 3. MCC authorization is required prior to its usage or removal.

name: Autopilot Systems
rem: (O) One autopilot system may be inoperative provided: 1. Components associated with the mode or function of the autopilot to be used are operative, 2. When destination weather is less than 4000 RVR, 3/4 mi. one flight director is operative, 3. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, and 4. RNAV operations do not require Autopilot Systems use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements.

name: Autopilot Systems
rem: (O) Both autopilot systems may be inoperative provided: 1. One flight director is operative, unless destination weather is less than 4000 RVR, 3/4 mi. both flight directors must be operative, 2. Both autothrust systems are operative, 3. Aircraft is not dispatched to an airport where the anticipated approach is a non–precision approach unless destination weather is 1000 ft./3 mi. or greater, 4. The airplane remains at or below FL280, and 5. Number of flight segments and segment duration is acceptable to flight crew, 6. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, and 7. RNAV operations do not require Autopilot Systems use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements. Note: Any mode which operates normally may be used. RVSM / PRM Approaches Not Authorized Theater Restrictions May Apply

name: Flight Director Systems
rem: (O) One flight director may be inoperative provided: 1. When destination weather is less than 4000 RVR, 3/4 mi. one autopilot must be functional in the approach mode, and 2. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements.

name: Flight Director Systems
rem: (O) Both flight directors may be inoperative provided: 1. One autopilot is operative unless destination weather is less than 4000 RVR, 3/4 mi. both autopilots must be functional in the approach mode, 2. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, and 3. RNAV operations do not require Flight Director Systems use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements.
oproc: 1. Pilot with the operative flight director must accomplish the takeoff. 2. When destination weather is less than 4000 RVR, 3/4 mi. one autopilot and one flight director must be operative. 22-10-02B 1. The following will not be available on takeoff or a go–around on either PFD: A. No FD bars. B. No A/THR arming. C. Target speed on PFD as selected on the FCU or defaulted to 100 kt. D. No A/THR activation when thrust levers are set to CL detent. 2. When a takeoff or a go–around is performed without FDs proceed as follows: A. Acquire pitch attitude of 17.5 degrees to maintain the speed target (V2+10) with two engines operative. OR B. Acquire pitch to maintain at least V2 (pitch about 12.5 degrees) in case of engine failure. 3. When reaching THR RED ALT and ACCEL ALT: A. Select CLB speed on the FCU. B. Set thrust levers to CL detent. 4. When reaching level off altitude or FL: A. Select CRUISE speed or mach number on FCU. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: AP Disconnect Function
rem: (O) One may be inoperative provided: 1. One flight director is operative, unless destination weather is less than 4000 RVR, 3/4 mi. both flight directors must be operative, 2. Aircraft is not dispatched to an airport where the anticipated approach is a non–precision approach unless destination weather is 1000 ft./3 mi. or greater. 3. Both autothrust systems are operative, 4. Autopilots are not utilized at less than initial approach altitude, 5. Priority function is verified to operate normally before each departure, 6. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, and 7. RNAV operations do not require AP Disconnect Function use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements. PRM Approaches Not Authorized

name: AP Disconnect Function
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Autopilots are not engaged, 2. Priority function is verified to operate normally before each departure, 3. Number of flight segments and segment duration is acceptable to flight crew, 4. The airplane remains at or below FL280, 5. Defer Autopilot Systems per MEL 22-10-01B, 6. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, and 7. RNAV operations not permitted. RVSM Not Authorized

name: Autoland Lights
rem: May be inoperative.

name: Autoland Lights
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements.
oproc: 1. CAT II – Not Authorized. 2. CAT III – Not Authorized. 3. Autoland – Not Authorized.

name: AP Disengagement Warning System
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Both autopilots are not used and deferred per MEL 22-10-01B, 2. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, and 3. The airplane remains at or below FL280. RVSM Not Authorized Theater Restrictions May Apply
oproc: 1. CAT II – Not Authorized. 2. CAT III – Not Authorized. 3. RVSM – Not Authorized. 4. Do not engage autopilots. 5. Comply with MEL Item 22–10–01B (O) procedure.
mproc: 1. Defer autopilots inoperative per MEL Item 22-10-01B.

name: Side Sticks and Rudder Locking Solenoids in AP Mode
rem: (O) May be inoperative unlocked provided: 1. Autopilot Disconnect/Disengagement Warning operates normally, 2. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, and 3. Autoland is not used. Note: If one of the locking solenoids is inoperative in the unlocked position, the affected side stick or pedals will move freely and the “hard point” will not be felt any longer by the pilots. This may result in an unintentional AP disconnect.
oproc: 1. CAT II – Not Authorized. 2. CAT III – Not Authorized. 3. Autoland – Not Authorized.

name: AP/FD TCAS Mode (A319 ships 3101-3102,
rem: (O) May be inoperative.
oproc: In the case of a TCAS Resolution Advisory, the flight crew must apply the basic TCAS procedure. Refer to FCTM, 7.11.3 – The RA Maneuver.

name: Soft Go-Around Function
rem: (O) May be inoperative.
oproc: 1. Maximum thrust is required for Go-Around maneuvers.

name: Autothrust Function (All A319 Aircraft) (All A320 Aircraft) (A321 Aircraft Ships
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. All thrust lever sensors are verified to operate normally, 2. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, and 3. Both autopilot systems are operative. Note: Alpha floor is not available with autothrust function inoperative. Note: Normal takeoff FMA indications will not be displayed with A/THR inoperative.

name: Autothrust Function (A321 Ships 5001- Subsequent)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. All thrust lever sensors are verified to operate normally, 2. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, 3. Both autopilot systems are operative, and 4. Soft Go-Around function is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 22-10-09. Note: Alpha floor is not available with autothrust function inoperative. Note: Normal takeoff FMA indications will not be displayed with A/THR inoperative.
oproc: 1. Check that no ONE TLA FAULT warning is present, (if necessary use RCL pb on ECAM control panel). 2. After engines start, push either autothrottle instinctive disconnect pb for at least 15 seconds. 3. Check that A/THR is displayed in INOP SYS on ECAM STATUS page. A. Refer to Volume 1 A320 5.4.2 Operations with Autothrust OFF Note: The A/THR system is permanently disconnected for the remainder of the flight. All A/THR functions including ALPHA FLOOR are lost. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Autothrust Instinctive Disconnect Switches
rem: (O) One may be inoperative provided ability to disconnect A/THR by means of the remaining Instinctive Disconnect pb and by the FCU A/THR pb, is verified PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: Note: Disregard ECAM warnings. Use the EMER CANCEL pb on ECAM control panel as necessary. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, after IRS alignment: 1. - A. ENG/FADEC GND PWR 1 and 2 – ON. B. THRUST LEVERS – MCT. C. FD – OFF. D. C/B on panel 121 VU AUTO FLT/ FMGC/2 location M17 – PULL. E. A/THR – ON. F. Check that green SPEED and white A/THR indications appear on PFD and that A/THR pb is illuminated. 2. - A. A/THR instinctive disconnect pb switch (non affected throttle) – PUSH. B. Check that the A/THR pb is extinguished, that green SPEED and white A/THR indications are no longer displayed on PFD. C. A/THR – ON. D. Check that the A/THR pb is illuminated and that green SPEED and white A/THR indications are displayed on PFD. E. A/THR (pb on FCU)- OFF. F. Check that the A/THR pb is extinguished, that green SPEED and white A/THR indications are no longer displayed on PFD. G. A/THR – ON. 3. - A. C/B on panel 121 VU AUTO FLT/ FMGC/2 location M17 – PUSH. B. C/B on panel 49 VU AUTO FLT/ FMGC/1 location B02 (H08 on A321NEO) – PULL. C. Wait at least 2 minutes, then repeat the steps listed under No. 2. Of this procedure. 4. - A. A/THR – OFF. B. C/B on panel 49 VU AUTO FLT/ FMGC/1 location B02 (H08 on A321NEO) – PUSH. C. ENG/FADEC GND PWR 1 and 2 – OFF. D. THRUST LEVER – IDLE. E. RCL pb on ECAM control panel – PUSH. F. Wait at least 2 minutes before using MCDU and FD.

name: Autothrust Disengagement Warning System
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Autothrust is disconnected and is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 22-30-01A, and 2. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements. Note: Alpha floor is not available.
oproc: 1. Check that no ONE TLA FAULT warning is present (if necessary, use RCL pb on ECAM control panel). 2. After engines start, push either autothrottle instinctive disconnect pb for at least 15 seconds. 3. Check that A/THR is displayed in INOP SYS on ECAM STATUS page. Note: The A/THR system is permanently disconnected for the remainder of the flight. All A/THR functions including ALPHA FLOOR are lost.
mproc: Defer Autothrust Function inoperative per MEL Item 22-30-01A.

name: System 1
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. System 2 is operative, and 2. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements.

name: System 2
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. System 2 is deactivated in case of actuator leakage, 2. System 1 is operative, and 3. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements.
oproc: CAT III – Not Authorized. 22-63-01B CAT III – Not Authorized.
mproc: 1. Deactivate Yaw Damper Servo – Actuator by performing AMM Task 27-26-00-040-001.

name: FAC 2
rem: (O) (M) FAC 2 may be inoperative provided: 1. Both FCU channels operate normally, 2. ELAC, SEC, ADIRS, SFCC, RA, and LGCIU systems operate normally, 3. Departure weather is 1000 ft./3 mi. or greater, 4. Destination (and alternate if required) station weather is forecast to be 1000 ft./3 mi. or greater at the time of arrival, 5. If, upon arrival, the actual destination/alternate weather is below 1000 ft./3 mi., landing is authorized provided FAC 1 is operational, 6. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, and 7. RNAV operations do not require Autopilot Systems use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements. Note: Loss of FAC 1 will result in Direct Law Mode at landing gear down.
oproc: 1. CAT II/III – Not Authorized. 2. Verify that: A. Departure weather is 1000 ft./3 mi. or greater. B. Destination (and alternate if required) station weather is forecast to be 1000 ft./3 mi. or greater at the time of arrival. 3. Set FAC 2 pb switch to OFF. 4. If, upon arrival, the actual destination/alternate weather is below 1000 ft./3 mi., landing is authorized provided FAC 1 is operational. 5. After engine start: A. Display the ECAM F/CTL page, B. Turn RUD TRIM selector to NOSE/R position and hold until between R 5 and R 10 is achieved, then release and observe rudder displacement on ECAM F/CTL page, C. Push the RUD TRIM/RESET pb and verify rudder trim is centered, D. Turn RUD TRIM selector to NOSE/L position and hold until between L 5 and L 10 is achieved, then release and observe rudder displacement on ECAM F/CTL page, E. Push the RUD TRIM/RESET pb and VERIFY rudder trim is centered. Note: The following will be inoperative: ● Yaw Damper System 2 ● Rudder Trim system 2 ● Rudder Travel Limiter System 2 CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: FAULT Lights
rem: One may be inoperative.

name: OFF Lights
placard: ___ FAC FAULT LIGHT INOP ___ FAC OFF LIGHT INOP
rem: May be inoperative.
ptext: ___ FAC FAULT LIGHT INOP ___ FAC OFF LIGHT INOP

name: Windshear Warning and Flight Guidance System (Reactive)
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided takeoffs, approaches and landings are not conducted when windshear of greater than 15 kts (plus or minus) at or below 1000 ft and/or microburst alerts are in effect for the runway of intended use. Note: For Predictive Windshear Detection and Avoidance System see MEL 34-41-01F.
oproc: 1. Reference Flight Crew Training Manual Chapter 7 for the Windshear Guidance and Windshear Escape Maneuver.

name: FMS 1
rem: (O) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS), may be inoperative provided: 1. FMS 2 is operative, 2. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, and 3. RNAV operations do not require its use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements.

name: FMS 2
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. FMS 1 is operative, 2. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, and 3. RNAV operations do not require its use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements.

name: Performance Information
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided RNAV operations do not require its use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements.

name: Fuel/Time Predictions
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided RNAV operations do not require its use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements.
oproc: 1. CAT III operations - Not Authorized. 2. RNP AR operations - Not Authorized. 3. RNAV 10 operations - Not Authorized. 4. RNP 4 operations - Not Authorized. 5. RNP 2 operations in oceanic and remote continental area - Not Authorized. 6. In case of FMS 1+2 failure In Flight: A. Use MCDU NAV B/UP. See Volume 1 A320 5.11.7 A321/321NEO Backup Navigation (NAV B/UP) Operation. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

rem: (O) May be inoperative.

name: Channels
rem: (O) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS), one may be inoperative provided: 1. 2 RMPs, all DUs, both RAs, both LGCIUs, both FACs, both cabin pressure controllers, the three ADIRS, and standby altimeter (or ISIS Baro- altimeter function) operate normally, and 2. RNAV operations do not require FCU use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements.

name: Channels
rem: (O) One may be inoperative provided: 1. 2 RMPs, all DUs, both RAs, both LGCIUs, both FACs, both cabin pressure controllers, three ADIRS, and standby altimeter (or ISIS Baro- altimeter function) operate normally, 2. RNAV operations do not require FCU use, refer toNavigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements, and 3. Repairs are made within one flight leg.
oproc: RNP AR Operations – Not Authorized RNAV GPS Approaches - Not Authorized

name: Autopilot / Flight Director Related Indications
rem: (O) Indications may be inoperative on PNF FMA provided: 1. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use: refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, and 2. RNAV operations do not require FCU use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements.

name: Autopilot / Flight Director Related Indications
rem: (O) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS), indications may be inoperative on both FMAs provided: 1. Autopilots / Flight Directors are not engaged, 2. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use: refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, and 3. RNAV operations do not require FCU use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements. RVSM Not Authorized

name: Autothrust Related Indications
rem: (O) Indications may be inoperative on PNF FMA provided repairs are made within three flight days.

name: Autothrust Related Indications
rem: (O) (M) Indications may be inoperative on both FMAs provided: 1. Autothrust is disconnected and deferred per MEL 22-30-01A, and 2. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use: refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements. Note: Alpha floor is not available.

name: Approach and Landing Capabilities
rem: (O) One or more may be inoperative on one FMA provided: 1. When destination weather is less than 4000 RVR, 3/4 mi., one autopilot and one flight director must be operative, 2. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use: refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, and 3. RNAV operations do not require FCU use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements. PAGE

name: Approach and Landing Capabilities
rem: (O) One or more may be inoperative on both FMAs provided: 1. When destination weather is less than 4000 RVR, 3/4 mi., one autopilot and one flight director must be operative, 2. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use: refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, and 3. RNAV operations do not require FCU use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements.
oproc: Pilot flying must have operative autopilot / flight director system. RNP AR operations below 0.3 NM – Not Authorized 22-81-02B CAT III operations – Not Authorized CAT II operations – Not Authorized RNP AR operations – Not Authorized RVSM operations – Not Authorized 22-81-02C Pilot flying must have operative FMA. 22-81-02D 1. CAT III – Not Authorized. 2. Check that no ONE TLA FAULT warning is present (if necessary, use RCL pb on ECAM control panel). 3. After engine start, push either autothrottle instinctive disconnect pb for at least 15 seconds. Note: The A/THR system is permanently disconnected for the remainder of the flight. All A/THR functions including ALPHA FLOOR are lost. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Mode Engagement Pb LOC
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided APPR pb operates normally.

name: Mode Engagement Pb
rem: May be inoperative provided procedures do not require its use.

name: Mode Engagement Pb APPR
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. LOC pb operates normally, 2. Destination weather must be 4000 RVR, 3/4 mi. or greater, 3. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, and 4. RNAV operations do not require Mode Engagement Pb APPR use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements.

rem: SPD MACH, HDG TRK, or V/S FPA windows may be inoperative provided associated indications are operative on both PFD’s and both ND’s.

Note: HG (inches of mercury) is required for all operations within North America, Alaska, and Hawaii. Altimeter setting by country may be found in the Jeppesen Airway Manual.

Note: HG (inches of mercury) is required for all operations within North America, Alaska, and Hawaii. Altimeter setting by country may be found in the Jeppesen Airway Manual.

rem: (O) One may be inoperative provided: 1. ND unit associated with the operative ND selector is operative, 2. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, and 3. RNAV operations do not require ND Mode Selector use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements.

name: ADF/VOR Sel

placard: PANEL ___ BARO REFERENCE DISPLAY WINDOW INOP PANEL ___ BARO REFERENCE SEL OUTER RING Hg INOP PANEL ___ BARO REFERENCE SEL OUTER RING hPa INOP PANEL ___ ILS PUSHBUTTON INOP PANEL ___ OPTIONAL DATA DISPLAY PB INOP PANEL ___ ND RANGE SELECTOR INOP PANEL ___ ND MODE SELECTOR INOP PANEL ___ ADF/VOR SELECTOR INOP PANEL DISPLAY PB LIGHT BARS INOP
rem: May be inoperative provided associated indication is available on associated PFD or ND.

name: FMGC 1
rem: (O) (M) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS), may be inoperative provided: 1. FMGC 1 is deactivated. 2. FMGC 2 is operative, 3. Alternate procedures are used, 4. Departure airport weather is at VFR minimums (1000 ft./3 mi. ) and destination airport weather for proposed arrival time is forecast to be and remain within VFR minimums (1000 ft./3 mi.), 5. Associated autopilot is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 22-10-01A, 6. Associated flight director is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 22-10-02A, 7. For A321 aircraft, EGPWS/TAWS function is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 34-48-01F, 8. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, 9. RNAV operations do not require FMGC use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements, and 10. Repairs are made within three flight legs. Note: When FMGC 1 is deactivated, the F/CTL MAINTENANCE message is displayed on the ECAM STATUS page. Note: Refer to Airway Manual, Navigation, Airspace, Class 1 Operations. RNP-10 Operations Not Authorized Theater Restrictions May Apply PAGE

name: FMGC 2
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. FMGC 2 is deactivated, 2. FMGC 1 must be operative, 3. Alternate procedures are used, 4. Departure airport weather is at VFR minimums (1000 ft./3 mi. ) and destination airport weather for proposed arrival time is forecast to be and remain within VFR minimums (1000 ft./3 mi.), 5. Associated autopilot is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 22-10-01A, 6. Associated flight director is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 22-10-02A, 7. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, 8. RNAV operations do not require FMGC use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements, and 9. Repairs are made within three flight legs. Note: Refer to Airway Manual, Navigation, Airspace, Class 1 Operations.. RNP-10 Operations Not Authorized Theater Restrictions May Apply
oproc: 1. Verify that: A. Departure weather is 1000 ft./3 mi. or greater. B. Destination (and alternate if required) station weather is forecast to be 1000 ft./3 mi. or greater at the time of arrival. 2. Remaining FMGC is operative for landing. 3. If, upon arrival, the actual destination/alternate weather is below 1000 ft./3 mi., landing is authorized provided one FMGC is operational. Note: During IR alignment, IRS IN ALIGN memo cannot be displayed until the operational FD is engaged. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

XPDR selection is checked operative on RMP 1. Note: Refer to Airway Manual, Communications, Communication Equipment Requirements chart. RNP-10 Operations Not Authorized

name: RMP 1 Reception Knob (Ships 5029, and 5031- Subsequent)
