rem: (O) HF 1 key ONLY may be inoperative on RMP 2 provided HF radio is not required for flight. Note: Refer to Airway Manual, Communications, Communication Equipment Requirements chart. RNP-10 Operations Not Authorized

name: RMP 2 HF Comm Select Key(s) (For Ships 3162, 3239,
rem: (O) One or both may be inoperative provided affected HF radio is not required for flight. Note: Refer to Airway Manual, Communications, Communication Equipment Requirements chart. RNP-10 Operations Not Authorized

name: RMP 2 Keys (Ships 5029. and 5031- Subsequent)
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. VHF 1 communication is checked operative on one RMP, and 2. If HF is required, HF 1 communication is checked operative on one RMP. Note: Refer to Airway Manual, Communications, Communication Equipment Requirements chart. RNP-10 Operations Not Authorized

name: RMP 2 Reception Knob (Ships 5029. and 5031- Subsequent)
rem: May be inoperative provided: 1. VHF 1 reception knob is operative on one RMP, and 2. If HF is required, HF 1 reception knob is operative on one RMP. Note: Refer to Airway Manual, Communications, Communication Equipment Requirements chart. RNP-10 Operations Not Authorized PAGE

name: ACARS
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided alternate procedures are used. Refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for PBCS Equipment Requirements. Note: Any ACARS function or mode that operates normally may be used. Note: If an Engine Performance Report Form is not available and MCC determines that there is no requirement for ENCON readings during that flight segment, the Engine Performance Report Form may be boarded at the destination. It is desired to have the ENCON data available once per flight day. Note: Refer to Airway Manual, Communications, Communication Equipment Requirements chart. Note: For All A319 and A321 Aircraft and Ships 3271-3278, use ATSU MEL item 46-21-01A.

name: Chime
rem: (O) May be inoperative. Note: For All A319 and A321 Aircraft and Ships 3271-3278, use ATSU MEL item 46-21-01B.

name: Message Storage
rem: (O) May be inoperative. Note: For All A319 and A321 Aircraft and Ships 3271-3278, use ATSU MEL item 46-21-01C.

name: UTC Time Reset
rem: (O) May be inoperative. Note: For All A319 and A321 Aircraft and Ships 3271-3278, use ATSU MEL item 46-21-01D.

name: Out, Off, On, In Times Function
rem: (O) May be inoperative. Note: For All A319 and A321 Aircraft and Ships 3271-3278, use ATSU MEL item 46-21-01E.

name: Weight Data Page
rem: (O) May be inoperative. Note: For All A319 and A321 Aircraft and Ships 3271-3278, use ATSU MEL item 46-21-01F. PAGE

name: Satellite Communication System (A321NEO Ships 5001-5050, 5057-5071, 5089 and Subsequent) (A321NEO Ships 5051-5056 and 5072-5088 Post AA
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided alternate procedures are used. Refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for PBCS Equipment Requirements. Note: Refer to Airway Manual, Communications, Communication Equipment Requirements chart. Note: CPDLC-DCL function will operate in the VHF environment.

name: Voice Channels (A321NEO Ships 5001-5050, 5057-5071, 5089 and Subsequent) (A321NEO Ships 5051-5056 and 5072-5088 Post AA
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided alternate procedures are used. Note: Refer to Airway Manual, Communications, Communication Equipment Requirements chart.

name: Data Channel (A321NEO Ships 5001-5050, 5057-5071, 5089 and Subsequent) (A321NEO Ships 5051-5056 and 5072-5088 Post AA
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided alternate procedures are used. Refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for PBCS Equipment Requirements. Note: Refer to Airway Manual, Communications, Communication Equipment Requirements chart. Note: CPDLC-DCL function will operate in the VHF environment.
oproc: Use VHF or HF for communications. 23-28-01B Use VHF, HF or Satellite Data Link ATSU, ADS and CPDLC for communications. 23-28-01C Use VHF, HF or Satellite Voice for communications.

name: Passenger Address System
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. Alternate, normal and emergency procedures and/or operating restrictions are used, 2. Flight Attendant alerting system (audio and visual) operates normally, and 3. Attendant Indication Panels (AIP) operates normally. Note: Any station function(s) that operates normally may be used.

name: Lavatory Speakers
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided alternate procedures are used.

name: Cabin Speakers
placard: ___ PA SYSTEM INOP PA LAVATORY SPEAKERS INOP PA CABIN SPEAKERS INOP
rem: May be inoperative provided inoperative speakers are not adjacent to each other.
oproc: 1. Advise the flight attendants to make all passenger briefings, and the Lead Flight Attendant/Purser to advise the captain, prior to departure, when the briefings are complete. 2. To accommodate ditching or evacuation, the captain must instruct the Lead Flight Attendant/ Purser on the interphone to make the necessary calls using the aircraft megaphones or other methods. 3. Refer to QRH/ABN–ABN COM PA Inoperative or QRH/ABN–ABN COM Interphone Inoperative for Alternate Procedures for PA or Interphone Inoperative. 23-31-01B Captain to brief Lead Flight Attendant/Purser to notify occupant(s) of affected lavatory(s) of any emergency or safety of flight announcements.
ptext: ___ PA SYSTEM INOP PA LAVATORY SPEAKERS INOP PA CABIN SPEAKERS INOP

name: In-flight Entertainment (IFE) System (System Deactivated)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided system is deactivated. Note: Approval for flight must be obtained by the MCC Shift Manager.

name: In-flight Entertainment (IFE) System (Partially Operative)
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided alternate procedures are used. Note: This item is for use when the IFE system is partially inoperative (i.e., zone inop, software load corrupt). For individual IFE components see the Nonessential Equipment and Furnishings List (NEF) for deferral. Note: Any part of the system that is operational may be used.
oproc: Captain to brief Lead Flight Attendant that it will be necessary to do passenger briefings and emergency equipment demonstrations verbally.
mproc: 1. To deactivate the IFE system: A. For the A319 aircraft pull and secure the IFE CONTROL circuit breaker on the 122VU panel located at Z11. B. For the A320 aircraft pull and secure the IFE CONTROL circuit breaker on the 2000VU panel located at A08. C. For the A321 aircraft pull and secure the IFE-VCC-DC circuit breaker on the 2000VU panel located at C01.

name: Ground External Horn
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided avionics equipment ventilation is monitored in the cockpit while aircraft is on the ground and aircraft electrical network is supplied (not required when operating on Maint Bus). Note: When the flight crew leaves the aircraft, maintenance must monitor the APU and/ or avionics ventilation. If maintenance is not available, the flight crew must shut down the APU, ADIRS and de–select main electrical power from the aircraft. Note: The Maint Bus Switch can be selected before leaving the aircraft to provide power to the ground service busses. Coordinate with station to determine if this is required.
oproc: 1. Flight crew or maintenance personnel to monitor APU on the flight deck during operation. 2. Avionic ventilation must be monitored on the flight deck, by flight crew or maintenance personnel for ECAM alerts, when aircraft main electrical power is supplied (not required when operating on Maint Bus). 3. When the flight crew leaves the aircraft, maintenance must monitor the APU and/or avionics ventilation. If maintenance is not available, the flight crew must: ADIRS – OFF. APU – OFF. EXT PWR – OFF. Note: The Maint Bus Switch can be selected before leaving the aircraft to provide power to the ground service busses. Coordinate with station to determine if this is required.
mproc: 1. Pull and secure the EIS/HORN/SPLY circuit breaker located on the 121VU panel at P09.

name: Flight Attendant Call Lights
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. The PA system is operative, 2. Affected light is not used for Lavatory Smoke Detector Alerting, and 3. Alternate procedures for contacting flight attendants are used. Note: The flight attendant chime must always be operative. Note: Passenger to Attendant Call System is considered an NEF item. See NEF item 33–24–09. Note: Any visual alerting system function(s) that operate(s) normally may be used.
oproc: Captain to brief Lead Flight Attendant/Purser which light(s) is inoperative and that PA, interphone or face–to–face communications is required at all affected locations. Note: Chime is still operative.

name: Flight Deck To Ground Interphone System
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided alternate procedures are used.

name: External Power Panel Call Light
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided alternate procedures are used.

name: External Power Panel Call Switch
placard: TO GROUND INTERPHONE SYSTEM INOP POWER PANEL CALL LIGHT INOP POWER PANEL CALL SWITCH INOP
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided alternate procedures are used.
oproc: Use standard ramp hand signals. Reference FOM Chapter 30 Ramp Operations. 23-43-01B, 23-43-01C Use standard ramp hand signals to notify ground crew of interphone call. Reference FOM Chapter 30 Ramp Operations.
ptext: TO GROUND INTERPHONE SYSTEM INOP POWER PANEL CALL LIGHT INOP POWER PANEL CALL SWITCH INOP

name: Cabin to Cabin Function
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided alternate communications procedures between the affected flight attendants stations are used. Note: Any station function(s) that operate normally may be used.
oproc: Captain to brief Lead Flight Attendant/Purser that communication between flight attendants is via operative positions, PA or face to face.

name: Flight Deck Handset
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. Flight deck to cabin communications operates normally, and 2. Alternate procedures are used.

name: Cabin Attendant Handsets
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. One forward and one aft handset operate normally, 2. Operative handsets are located at operative flight attendant seats, and 3. Alternate communications procedures between the affected Flight Attendant stations are used. Note: Any operative handset at an inoperative flight attendant seat shall not be counted to satisfy this requirement. Note: Any handset(s) function(s) that operate normally may be used. Note: Handsets may be relocated to meet the requirements of the Remarks & Exceptions column.

name: Cabin Attendant Handsets
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. One forward and one additional handset operate normally, 2. Operative handsets are located at operative flight attendant seats, and 3. Alternate communications procedures between the affected Flight Attendant stations are used. Note: Any operative handset at an inoperative flight attendant seat shall not be counted to satisfy this requirement. Note: Any handset(s) function(s) that operate normally may be used. Note: Handsets may be relocated to meet the requirements of the Remarks & Exceptions column.
oproc: Use the ACP for PA announcements. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Service Interphone Jack System (Maintenance Outlets)
rem: One or more may be inoperative.

name: SELCAL (AMU)
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided ACARS/ATSU are operative.

name: SECAL (AMU)
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided flight crew continuously monitors appropriate radio frequencies.
oproc: 1. If on an HF route, maintain continuous listening watch on appropriate HF frequency. 23-51-01B 1. Maintain a listening watch on the appropriate VHF frequencies. 2. If on an HF route, maintain continuous listening watch on appropriate HF frequency.

name: CAPT and F/O
rem: (O) One may be inoperative provided: 1. ACP 3 operates normally, and 2. AUDIO SWITCHING selector operates normally.

name: ACP 3
rem: (M) ACP 3 may be inoperative provided: 1. Primary observers sliding seat is deferred per MEL 25-11-06A, 25-11-06B, or 25-11-06C as appropriate, and 2. Repairs are made within two flight days.

name: ATT Call Lights (Flight Deck Call Lights/CAPT and F/O
rem: One or both may be inoperative provided the flight deck buzzer is operative. Note: The flight deck buzzer must always be operative.

name: Transmission key(s)
rem: One may be inoperative on each ACP provided: 1. VHF 1 transmission key operates normally on either CAPT ACP or F/O ACP, and 2. If HF 1 is required: HF 1 transmission key operates normally on either CAPT ACP or F/O ACP.

name: Reception Knob(s)
rem: One may be inoperative on each ACP provided: 1. VHF 1 reception knob operates normally on either CAPT ACP or F/O ACP, and 2. If HF 1 is required: HF 1 reception knob operates normally on either CAPT ACP or F/O ACP.

name: Reception Knob Light(s)
placard: AUDIO CONTROL PANEL INOP CONTROL PANEL (ACP 3) INOP DECK ATT CALL LIGHT INOP ACP TRANSMISSION KEY(S) INOP ACP RECEPTION KNOB(S) INOP RECEPTION KNOB LIGHT(S) INOP
rem: May be inoperative.
oproc: Utilize ACP 3 by placing AUDIO SWITCHING selector to CAPT 3 or F/O 3 as required. Note: It is the captain’s discretion (workload management) as to whether or not Cabin Medical Communication is practical. Refer to AOM Vol 1, Supplementary Procedures – Cabin Medical Communication System. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE
ptext: AUDIO CONTROL PANEL INOP CONTROL PANEL (ACP 3) INOP DECK ATT CALL LIGHT INOP ACP TRANSMISSION KEY(S) INOP ACP RECEPTION KNOB(S) INOP RECEPTION KNOB LIGHT(S) INOP

rem: Must operate in NORM position.

rem: May be inoperative provided: 1. Associated hand microphone is installed and operates normally, 2. Flight Data Recorder (FDR) operates normally, and 3. Repairs are made within three flight days.

rem: Either the Captain’s or F/O’s earphone/headphone may be inoperative provided associated flight deck speaker operates normally.

name: CAPT/F/O
rem: May be inoperative provided associated boom microphones operate normally.

placard: HAND MICROPHONE INOP HAND MICROPHONE INOP OR MISSING

name: Cockpit Speakers - F/O Speaker (Speaker 2 Only) (All A319 aircraft) (All A320 aircraft) (All A321CEO aircraft) (A321NEO aircraft
rem: (M) Only F/O's loudspeaker (speaker number 2) may be inoperative provided at least one crewmember on flight deck duty wears a headset.

name: Cockpit Speakers (A321NEO aircraft 5029 and Subsequent)
rem: One may be inoperative provided crewmember on affected side wears a headset for entire flight.

name: Cockpit Speakers (A321NEO aircraft 5029 and Subsequent)
placard: SPEAKER (SPEAKER 2) INOP COCKPIT SPEAKER INOP COCKPIT SPEAKERS INOP
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. Both crewmembers wear a headset for entire flight, and 2. Observers headset is checked operative.
oproc: 1. Check that the third occupant boomset is operative.
mproc: 1. Deactivate the F/O speaker as follows: A. On C/B panel 121VU: pull and secure circuit breaker EIS/F/O/LOUD/SPKR location P08 located behind the F/O on rear bulkhead.
ptext: SPEAKER (SPEAKER 2) INOP COCKPIT SPEAKER INOP COCKPIT SPEAKERS INOP

name: Side Stick Radio Selector (PTT) (Transmit Switch) (All A319 aircraft) (All A320 aircraft) (All A321CEO aircraft) (A321NEO ships 5001-5028)
rem: May be inoperative in open/neutral position (non transmitting position) provided INT/RAD switches on CAPT ACP, F/O ACP and ACP3 operate normally.

name: Side Stick Radio Selector (PTT) (Transmit Switch) (All A319 aircraft) (All A320 aircraft) (All A321CEO aircraft) (A321NEO ships 5001-5028)
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. INT/RAD switches on CAPT ACP, F/O ACP and ACP3 operate normally, and 2. Affected switch is deactivated in open/neutral position (non transmitting position).

name: Side Stick Radio Selector (PTT) (Transmit Switch) (A321NEO ships 5029 and Subsequent)
rem: May be inoperative in open/neutral position (non-transmitting position) provided INT/RAD switches on RMP1, on RMP2 and on ACP 3 are operative.

name: Side Stick Radio Selector (PTT) (Transmit Switch) (A321NEO ships 5029 and Subsequent)
placard: TRANSMIT SWITCH INOP TRANSMIT SWITCH INOP TRANSMIT SWITCH INOP TRANSMIT SWITCH INOP
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. INT/RAD switches on RMP 1, RMP 2, and ACP3 operate normally, and 2. Affected switch is deactivated in open/neutral position (non-transmitting position).
mproc: Deactivate the side stick radio PTT switch per AMM Task 23-51-00-040-001.
ptext: TRANSMIT SWITCH INOP TRANSMIT SWITCH INOP TRANSMIT SWITCH INOP TRANSMIT SWITCH INOP

name: Cockpit Voice Recorder System (CVR)
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. The flight data recorder (FDR) operates normally, and 2. Repairs are made within three flight days.
mproc: Note: A321NEO aircraft has Airbus MOD 165565 embodied which incorporate two CVDR's for the Cockpit Voice Recorder (CVR) and the Digital Flight Data Recorder (DFDR) functions. Delta is not authorized by the FAA to consider the two CVDR's as independent systems, currently both units are consided as one system. If the RECORDER CVR FAULT is displayed the FDR function is considered to be operative as long as the RECORDER FDR FAULT is not displayed.

name: RCDR/GND CTL pb-sw
rem: (O) May be inoperative in AUTO position provided repairs are made within three flight days.

name: RCDR/GND CTL pb-sw (Inoperative ON)
rem: (O) May be inoperative in ON position.

name: RCDR/GND CTL pb-sw
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. CVR is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 23-71-01 2. The flight data recorder (FDR) operates normally, and 3. Repairs are made within three flight days.

name: RCDR/GND CTL pb-sw ON Light
rem: May be inoperative.

name: CVR Erase p.b.
rem: May be inoperative.

name: CVR TEST p.b.
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. CVR is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 23-71-01, 2. The flight data recorder (FDR) operates normally, and 3. Repairs are made within three flight days.
oproc: On ground: CVR can only be tested through the CVR test pb during the first five minutes after supply of the aircraft electrical network. 23-71-02B After landing: If needed, CVR recording can only be stopped by pulling C/B on panel 49 VU COM/CVR/SPLY location E14. Note: Normally CVR stops recording five minutes after second engine is shut down. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Viewing Ports
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. Alternate procedures are used, and 2. Repairs are made within three flight days.
oproc: 1. Captain to brief Lead Flight Attendant/Purser that flight deck door view port is inoperative/unusable. 2. Crewmember will use interphone to notify flight deck of desired access. 3. Communicate via interphone to coordinate flight deck entry/exit.

name: Cabin Inter-communication Data System (CID) - CIDS Director
rem: (M) One may be inoperative. Note: Failure of a single CIDS director is indicated by a MAINTENANCE message on the STATUS SD page. (Refer to MEL 23-00-00) Fault(s) Indicated by CIDS 1(2). Note: In the case of disturbance of the CIDS function, the deactivation/removal of the affected CIDS director may recover normal operation of the CIDS function. Refer to AMM TASK 23-73-00-040-001.
mproc: Deactivate the CIDS Director per AMM Task 23-73-00-040-001. Note: After accessing the AMM Task, ensure the appropriate filter is applied by selecting the funnel icon located at the top of the page.

name: Cabin Inter-communication Data System (CIDS)
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. No two consecutive or adjacent DEU’s are verified to be inoperative, and 2. Lavatory speaker is verified to operate normally, or return to seat sign is verified to operate normally for an associated inoperative DEU. Note: The following items may be affected: Loudspeakers, No Smoking/Fasten Seat Belt Signs, general illumination, and Passenger Call.

name: Cabin Inter-communication Data System (CIDS) CIDS
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. No two consecutive or adjacent DEU’s are verified to be inoperative, and 2. Lavatory door is locked closed and placarded “UNSERVICEABLE–DO NOT USE” for an inoperative DEU affecting a lavatory. Note: These provisos are not intended to prohibit lavatory use or inspections by crewmembers. Ensure lavatory door is locked closed after crewmember inspection. Note: The following items may be affected in cabin and lavatory: Loudspeakers, No Smoking/ Fasten Seat Belt Signs, general illumination, and Passenger Call.
oproc: 1. Captain to brief Lead Flight Attendant/Purser that the affected lavatory door is to remain locked and not entered except for use or inspection by crewmembers. 2. Captain is to ensure the lavatory door is locked closed if crewmember inspection has been performed or if the lavatory is used by a crewmember.
mproc: 1. Locate the inoperative DEU-A(s) using the below graphics. 2. Verify that no two consecutive or adjacent DEU’s are inoperative. 3. If a Lavatory is affected by the inoperative DEU, ensure the lavatory speaker operates normally, or the return to seat sign operates normally for the associated inoperative DEU. 4. Verify operation of Loudspeakers, No Smoking/Fasten Seat Belt Signs, General Illumination and Passenger Call. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: CIDS Decoder/Encoder Unit
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. DEU B and the associated handset are operative at each pair of floor level exit doors, and 2. Alternate procedures are used. Note: The following items may be affected: Cabin attendant station intercommunications, Emergency Overwing Exit slide bottle pressure indications on PTP, waste system control, lavatory control, and lavatory smoke detection system.

name: Attendant Indication Panels
rem: (O) May be inoperative at a cabin attendant station provided: 1. Corresponding area call panel(s) operates normally, 2. Passenger address and cabin interphone at affected station(s) operates normally, and 3. Alternate procedures are used.

name: Area Call Panel
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. Corresponding Attendant Indication panel(s) (AIP) operates normally, 2. Passenger address and cabin interphone at affected station(s) operates normally, and 3. Alternate procedures are used.

name: Area Call Panel

rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. Corresponding Attendant Indication panel(s) (AIP) operates normally, 2. Passenger address and cabin interphone at affected station(s) operates normally, and 3. Alternate procedures are used.

PTP messages are checked PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required). Note: LIGHT EMER pb must always be operative. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Cabin Lighting Control Functions on FAP
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided lighting is sufficient for cabin attendants to perform their duties. .

rem: (O) May be inoperative provided alternate procedures are used. Note: LIGHT EMER pb must always be operative

Each lavatory flushes properly PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE. 23-73-04B Captain to brief Lead Flight Attendant/Purser to ensure cabin lighting is sufficient for cabin attendants to perform their duties. name: Programmed Cabin Assignment Module (CAM)
rem: May be inoperative.

name: Programming and Test Panel

PTP system emergency light test is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 23-73-07B, and 4. Slide bottle pressure indication on PTP for overwing emergency exits is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 23-73-07C.

name: SYS EMER LIGHT TEST
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided emergency lights are verified to operate normally PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required). Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Slide Bottle Pressure Indication (on PTP) for Overwing Emergency Exits
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided associated slide bottle pressure is verified before the FIRST FLIGHT OF EACH DAY (Log Book Entry Required). Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Drain Masts (Indication on
rem: May be inoperative. Note: For Drain Mast Heaters being inoperative refer to MEL 30-71-01.

mproc: 1. Defer PTP SYS EMER LIGHT TEST per MEL 23-73-07B 2. Defer Slide Bottle Pressure Indication for Overwing Emergency Exits inoperative per MEL 23-73-07C. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

SYSTEM INOP light on WATER WASTE panel on FAP is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 23-73-08C. Note: EMER p.b. must always be operative.

name: Cabin Lighting Control Functions on FAP
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided lighting is sufficient for cabin attendants to perform their duties.

name: SYSTEM INOP Light on WATER WASTE Panel on FAP
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided alternate procedures are used.

ptext: STATUS MESSAGE “DC BUS TIE” DISPLAYED STATUS MESSAGE “GPCU” OR “AC GEN” DISPLAYED

name: Engine Driven Generator Channel (IDG, GCU, Line Contactor)
rem: (O) (M) One may be inoperative provided: 1. APU generator operates normally and is used throughout flight, 2. The APU oil quantity is checked PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required), 3. APU and remaining (operative) IDG Log Book history for past 30 days is reviewed and approved by MCC prior to deferral, 4. All busses can be powered, 5. All indications and warnings associated with the remaining engine and APU driven generator channels operate normally, 6. Galley automatic shedding is verified to operate normally, 7. Aircraft remains at or below FL330, 8. If the APU is to be used as an alternate power source and has been operating for extended period of time in heavy falling or blowing snow, inspect the inlet plenum to ensure that accumulations of snow or ice do not exist PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, and 9. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements. Note: This item is AD related. Theater Restrictions May Apply Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard) PAGE

name: Engine Driven Generator Channel (IDG, GCU, Line Contactor)
rem: (O) (M) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS), one may be inoperative provided: 1. APU generator operates normally and is used throughout flight, 2. The APU oil quantity is checked PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required), 3. APU and remaining (operative) IDG Log Book history for past 30 days is reviewed and approved by MCC prior to deferral, 4. All busses can be powered, 5. All indications and warnings associated with the remaining engine and APU driven generator channels operate normally, 6. Galley automatic shedding is verified to operate normally, 7. Aircraft remains at or below FL330, 8. Both IDG heat exchangers bypass valves of the remaining AC main generation are checked PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, 9. If the associated IDG is disconnected, the wing tank on the affect side is checked to be above -30°C before takeoff, 10. If the associated IDG is disconnected, the wing tank fuel temperature is monitored inflight and checked to be at or above -38°C, 11. If the APU is to be used as an alternate power source and has been operating for extended period of time in heavy falling or blowing snow, inspect the inlet plenum to ensure that accumulations of snow or ice do not exist PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, and 12. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements. Note: This item is AD related. Theater Restrictions May Apply Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: APU Generator Channel (APU Pneumatic Operative)
rem: (O) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS) beyond 120 minutes, may be inoperative provided: 1. APU GEN pb-sw set to OFF, 2. External power is available for engine start, and 3. Repairs are made within four flight legs. Note: When GPCU/Ground Power Control Function of the GAPCU and APU generator are both inoperative, engines cannot be started. Theater Restrictions May Apply

name: APU Generator Channel (APU Pneumatic Operative)
rem: (O) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS), may be inoperative provided: 1. APU GEN pb switch set to OFF, and 2. External power is available for engine start. Note: When GPCU/Ground Power Control Function of the GAPCU and APU generator are both inoperative, engines cannot be started. Theater Restrictions May Apply

name: APU Generator Channel (APU Considered Inop)
rem: (O) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS), may be inoperative provided APU is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 49-10-01A or 49-10-01B as applicable. Note: When GPCU/Ground Power Control Function of the GAPCU and APU generator are both inoperative, engines cannot be started. Note: Two air carts required for engine start, unless a single cart is high volume output. Theater Restrictions May Apply

name: APU Generator Channel (APU Generator Deactivated)
rem: (O) (M) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS), maybe inoperative provided the APU generator is deactivated. Note: When GPCU/Ground Power Control Function of the GAPCU and APU generator are both inoperative, engines cannot be started. Theater Restrictions May Apply PAGE

name: Manual Transfer to the AC BUS 2 (ALTN Function)
rem: (O) ALTN function may be inoperative provided: 1. AC ESS FEED pb switch is set at NORM position, 2. It is checked on the ELEC SD page that the AC BUS 1 supplies the AC ESS BUS, and 3. TR 2 operates normally.

name: Automatic Transfer to AC
placard: ESS FEED ALTN FUNCTION INOP ESS FEED ALTN FUNCTION INOP
rem: (O) ALTN function may be inoperative provided: 1. AC ESS FEED pb switch is set at NORM position, 2. It is checked on the ELEC SD page that the AC BUS 1 supplies the AC ESS BUS, and 3. TR 2 operates normally.
oproc: On ECAM ELEC page, check that the AC BUS 1 supplies the AC ESS BUS.
ptext: ESS FEED ALTN FUNCTION INOP ESS FEED ALTN FUNCTION INOP

name: IDG FAULT Lights
rem: One may be inoperative provided frequency and temperature indications are available on ECAM ELEC page and are monitored.

name: FAULT Lights
rem: One GEN and/or the APU FAULT Light may be inoperative provided the associated indications are available on ECAM ELEC page.

name: OFF Lights
placard: ___ GEN FAULT LIGHT INOP ___ GEN OFF LIGHT(S) INOP
rem: May be inoperative.
ptext: ___ GEN FAULT LIGHT INOP ___ GEN OFF LIGHT(S) INOP

name: AC ESS FEED FAULT/ALTN Light
rem: May be inoperative provided AC ESS bus indication is available on ECAM ELEC page.

name: FAULT Light
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. AC load indication for each generator channel is available on ECAM ELEC page, and 2. Automatic shedding operates normally.

name: OFF Light
placard: GALLEY FAULT LIGHT INOP SWITCH OFF LIGHT INOP
rem: May be inoperative.
oproc: For ships 3209 to 3269: 1. With the electrical network supplied from APU or external power: A. GALLEY – Check AUTO. B. C/B on panel 122VU ELEC/GALLEY/GND/FLT/ LOGIC location S24 –PULL. C. Check for white GALLEY SHED on lower ECAM display electrical page. D. Check FWD galley oven No. 1 does not operate. E. C/B on panel 122VU ELEC/GALLEY/GND/FLT/ LOGIC location S24 –PUSH. For ships 3270 to 3278, 3101 to 3171, and all A321 aircraft: 1. With the electrical network supplied from APU or external power: A. GALY & CAB – Check AUTO. B. C/B on panel 122VU ELEC/GALY & CAB/GND/FLT/ LOGIC location S24 –PULL. C. Check for white GALLEY SHED on lower ECAM display electrical page. D. Check FWD galley oven No. 1 does not operate. E. C/B on panel 122VU ELEC/GALY & CAB/GND/FLT/ LOGIC location S24 –PUSH.
ptext: GALLEY FAULT LIGHT INOP SWITCH OFF LIGHT INOP

name: RAT and EMER GEN FAULT Light
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided indications are available on associated ECAM ELEC page.
oproc: Monitor ECAM indications for associated light when required.

name: Indications on ECAM ELEC Page
rem: (O) Indications related to AC generation may be inoperative provided: 1. Load, voltage and frequency indications of at least one engine driven generator are operative, 2. Associated GEN FAULT caution on ECAM operates normally, and 3. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, check automatic shedding operates normally. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: APU GEN Parameters
rem: Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS), may be inoperative provided both Engine Driven Generators are operative
oproc: 1. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE: A. With the electrical network supplied from APU or external power: i. Check that FAULT light of GEN 1 (2) pb switch is ON (pb at ON position). ii. GALLEY – Check AUTO. iii. C/B on panel 122VU ELEC/GALLEY/GND/FLT/ LOGIC location S24 – PULL. iv. Check FWD galley oven No. 1 does not operate. v. C/B on panel 122VU ELEC/GALLEY/GND/FLT/ LOGIC location S24 – PUSH.

name: OFF Light
rem: May be inoperative. Note: A/C 3134–3141 and 3272–3275 have the Commercial pb switch installed but deactivated. AA 2426-01003 and AA 2337-01169 reactivates the Commercial pb switch.

name: Transformer / Rectifier Units
rem: (O) (M) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS), TR 1 may be inoperative provided: 1. TR 2 and Essential TR operate normally, 2. Extract fan operates normally, 3. Battery voltage indicator operates normally, 4. Both packs operate normally, 5. Repairs are made within two flight days, and 6. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements.

name: Transformer / Rectifier Units
rem: (O) (M) TR 2 may be inoperative provided: 1. TR 1 and Essential TR operate normally, 2. Extract fan operates normally, 3. Battery voltage indicator operates normally, 4. Both packs operate normally, 5. Repairs are made within two flight days, 6. AC ESS FEED control is checked operative ONCE EACH FLIGHT DAY (Log Book Entry Required), 7. Standby Horizon operates normally, 8. Standby Compass operates normally, and 9. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Transformer / Rectifier Units
rem: (O) (M) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS), TR 2 may be inoperative provided: 1. TR 1 and Essential TR operate normally, 2. Extract fan operates normally, 3. Battery voltage indicator operates normally, 4. Both packs operate normally, 5. Repairs are made within two flight days, 6. AC ESS FEED control is checked operative ONCE EACH FLIGHT DAY (LOG Book Entry Required), 7. ISIS Horizon operates normally, 8. Standby Compass operates normally, and 9. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Battery Voltmeters (Indications on overhead panel)
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. Battery indications are available on ECAM, and 2. Battery voltage is confirmed adequate before APU start. Note: External electrical power will be required to check battery voltage(s) on ECAM ELEC Page prior to APU start when aircraft engines, (generators), are not running.
oproc: 1. Ensure external power source is available. 2. Select battery switch(s) ON. 3. Utilize battery voltmeter indications on ECAM ELEC Page as required. Note: Under this MEL, battery voltage may be checked using this method to satisfy the BATT OFF check.

name: FAULT Lights
rem: May be inoperative.

placard: BAT FAULT LIGHT INOP BAT OFF LIGHT INOP
rem: May be inoperative.
ptext: BAT FAULT LIGHT INOP BAT OFF LIGHT INOP

name: DC TIE Contactor 1 (DC BUS
rem: May be inoperative open provided DC TIE contactor ESS operates normally.

name: DC TIE Contactor ESS (DC
placard: DC TIE CONTACTOR INOP OPEN DC TIE CONTACTOR INOP OPEN
rem: May be inoperative open provided DC TIE contactor 1 operates normally.
ptext: DC TIE CONTACTOR INOP OPEN DC TIE CONTACTOR INOP OPEN

name: OFF Light
rem: May be inoperative.

name: ECAM ELEC Page (DC)
rem: Indications related to DC generation may be Inoperative.

name: External Power Panel ADIRU/AVNCS Vent Caution Light
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided avionics ventilation system warning horn is verified to operate normally.
mproc: 1. To verify that the avionics ventilation system warning horn operates normally accomplish AMM Task 21-26-00-040-012.

name: Ground Power Control Unit (GPCU) / Ground Power Control Function of the Ground and Auxiliary Power Control Unit (GAPCU)
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. External power is not used, and 2. APU generator operates normally. Note: When GPCU / Ground Power Control Function of GAPCU and APU generator are both inoperative, engines cannot be started.

name: Receptacle
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Receptacle is visually inspected, 2. External power is not used, 3. EXT PWR pb is placarded inoperative, and 4. APU generator operates normally. Note: When GPCU / Ground Power Control Function of GAPCU and APU generator are both inoperative, engines cannot be started.
mproc: 1. Open the AC External Power Control Door 121AL. 2. Place a strip of Red colored OM2 “UNSERVICEABLE – DO NOT USE” tape (or equivalent*) over the external power receptacle. *An acceptable alternate to the OM2 tape is a section of 2 inch wide masking tape with the words “UNSERVICEABLE–DO NOT USE” printed legibly in 1 inch black letters. 3. Close the AC External Power Control door. 24-41-01B 1. Visually inspect the external power receptacle by perform AMM Task 24-41-00-040-001. 2. Place a strip of Red colored OM2 “UNSERVICEABLE – DO NOT USE” tape (or equivalent*) over the external power receptacle. *An acceptable alternate to the OM2 tape is a section of 2 inch wide masking tape with the words “UNSERVICEABLE–DO NOT USE” printed legibly in 1 inch black letters

name: External Power NOT IN USE and AVAIL Panel Lights
placard: NOT IN USE AND AVAIL PANEL LIGHTS INOP
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided alternate procedures are used.
oproc: Use standard DELTA ramp hand signals. FOM Chap 30 Ramp Operations.
ptext: NOT IN USE AND AVAIL PANEL LIGHTS INOP

name: AVAIL Light
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided alternate procedures are used.

name: ON Light
placard: EXT PWR AVAIL LIGHT INOP PWR PB SWITCH ON LIGHT INOP
rem: May be inoperative.
oproc: Use standard DELTA ramp hand signals. FOM Chap 30 Ramp Operations.
ptext: EXT PWR AVAIL LIGHT INOP PWR PB SWITCH ON LIGHT INOP

name: C/B TRIPPED
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided alternate procedures are used to verify that no circuit breaker is tripped on associated C/B panel.

name: ELEC IDG 1(2) OIL LO LVL Alert
rem: (M) One may be inoperative provided: 1. Sight glass of the affected IDG shows a correct oil level, and 2. Differential Pressure Indicator (DPI) of the oil filter is not extended on the affected IDG.

name: ELEC IDG 1(2) OIL LO LVL Alert
rem: (O) (M) One may be inoperative provided: 1. Associated generator is set to OFF, and 2. Associated AC Main Generation is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 24-20-01A.

name: ELEC IDG 1(2) OIL LO LVL Alert
rem: One may be inoperative for 15 flight-hours or 1 flight-day, whichever occurs first.

name: ELEC IDG 1(2) FILTER CLOG Alert
rem: (M) One may be inoperative provided: 1. Sight glass of the affected IDG shows a correct oil level, and 2. Differential Pressure Indicator (DPI) of the oil filter is not extended on the affected IDG.

name: ELEC IDG 1(2) FILTER CLOG Alert
rem: (O) (M) One may be inoperative provided: 1. Associated generator is set to OFF, and 2. Associated AC Main Generation is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 24-20-01A.

name: Automatic Load Shedding System
rem: May be inoperative provided: 1. GALLEY/GALY&CAB pb switch on the flight deck operates normally, and 2. GALLEY/GALY&CAB FAULT light on the flight deck operates normally.

name: Automatic and Manual Load Shedding Systems
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided all GALLEY/GALY&CAB loads are disconnected. Note: Ovens and coffee makers are inoperative.

name: COMMERCIAL Supply System
placard: GALLEY LOAD SHEDDING SYSTEM INOP AND MANUAL GALLEY LOAD SHEDDING SYSTEM INOP SUPPLY SYS INOP
rem: (O) May be inoperative. Note: A/C 3134–3141 and 3272–3275 have the Commercial pb switch installed but deactivated. AA 2426-01003 and AA 2337-01169 reactivates the Commercial pb switch.
ptext: GALLEY LOAD SHEDDING SYSTEM INOP AND MANUAL GALLEY LOAD SHEDDING SYSTEM INOP SUPPLY SYS INOP

name: Fault(s) Indicated by ELT
rem: Note: Dispatch with maintenance status message displayed on ECAM is permitted without CFDS interrogation. Note: ELT maintenance status message may be spuriously displayed. In this case, flight crew and/or maintenance personnel should disregard ELT maintenance status message provided that it was confirmed to be false by troubleshooting within last 28 days. To confirm that ELT maintenance status message is spurious, apply TSM task 31-39-00-810-813.

name: Recline
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Seat is secured in an upright position acceptable to the affected crewmember, and 2. Repairs are made within two flight days.

name: Electrical Adjustment
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Horizontal and vertical mechanical adjustments operate normally, and 2. Associated electrical control is deactivated.

name: Primary Horizontal Mechanical Adjustment
rem: May be inoperative provided backup horizontal mechanical adjustment is installed and operates normally.

name: Backup Horizontal Mechanical Adjustment
rem: May be inoperative provided primary horizontal mechanical adjustment operates normally.

name: Vertical Mechanical Adjustment
rem: May be inoperative provided vertical electrical adjustment operates normally.

name: Lumbar
placard: ___ RECLINE INOP ELECTRICAL ADJUSTMENT INOP HORIZONTAL MECHANICAL ADJUSTMENT INOP HORIZONTAL MECHANICAL ADJUSTMENT INOP MECHANICAL ADJUSTMENT INOP LUMBAR ADJUSTMENT INOP
rem: May be inoperative provided seat contour is satisfactory to individual/crewmember requirements.
mproc: 1. Ensure that the affected seat is secured in an upright position by performing a functional test to verify that the seat cannot be moved. If the affected seat can be moved, do the following procedure to secure seatback in an upright position: ( Ref. Figure 25-1 PILOT SEAT ADJUSTMENT (Recline) A. Remove the affected seat cushion. B. If installed, remove the headrest. C. Remove the fourteen screws. (2). D. Remove the upper back shroud (1) from the affected seat backrest (3). E. Loosen the screw (4) on the left and right lock levers (6). F. Remove the cable (5) from the left and right lock levers (6). G. Push on the left and right lock levers (6) at the same time and push the affected seat backrest (3). CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE
ptext: ___ RECLINE INOP ELECTRICAL ADJUSTMENT INOP HORIZONTAL MECHANICAL ADJUSTMENT INOP HORIZONTAL MECHANICAL ADJUSTMENT INOP MECHANICAL ADJUSTMENT INOP LUMBAR ADJUSTMENT INOP

name: Crewmember Shoulder Harness - Primary Observer’s Sliding Seat (3rd Occupant)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. A passenger seat in the passenger cabin is made available to an FAA inspector for the performance of official duties, or 2. Additional Observers seat is available, functional and acceptable, including peripheral equipment, to an FAA inspector for the performance of official duties, and 3. Operators are limited to not more than two flight days before repairs are made.

name: Crewmember Shoulder Harness - Additional Observer’s Fixed Seat (4th Occupant)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided that if the forward observers seat is inoperative, a passenger seat in the passenger cabin is made available and is acceptable to an FAA inspector for the performance of official duties.
oproc: Captain to notify Dispatch if an FAA Inspector requires a passenger seat in the cabin. 25-11-02B Captain to notify Dispatch if an FAA Inspector requires a passenger seat in the cabin.
mproc: 1. Placard the inoperative seat with OM2, “UNSERVICEABLE – DO NOT USE” tape or equivalent. Note: An acceptable alternate to the OM2 tape is a section of 2 inch wide masking tape with the words “UNSERVICEABLE – DO NOT USE” printed legibly in 1 inch black letters. 25-11-02B To placard an inoperative seat accomplish the following: 1. Placard the inoperative seat with OM2, “UNSERVICEABLE – DO NOT USE” tape or equivalent. OR To remove an inoperative seat accomplish the following: A. Remove seat (Ref. AMM TASK 25–11–42–000–001). Note: An acceptable alternate to the OM2 tape is a section of 2 inch wide masking tape with the words “UNSERVICEABLE – DO NOT USE” printed legibly in 1 inch black letters.

name: Height Control
rem: May be inoperative provided setting is acceptable to crewmember(s).

name: Pitch Control (Tilt)
rem: May be inoperative provided setting is acceptable to crewmember(s).

name: Armrest Memory Position Display
placard: ARMREST HEIGHT CONTROL INOP ARMREST PITCH CONTROL INOP ARMREST MEMORY POSITION DISPLAY INOP
rem: May be inoperative.
ptext: ARMREST HEIGHT CONTROL INOP ARMREST PITCH CONTROL INOP ARMREST MEMORY POSITION DISPLAY INOP

name: Capt and F/O Inboard Armrest Vertical Adjustment Controls
rem: May be inoperative.

name: Primary Observer’s Sliding Seat (3rd Occupant) (Including associated equipment) (Seat Unusable)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. A passenger seat in the passenger cabin is made available to an FAA inspector for the performance of official duties, and 2. Repairs are made within two flight days.

name: Primary Observer’s Sliding Seat (3rd Occupant) (Including associated equipment) (Seat Unusable)
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Second observer’s (4th occupant– fixed seat) seat is available to the FAA inspector for the performance of official duties, and 2. Repairs are made within two flight days.

name: Primary Observer’s Sliding (3rd Seat Occupant) (Including associated equipment) Seat Usable (3rd Occupant) (Including associated equipment) (Seat Usable)
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. Required minimum safety equipment (safety belt and oxygen) is available, 2. Seat is acceptable to the FAA inspector for the performance of official duties, and 3. Repairs are made within two flight days. Note: These provisos are intended to provide for occupancy of the above seat by an FAA inspector when the minimum safety equipment (oxygen and safety belt) is functional and the inspector determines the conditions to be acceptable. Note: The pilot-in-command will determine if the minimum safety equipment is functional for other persons (other than FAA) authorized to occupy seat.
oproc: Captain to notify Dispatch if an FAA Inspector requires a passenger seat in the cabin. 25-11-06C Captain is to ensure that minimum safety equipment (safety belt and oxygen) is functional if seat is to be occupied, (by other than FAA personnel). CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Additional Observer’s Fixed Seat (4th Occupant) (Including associated equipment) (Seat Unusable)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative or removed
oproc: Captain to notify Dispatch if an FAA Inspector requires a passenger seat in the cabin.
mproc: To placard an inoperative seat accomplish the following: 1. Placard the inoperative seat with OM2, “UNSERVICEABLE – DO NOT USE” tape or equivalent. OR To remove an inoperative seat accomplish the following: A. Remove seat per AMM TASK 25–11–42–000–001. Note: An acceptable alternate to the OM2 tape is a section of 2 inch wide masking tape with the words “UNSERVICEABLE – DO NOT USE” printed legibly in 1 inch black letters.

name: Sliding Tables
rem: (M) May be inoperative in the stowed position.

name: Sliding Tables
rem: (M) May be removed.

name: Table Tilt Function
placard: ___ SLIDING TABLE(S) INOP SLIDING TABLE(S) REMOVED SLIDING TABLE TILT(S) INOP
rem: May be inoperative provided associated table can be stowed.
mproc: 1. Ensure table is in stowed position or removed. A. To remove the defective Captain and/or First Officer sliding table(s). i. Pull the table. Do not remove it fully from its housing. ii. Remove the screw from the bottom. iii. Remove the sliding table.
ptext: ___ SLIDING TABLE(S) INOP SLIDING TABLE(S) REMOVED SLIDING TABLE TILT(S) INOP

name: CAPT and F/O Retractable Footrests
rem: (M) May be inoperative secured in the retracted position or removed.
mproc: 1. Footrest can be inoperative in stowed position or removed. A. Footrest can be stowed per the following: i. Inoperative foot rests with failed actuators must be secured in the retracted position with .032 diameter minimum safety wire until MEL is cleared. B. Footrest can be removed per the following: i. Perform AMM Task 25-13-00-040-002.

name: Passenger Seat(s) (Seat Unusable)
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Seat does not restrict access to any emergency exit, egress route, or main aisle, and 2. Affected seat(s) is blocked and placarded “UNSERVICEABLE – DO NOT USE”, and 3. Seat is not required for use by a displaced flight attendant. Note: A seat with an inoperative seat belt/shoulder harness is considered inoperative. Note: Inoperative seats do not affect the required number of Flight Attendants. Note: Affected seat(s) may include the seat(s) behind and/or adjacent outboard seat(s). Note: MCC must contact Flight Control for coordinating with ROC to remove seat from inventory until repaired.

name: Positioning Controls for Taxi, Takeoff, and Landing (TTL) (Mechanical Seat) (Seat Useable)
rem: (M) May be inoperative and seat occupied provided seat is secured in the taxi, takeoff, and landing (TTL) position. Note: A seat with an inoperative recline mechanism is considered inoperative if the seat cannot be secured upright. Apply MEL Item 25–21–01A.

name: Positioning Controls for Taxi, Takeoff, and Landing (TTL) (Mechanical Seat)(Seat Useable)
rem: May be inoperative and seat occupied provided seat back is immovable in the taxi, takeoff, and landing (TTL) position. Note: If a seat back is not immovable in the full upright position, apply MEL Item 25–21– 01B.

name: Passenger Seat(s) Underseat Baggage Restraining Bars
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Baggage is not stowed under seat with inoperative restraining bar, Associated seat is placarded “DO NOT STOW BAGGAGE UNDER THIS SEAT,” and 2. Procedures are used to alert Cabin Crew of inoperative restraining bar.

name: Passenger Seat(s) Armrests With Seat Positioning Controls for Taxi, Takeoff, and Landing (TTL) and/or Other Controls
rem: (M) May be inoperative or missing and seat occupied provided: 1. Armrest does not restrict access to any emergency exit, egress route, or main aisle, and 2. If armrest with seat control is missing or removed, seat is secured in taxi, takeoff, and landing (TTL) position. PAGE

name: Passenger Seat(s) Armrests Without Seat Positioning Controls for Taxi, Takeoff, and Landing (TTL) and/or Other Controls
rem: May be inoperative or missing and seat occupied provided armrest does not restrict access to any emergency exit, egress route, or main aisle.

name: Seat Belt / Air Bags
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided affected seat(s) is blocked and placarded “UNSERVICEABLE – DO NOT USE”.
oproc: Brief Flight attendants to ensure baggage is not stowed under associated seat.
mproc: Note: MCC must contact Flight Control for coordinating with ROC to remove seat from inventory until repaired. 1. Install Seat INOP Block-Out Cover part number 25-5136-001 per ERA 10-485792-14MA. A. If the seat is stuck in the recline position block off affected seat(s) and the seat directly behind the inoperative seat by installing a Seat INOP Block-Out Cover part number 25-5136-001 per ERA 10-485792-14MA. Affected seats would be any seat with blocked access to an aisle by the inoperative seat Note: The inoperative seat or affected seat(s) must not block access to an Emergency Exit or aisle. B. If the seatback is loose or blocks access to an Emergency Exit secure upright with a Seat INOP Block-Out Cover part number 25-5136-001 per ERA 10-485792-14MA or alternatively for seat backs without AVOD installed remove the seat back and placard the seatback “MUST REMAIN WITH AIRPLANE” and stow in available closet. Block off affected seat directly behind the inoperative seat(s) by installing a Seat INOP Block-Out Cover part number 25-5136-001 per ERA 10-485792-14MA. Install a Seat INOP Block-Out Placard part number 11-0972-001 per ERA 10-485792-14MA or equivalent* on seat with removed seat back. 2. In the event that the Seat INOP Block-Out Cover is not available the seat may be made inoperative per one of the following provisions provided the MCC is contacted to schedule the installation of a Seat INOP Block-Out Cover at the next maintenance station the cover is available: CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Required Flight Attendant Seats
rem: (O) (M) One required seat position or assembly (dual position) may be inoperative provided: 1. Affected seat position or assembly is not occupied, 2. Flight Attendant(s) displaced by inoperative seat(s) occupies either an adjacent flight attendant seat or the passenger seat which is most accessible to the inoperative seat(s) so as to most effectively perform assigned duties, 3. Alternate procedures are used as published in crewmember manuals, 4. Folding type seat stows automatically or is secured in the retracted position and is checked ONCE EACH FLIGHT DAY (Log Book Entry Required), and 5. If a passenger seat is needed for a required flight attendant, the assigned seat is placarded “FOR FLIGHT ATTENDANT USE ONLY”. Note: An automatic folding seat that will not stow automatically is considered inoperative. Note: A seat with a missing or inoperative restraint system is considered inoperative. Note: If one side of a dual seat assembly is inoperative and a flight attendant is displaced to the adjacent seat, the adjacent seat must operate normally. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard) PAGE

name: Required Flight Attendant Seats
rem: (O) (M) One required seat position or assembly (dual position) may be inoperative provided: 1. Affected seat position or assembly is not occupied, 2. Flight Attendant(s) displaced by inoperative seat(s) occupies either an adjacent flight attendant seat or the passenger seat which is most accessible to the inoperative seat(s) so as to most effectively perform assigned duties, 3. Alternate procedures are used as published in crewmember manuals, 4. Folding type seat stows automatically or is secured in the retracted position and is checked ONCE EACH FLIGHT DAY (Log Book Entry Required), and 5. If a passenger seat is needed for a required flight attendant, the assigned seat is placarded “FOR FLIGHT ATTENDANT USE ONLY”. Note: An automatic folding seat that will not stow automatically is considered inoperative. Note: A seat with a missing or inoperative restraint system is considered inoperative. Note: If one side of a dual seat assembly is inoperative and a flight attendant is displaced to the adjacent seat, the adjacent seat must operate normally. Note: If swivel/pull-out swivel seat becomes inoperative, select a nearby passenger seat at most accessible to assigned exit that allows clear view of cabin. Do not occupy galley FA seat. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Excess (Non-required) Flight Attendant Seats
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Affected seat position or seat assembly is not occupied, and 2. Folding type seat stows automatically or is secured in the retracted position and is checked ONCE EACH FLIGHT DAY (Log Book Entry Required). Note: An automatic folding seat that will not stow automatically is considered inoperative. Note: A seat with a missing or inoperative restraint system is considered inoperative. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard) PAGE

name: Non-Essential Equipment & Furnishings (NEF)
rem: May be inoperative, damaged, or missing provided that item(s) is deferred in accordance with operator’s NEF deferral program. Note: Do not use this MEL number to defer the discrepancy. Defer the discrepancy under the NEF program per TOPP 40-40-08. The NEF List and procedures can be found in the on-line NEF Application. Note: Exterior lavatory door ash trays are not considered NEF items.

name: Lavatory Door Ashtrays (On outside of door)
rem: Up to 50% may be missing or inoperative for 10 days. Note: This item is A.D. related.

name: Lavatory Door Ashtrays (On outside of door)
placard: DOOR ASHTRAY MISSING DOOR ASHTRAY MISSING Furnishings items. See TOPP 40-40-08 for
rem: More than 50% may be missing or inoperative for 3 days. Note: This item is A.D. related.
ptext: DOOR ASHTRAY MISSING DOOR ASHTRAY MISSING Furnishings items. See TOPP 40-40-08 for

name: Heating Function of Heated Floor Panels (Passenger/ Crew Doors, Emergency Exits, and Galley Areas)
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided the heating elements of the affected floor panel are deactivated.
mproc: 1. To deactivate the affected heating element pull and secure the following circuit breakers: A. For Door 2 – on the 2000VU panel pull and secure DR AREA HTG PANELS – DR2 – CTL located at A06 and DR AREA HTG PANELS – DR2 – HTR located at A08. B. For Door 3 – on the 2001VU panel pull and secure DR AREA HTG PNLS – DOOR3 – CTL located at F03 and DOOR AREA HTG PNL – DOOR3 – HTR located at F05. Note: Only the circuit breakers for the affected floor panels are required to be pulled and secured.

name: Storage Bin(s)/ Cabin, Galley, and Lavatory Storage Compartment / Closets
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Affected bin, compartment, or closet is secured CLOSED, 2. Associated bin, compartment, or closet is prominently placarded “UNSERVICEABLE – DO NOT USE”, 3. Any emergency equipment located in the affected bin, compartment, or closet is considered inoperative and associated MEL relief is applied, and 4. Affected bin, compartment, or closet is not used for storage of any item(s) except for those permanently affixed. Note: For overhead bins, if no partitions are installed, the entire overhead bin is considered one compartment. Note: Proviso is not intended to preclude crewmember inspections.

name: Storage Bin(s)/ Cabin, Galley, and Lavatory Storage Compartment / Closets
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. For nonretractable doors, affected door is removed, 2. For retractable doors, affected door is removed or secured in the retracted (fully open) position, 3. Affected bin, compartment, or closet is prominently placarded "UNSERVICEABLE - DO NOT USE", 4. Affected bin, compartment, or closet is not used for storage of any items except those permanently affixed, 5. Procedures are used to alert crewmembers and passengers of inoperative bins, compartments, or closets, and 6. Passengers are briefed that associated bin, compartment, or closet is not used. Note: For overhead bins, if no partitions are installed, the entire overhead bin is considered inoperative. Note: Any emergency equipment located in the affected bin, compartment, or closet (permanently affixed) is available for use. Note: A319, A320, and A321CEO aircraft are configured with bucket style overhead storage bins. The A319/320/321 AMM refers to these as Pivoting Overhead Storage Compartments. This relief cannot be used on Bucket Style / Pivoting Overhead Storage Bins. PAGE

name: Storage Bin(s)/ Cabin, Galley, and Lavatory Storage Compartment / Closets
rem: (M) May be inoperative in CLOSED position provided: 1. Affected bin, compartment, or closet is prominently placarded "UNSERVICEABLE - DO NOT USE", 2. Any emergency equipment located in the affected bin, compartment, or closet is considered inoperative and associated MEL relief is applied, and 3. Location placarding for any emergency equipment stored in affected bin, compartment, or closet is removed or obscured. Note: Use of this proviso may be dependant upon an operators aircraft security program, as appropriate.

name: Storage Compartment Key Locks
rem: (M) May be inoperative in the unlocked position provided doors can be secured by other means.

name: Multi Latch/ Quarter Turn Lug
rem: (M) One latch per compartment may be inoperative provided: 1. Remaining latch(es)/lug(s) on affected compartment(s) operate normally, and 2. If affected compartment is used for a galley cart, the cart remains empty.

name: Mid-Latch Locking Assembly
placard: BIN(S) / CABIN AND GALLEY AND COMPARTMENT / CLOSETS INOP placard affected emergency equipment with appropriate MEL relief BIN(S) / CABIN AND GALLEY AND COMPARTMENT / CLOSETS INOP AND DOOR(S) REMOVED / CABIN AND GALLEY AND LAVATORY COMPARTMENT / CLOSETS INOP COMPARTMENT KEY LOCK(S) INOP COMPARTMENT LATCH/LUG/LOCK INOP LOCKING ASSEMBLY INOP
rem: May be inoperative provided galley half-length carts are not used at affected location. Note: Galley full-length carts can be used.
ptext: BIN(S) / CABIN AND GALLEY AND COMPARTMENT / CLOSETS INOP placard affected emergency equipment with appropriate MEL relief BIN(S) / CABIN AND GALLEY AND COMPARTMENT / CLOSETS INOP AND DOOR(S) REMOVED / CABIN AND GALLEY AND LAVATORY COMPARTMENT / CLOSETS INOP COMPARTMENT KEY LOCK(S) INOP COMPARTMENT LATCH/LUG/LOCK INOP LOCKING ASSEMBLY INOP

name: Lavatory Waste Receptacle Access Doors/ Covers/Flapper/Doors
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Associated waste container is empty, 2. Affected receptacle access doors/covers/flapper door is secured to prevent waste introduction into the receptacle, 3. Lavatory is used only by crewmembers, and 4. The lavatory door is locked closed and placarded: “Unserviceable – Do Not Use”, Note: These provisos are not intended to prohibit lavatory use or inspections by crewmembers. Ensure lavatory door is locked closed after crew member inspection. Note: Applicable doors include all lavatory paper and linen waste receptacle enclosure access doors and disposal doors.
oproc: 1. Captain to brief Lead Flight Attendant/Purser that the affected lavatory door is to remain locked and not entered except for use or inspection by crewmembers. 2. Captain is to ensure the lavatory door is locked closed if crewmember inspection has been performed or if the lavatory is used by a crewmember.
mproc: 1. Close and lock the Lavatory Door as follows: A. Lift the LAVATORY sign on the outer face of the lavatory door. B. Move the latch under the sign until the indicator shows the door is locked (OCCUPIED). C. Check that the door will not open. 2. Place a strip of Red Colored OM2 UNSERVICEABLE – DO NOT USE tape (or equivalent*) diagonally across the lavatory door and across the affected receptacle access doors/covers/flapper door to prevent use. Note: An acceptable alternate to the OM2 tape is a section of 2 inch wide masking tape with the words “UNSERVICEABLE – DO NOT USE” printed legibly in 1 inch black letters.

name: Galley/Cabin Waste Receptacles Access Doors/ Covers/Flapper Doors
placard: WASTE RECEPTACLES ACCESS DOOR and/or COVER IS INOP
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. The container is empty and the access is secured to prevent waste introduction into the compartment, and 2. Procedures are used to ensure that sufficient galley/cabin receptacles are available to accommodate all waste that may be generated on a flight. Note: This MEL item cannot be used to defer galley waste carts.
oproc: Captain to verify with Lead Flight Attendant/ Purser that sufficient galley/cabin waste receptacles are available to accommodate all waste that may be generated on the flight.
mproc: 1. Check the affected waste container is empty. 2. Place a strip of Red colored OM–2 UNSERVICEABLE – DO NOT USE tape or equivalent* across the affected Galley/ Cabin Waste Receptacle Access Door and/or Cover. Note: An acceptable alternate to the OM2 tape is a section of 2 inch wide masking tape with the words “UNSERVICEABLE – DO NOT USE” printed legibly in 1 inch black letters.
ptext: WASTE RECEPTACLES ACCESS DOOR and/or COVER IS INOP

name: Blow In/Out Panels in Lower Deck Cargo Compartment
rem: (O) May be damaged or missing provided PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE procedures are used to ensure forward cargo compartment remains empty, or verified to contain only empty cargo handling equipment, ballast, and/or fly-away kits (see MEL Instructions Section). Note: When this MEL is applied, on A321 NEO aircraft ONLY, restricting use of the entire FWD Cargo Compartment prohibits carrying Motorized Wheelchairs and Sharp, Dense, Heavy (SDH) items on-board aircraft. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Blow In/Out Panels in Lower Deck Cargo Compartment (AFT and BULK)
rem: (O) (M) May be damaged or missing provided PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE procedures are used to ensure AFT and BULK cargo compartment remains empty, or verified to contain only empty cargo handling equipment, ballast, and/or fly-away kits (see MEL Instructions Section). Note: When Blow In/Out Panels in AFT or BULK cargo compartments are damaged or missing, both cargo compartments remain empty or are verified to contain only empty cargo handling equipment, ballast (ballast may be loaded in ULDs), and/or Fly Away Kits. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, Captain is to ensure that no cargo is loaded in the forward cargo compartment. Note: When this MEL is applied, on A321 NEO aircraft ONLY, restricting use of the entire FWD Cargo Compartment prohibits carrying Motorized Wheelchairs and Sharp, Dense, Heavy (SDH) items on-board aircraft. 25-50-02B PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, Captain is to ensure that no cargo is loaded in the AFT and BULK cargo compartment. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Lining Panels in Lower Deck Cargo Compartment (FWD)
rem: (O) May be damaged provided PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE procedures are used to ensure forward cargo compartment remains empty, or verified to contain only empty cargo handling equipment, ballast, and/or fly-away kits (see MEL Instructions Section). Note: Not for use when cargo floor panels are damaged. Refer to SRM or contact Liaison Engineering to evaluate damage. Note: Lining panels covered by this MEL item include ceiling panels, sidewall panels, partition panels (including tarpaulin), cargo door panels, and actuator panels. Note: When this MEL is applied, on A321 NEO aircraft ONLY, restricting use of the entire FWD Cargo Compartment prohibits carrying Motorized Wheelchairs and Sharp, Dense, Heavy (SDH) items on-board aircraft. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Lining Panels in Lower Deck Cargo Compartment (AFT and BULK)
rem: (O) (M) May be damaged missing provided PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE procedures are used to ensure AFT and BULK cargo compartment remains empty, or verified to contain only empty cargo handling equipment, ballast, and/or fly-away kits (see MEL Instructions Section). Note: Not for use when cargo floor panels are damaged. Refer to SRM or contact Liaison Engineering to evaluate damage. Note: Lining panels covered by this MEL item include ceiling panels, sidewall panels, partition panels (including tarpaulin), cargo door panels, and actuator panels. Note: When lining panels in AFT or BULK cargo compartments are damaged, both cargo compartments remain empty or are verified to contain only empty cargo handling equipment, ballast (ballast may be loaded in ULDs), and/or Fly Away Kits. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard) PAGE

name: Lining Panels in Lower Deck Cargo Compartments
rem: May be missing provided associated cargo compartment remains empty. Note: Lining panels covered by this MEL item include ceiling panels, sidewall panels, partition panels (including tarpaulin), cargo door panels, and actuator panels. Note: When Lining Panels in AFT or BULK cargo compartments are missing, both cargo compartments remain empty. Note: MEL Instructions Section for empty cargo compartment does not apply to this relief. Cargo compartment must remain empty.
oproc: PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, Captain is to ensure that no cargo is loaded in the forward cargo compartment. Note: When this MEL is applied, on A321 NEO aircraft ONLY, restricting use of the entire FWD Cargo Compartment prohibits carrying Motorized Wheelchairs and Sharp, Dense, Heavy (SDH) items on-board aircraft. 25-50-03B PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, Captain is to ensure that no cargo is loaded in the AFT and BULK cargo compartment.
mproc: 1. If panels are damaged or missing in the AFT or BULK cargo compartment, verify AOG Parts Stowage Container is empty. 2. Place a strip of Red Colored OM2 UNSERVICEABLE – DO NOT USE tape (or equivalent*) across the container. Note: An acceptable alternate to the OM2 tape is a section of 2 inch wide masking tape with the words “UNSERVICEABLE – DO NOT USE” printed legibly in 1 inch black letters.

name: Cargo Restraint System (Bin 1 Door Netting)
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Bin 1 remains empty, and 2. Repairs are made within 120 consecutive calendar days. Note: MEL Instructions Section for empty cargo compartment does not apply to this relief. Cargo compartment section must remain empty unless cargo is tied down.

name: Cargo Restraint System (Netting Between Bins 1-2)
rem: (M) May be inoperative or missing provided: 1. Bins 1 and 2 remain empty or are verified to contain only empty cargo handling equipment, ballast, and/or fly-away kits (see MEL Instructions Section), and 2. Repairs are made within 120 consecutive calendar days.

name: Cargo Restraint System (Netting Between Bins 1-2)
rem: (M) May be inoperative or missing provided: 1. Approved cargo loading limits from the Aircraft Loading Manual (ALM) are observed, and 2. Repairs are made within 120 consecutive calendar days

name: Cargo Restraint System (Bin 2 AFT Netting)
rem: (M) May be inoperative or missing provided: 1. Bin 2 remains empty or is verified to contain only empty cargo handling equipment, ballast, and/or fly-away kits (see MEL Instructions Section), and 2. Repairs are made within 120 consecutive calendar days.

name: Cargo Restraint System (Bin 1 Attach/Tie Down Points)
rem: (M) May be inoperative or missing provided: 1. Sharp, Dense, and Heavy (SDH) cargo is not loaded in bin 1, and 2. Repairs are made within 120 consecutive calendar days.

name: Cargo Restraint System (Bin 2 Attach/Tie Down Points)
rem: (M) May be inoperative or missing provided: 1. Sharp, Dense, and Heavy (SDH) cargo is not loaded in bin 2, and 2. Repairs are made within 120 consecutive calendar days.

name: Cargo Restraint System (Bin 4 Door Netting)
rem: (M) May be inoperative or missing provided: 1. Approved cargo loading limits from the Aircraft Loading Manual (ALM) are observed, and 2. Repairs are made within 120 consecutive calendar days. PAGE

name: Cargo Restraint System (Bin 4 Door Netting)
rem: (M) May be inoperative or missing provided: 1. Bin 4 remains empty, and 2. Repairs are made within 120 consecutive calendar days. Note: MEL Instructions Section for empty cargo compartment does not apply to this relief. Cargo compartment section must remain empty unless cargo is tied down.

name: Cargo Restraint System (Netting Between Bins 3-4)
rem: (M) May be inoperative or missing provided: 1. Approved cargo loading limits from the Aircraft Loading Manual (ALM) are observed, and 2. Repairs are made within 120 consecutive calendar days.

name: Cargo Restraint System (Netting Between Bins 3-4)
rem: (M) May be inoperative or missing provided: 1. Bins 3 and 4 remain empty or is verified to contain only empty cargo handling equipment, ballast, and/or fly-away kits (see MEL Instructions Section), and 2. Repairs are made within 120 consecutive calendar days.

name: Cargo Restraint System (Bin 3 FWD Netting)
rem: (M) May be inoperative or missing provided: 1. Bin 3 remains empty or is verified to contain only empty cargo handling equipment, ballast, and/or fly-away kits (see MEL Instructions Section), and 2. Repairs are made within 120 consecutive calendar days.

name: Cargo Restraint System (Netting Between Bins 4-5)
rem: (M) May be inoperative or missing provided: 1. Bins 4 and 5 remain empty or is verified to contain only empty cargo handling equipment, ballast, and/or fly-away kits (see MEL Instructions Section), and 2. Repairs are made within 120 consecutive calendar days.

name: Cargo Restraint System (Bin 4 AFT Netting)
rem: (M) May be inoperative or missing provided: 1. Bin 4 remains empty or is verified to contain only empty cargo handling equipment, ballast, and/or fly-away kits (see MEL Instructions Section), and 2. Repairs are made within 120 consecutive calendar days. PAGE

name: Cargo Restraint System (Bin 5 Door Netting)
rem: (M) May be inoperative or missing provided: 1. Bin 5 remains empty, and 2. Repairs are made within 120 consecutive calendar days. Note: MEL Instructions Section for empty cargo compartment does not apply to this relief. Cargo compartment section must remain empty unless cargo is tied down.

name: Cargo Restraint System (Bin 5 AFT Netting)
rem: (M) May be inoperative or missing provided: 1. Bin 5 remains empty, and 2. Repairs are made within 120 consecutive calendar days. Note: MEL Instructions Section for empty cargo compartment does not apply to this relief. Cargo compartment section must remain empty unless cargo is tied down.

name: Cargo Restraint System (Netting Between Bins 5-6)
rem: (M) May be inoperative or missing provided: 1. Bins 5 and 6 empty or is verified to contain only empty cargo handling equipment, ballast, and/ or fly-away kits (see MEL Instructions Section), and 2. Repairs are made within 120 consecutive calendar days.

name: Cargo Restraint System (Bin 6 Door Netting)
rem: (M) May be inoperative or missing provided: 1. Bin 6 empty, and 2. Repairs are made within 120 consecutive calendar days. Note: MEL Instructions Section for empty cargo compartment does not apply to this relief. Cargo compartment section must remain empty unless cargo is tied down.

name: Cargo Restraint System (Bin 3 Attach/Tie Down Points)
rem: (M) May be inoperative or missing provided: 1. Sharp, Dense, and Heavy (SDH) cargo is not loaded in bin 3, and 2. Repairs are made within 120 consecutive calendar days.

name: Cargo Restraint System (Bin 4 Attach/Tie Down Points)
rem: (M) May be inoperative or missing provided: 1. Sharp, Dense, and Heavy (SDH) cargo is not loaded in bin 4, and 2. Repairs are made within 120 consecutive calendar days. PAGE

name: Protection Panel of Decompression Panels at the FWD Partition Wall of FWD Cargo Compartment
rem: (M) One or more may be damaged provided: 1. The affected protection panel is removed if the damage prevents correct operation of the decompression panel, and 2. Bin 1 must remain empty.
mproc: Deactivate the damaged protection panel by performing AMM Task 25-54-00-040-801.

name: SLIDE ARMED Warning Systems – SLIDE ARMED Indicator Light (At over wing emergency exits)
placard: SLIDE ARMED LIGHT SYSTEM IS INOP
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Associated Emergency Exit (white) SLIDE indications on ECAM DOOR/OXY page operates normally, and 2. ECAM advisory message for associated EMER EXIT operates normally.
oproc: Captain to brief Lead Flight Attendant/Purser which over wing Slide Armed Light(s) is inoperative.
mproc: 1. Ensure the (white) “SLIDE” is adjacent to the associated Emergency Exits on the DOOR/OXY ECAM page. 2. The following procedures must be accomplished for the Fwd and Aft Emergency Exits individually. A. Pull down the cover flap from the emergency exit hatch and remove it. Check that the ECAM advisory is triggered “DOOR L(R) FWD (AFT) EMER EXIT”. B. Install the cover flap on the hatch and push it against the hatch. Check that the ECAM advisory is extinguished.
ptext: SLIDE ARMED LIGHT SYSTEM IS INOP

name: Passenger Doors Armed Indication (SLIDE is displayed when door slide is Disarmed) (Doors Without Girt Bar Placard On Floor)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided associated slide(s) is verified engaged (armed) PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required). Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Passenger Doors Not Armed Indication (SLIDE is not displayed when door slide is Armed) (Doors Without Girt Bar Placard On Floor)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Associated slide(s) is verified engaged (armed) PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required), and 2. Aircraft is limited to Day operations only. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Passenger Doors Armed Indication (SLIDE is displayed when door slide is Disarmed) (Doors With Girt Bar Placard On Floor)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided alternate procedures are used.

name: Passenger Doors Not Armed Indication (SLIDE is not displayed when door slide is Armed) (Doors With Girt Bar Placard On Floor)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Alternate procedures are used, and 2. Aircraft is limited to Day operations only.

name: Overwing Emergency Exits Armed Indication (SLIDE is displayed when slide is Disarmed) (For A319, A320, & A321NEO Aircraft Only)
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided a visual check is made to verify that slide(s) is armed.

name: Overwing Emergency Exits Not Armed Indication (SLIDE is not displayed when slide is Armed) (For A319, A320, & A321NEO Aircraft Only)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Visual check is made to verify that slide(s) is armed, and 2. Aircraft is limited to Day operations only.

name: Cabin Emergency Exit Armed Indication (For A321CEO Aircraft Only)
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided alternate procedures are used. PAGE

name: “Fasten Seat Belts While Seated” Signs or Placards
placard: BELTS WHILE SEATED SIGN IS MISSING or ILLEGIBLE
rem: (M) One or more may be illegible or missing provided: 1. A legible sign or placard is visible from each occupied passenger seat, and 2. Each seat unable to meet the requirements of proviso 1. is placarded inoperative and deferred per MEL 25-21-01A.
mproc: If a seat is unable to meet the requirements of proviso 1, Maintenance is Required and Flight Crew May NOT Placard If applicable, defer affected passenger seat(s) inoperative per MEL Item 25-21-01A.
ptext: BELTS WHILE SEATED SIGN IS MISSING or ILLEGIBLE

name: Flashlight and Holders (Flight Deck or Cabin)
rem: (O) May be inoperative or removed provided: 1. Crewmember assigned to the affected position has an equivalent operative flashlight readily available, 2. Inoperative flashlight remains in a certified location or is removed from the aircraft, and 3. Location placarding is removed or obscured. Note: An expired item must be removed from the aircraft.

name: Flashlight and Holders - Tamper Seals or Tags
placard: FLASHLIGHT AND/OR HOLDER IS INOP OR MISSING SEALS OR TAGS INOP / DAMAGED / MISSING
rem: (O) May be inoperative, damaged, or missing provided proper installation and operation is verified PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: Captain to brief Lead Flight Attendant/Purser that the crewmember assign to the affected position has an equivelant operative flashlight readily available. 25-60-08B PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE: Captain to brief Lead Flight Attendant/Purser that one or more flashlight(s) have inoperative, damaged, or missing seals and proper installation and operation has to be verified.
ptext: FLASHLIGHT AND/OR HOLDER IS INOP OR MISSING SEALS OR TAGS INOP / DAMAGED / MISSING

name: Megaphones - Tamper Seals or Tags
rem: (O) May be inoperative, damaged, or missing provided proper installation and operation is verified PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE: 1. Captain to brief Lead Flight Attendant/Purser that one or more megaphone have inoperative,damaged, or missing seals and proper installation and operation has to be verified 2. Verify megaphone operation by: A. Raise volume to desired level to avoid “screeching.” (If applicable.) B. Hold narrow end 2-3 inches from your mouth. C. Press trigger. Note: When operating smaller, orange and white megaphone, squeeze the handle. (The front and back portion of handle will move together.) D. Speak clearly into the megaphone to affrim its operation.

name: Slide Raft Lanyards (White and/or Yellow)
rem: (O) (M) May be missing or damaged beyond serviceable limits provided aircraft is not operated on extended overwater flights.
