oproc: 1. Do not operate aircraft on extended overwater flights. 2. Brief cabin crew on the overwater equipment that is missing or damaged beyond serviceable limits.
mproc: 1. If the lanyard is chaffed, abraded or severed less than 25% of the actual width or cross section area, it is considered serviceable. No action is required. 2. If the lanyard is chaffed, abraded or severed greater than 25% of the actual width or cross section area, it is considered unserviceable and must be replaced or placarded inoperative.

name: Automated External Defibrillator (AED) and/or Associated Equipment
rem: (O) May be incomplete, inoperative or removed provided: 1. AED is labeled or placarded in a manner that will identify it as a unit that cannot be mistaken for a fully serviceable unit, 2. Location placarding is removed or obscured, and 3. Repairs or replacements are made within 1 flight. Note: An expired item must be removed from the aircraft. Note: Medical equipment installed in the aircraft as part of an Emergency Medical Service (EMS) operation is not considered part of the normal complement of equipment. No MEL relief applies to that equipment and 14 CFR maintenance and inspection requirements do not apply.

name: Automated External Defibrillator (AED) and/or Associated Equipment (Battery Low and/or Service Indication)
rem: (O) (M) May display fault indication provided AED is checked PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE to ensure AED is usable for flight. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Automated External Defibrillator (AED) and/ or Associated Equipment (Electrode Sets)
rem: (M) One set may be inoperative or removed. Note: An expired item must be removed from the aircraft.

name: Automated External Defibrillator (AED) Tamper Seals or Tags
rem: (O) May be inoperative, damaged, or missing provided proper servicing is verified at each preflight. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard) PAGE

name: Enhanced Emergency Medical Kit (EEMK) and/or Associated Equipment
rem: (O) Both may be incomplete or removed provided: 1. EEMK is labeled or placarded in a manner that will identify it as a unit that cannot be mistaken for a fully serviceable unit, 2. Location placarding is removed or obscured, and 3. Repairs or replacements are made within 1 flight. Note: An expired item must be removed from the aircraft. Note: Medical equipment installed in the aircraft as part of an EMS operation is not considered part of the normal complement of equipment. No MMEL relief applies to that equipment and 14 CFR maintenance and inspection requirements do not apply.

name: Enhanced Emergency Medical Kit (EEMK) and/or Associated Equipment
rem: May be incomplete or removed. Note: An expired item must be removed from the aircraft.

name: EEMK Tamper Seals or Tags
rem: (O) May be inoperative, damaged, or missing provided proper installation and operation is verified PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: First Aid Kits (FAK) and/or Associated Equipment
rem: (O) May be incomplete or removed provided: 1. The FAK is labeled or placarded in a manner that will identify it as a unit that cannot be mistaken for a fully serviceable unit, 2. Location placarding is removed or obscured, and 3. Repairs or replacements are made within 1 flight. Note: An expired item must be removed from the aircraft. Note: Medical equipment installed in the aircraft as part of an EMS operation is not considered part of the normal complement of equipment. No MEL relief applies to that equipment and 14 CFR maintenance and inspection requirements do not apply. PAGE

name: First Aid Kits (FAK) and/or Associated Equipment
rem: (O) May be incomplete or removed provided: 1. The FAK is labeled or placarded in a manner that will identify it as a unit that cannot be mistaken for a fully serviceable unit, 2. Location placarding is removed or obscured, and 3. Repairs or replacements are made within 1 flight. Note: An expired item must be removed from the aircraft. Note: Medical equipment installed in the aircraft as part of an EMS operation is not considered part of the normal complement of equipment. No MEL relief applies to that equipment and 14 CFR maintenance and inspection requirements do not apply.

name: First Aid Kits (FAK) Tamper Seals or Tags
rem: (O) May be inoperative, damaged, or missing provided proper FAK servicing is verified PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Red Pouch (Medical Accessory Pouch)
rem: May be incomplete or removed. Note: This Medical Accessory Pouch is in excess of those required by FAR.

name: EEMK/AED Bracket / Lock
rem: May be inoperative. Note: If Bracket/Lock is inoperative in a manner that prevents the associated EEMK or AED from being accessible, the affected EEMK and/or AED must be considered inoperative and deferred by the appropriate MEL in this section. PAGE

name: Overwing (For A319 Aircraft)
rem: (O) May be damaged or missing provided aircraft is not operated on extended overwater flights.

name: Overwing (For A320 and A321NEO Aircraft)
rem: (O) May be damaged or missing provided aircraft is not operated on extended overwater flights.

name: Escape Life Lines Flight Deck Escape Life Line Cover Plates
placard: ESCAPE LIFE LINE DAMAGED OR MISSING ESCAPE LIFE LINE DAMAGED OR MISSING DECK ESCAPE LIFE LINE COVER PLATES DAMAGED OR MISSING
rem: May be damaged or missing.
oproc: 1. Do not operate aircraft on extended overwater flights. 2. Brief cabin crew on the overwater equipment that is missing.
ptext: ESCAPE LIFE LINE DAMAGED OR MISSING ESCAPE LIFE LINE DAMAGED OR MISSING DECK ESCAPE LIFE LINE COVER PLATES DAMAGED OR MISSING

name: Passenger/Crew Life Vest
rem: (M) One life vest required for each passenger and crewmember. If a Passenger Life Vest is not available, seat(s) must be placarded “UNSERVICEABLE – DO NOT USE” and deferred per MEL 25-21-01A. Note: Universal life vests are installed in overwater aircraft and are acceptable for all occupants, including infants, under 35 pounds.

name: Passenger Life Vests
rem: May be inoperative or missing provided aircraft is not operated on extended overwater flights. Note: An expired item must be removed from the aircraft. Note: Universal life vests are installed in overwater aircraft and are acceptable for all occupants, including infants, under 35 pounds.

name: Spare Life Vests
rem: May be inoperative or missing. Note: An expired item must be removed from the aircraft. Note: Universal life vests are installed in overwater aircraft and are acceptable for all occupants, including infants, under 35 pounds.

name: Life Rafts (If equipped)
rem: (O) May be inoperative or missing. Refer to Airway Manual 4.2.13.1 Limited Overwater Operations for requirements and limitations. Note: An expired item must be removed from the aircraft. Note: Performance corrections must be applied if Life Raft(s) is removed.

name: Overwater Survival Kits Broken Seal
rem: (O) May continue in service after being opened (seal broken) provided it is inventoried prior to each overwater departure for minimum contents.

name: Overwater Survival Kits
rem: (O) May be inoperative or missing provided: 1. Aircraft is not operated on extended overwater flights, 2. Operations solely within the state of Alaska over uninhabited areas are Not Authorized, and 3. Dispatch is prohibited for flights to and from Alaska.
oproc: Inventory overwater survival kit prior to each overwater departure to ensure it contains the minimum required items as follows: 1. One hand inflation pump. 2. One canopy and pole assembly. 3. One small plastic sealed module. 4. One large plastic sealed module. Note: If the plastic sealed module’s plastic cover is intact, the contents are considered secure and no further inventory is necessary. 25-60-17B 1. Brief cabin crew on the overwater equipment that is missing. 2. Do not operate aircraft on extended overwater flights. 3. Operations solely within the state of Alaska over uninhabited areas are Not Authorized, and 4. Dispatch is prohibited for flights to and from Alaska.

name: Emergency Locator Transmitter – Survival (Portable) Type ELTs (ADT 406 S or Safran Orlia Ultima S)
rem: (O) One may be inoperative or missing provided: 1. One fixed or survival (portable) ELT transmitter is operative, and 2. Departures to a country other than the United States or Canada are Not Authorized. Note: An expired item must be removed from the aircraft.

name: Emergency Locator Transmitter – Survival (Portable) Type ELTs (ADT 406 S or Safran Orlia Ultima
rem: (O) One on the A321 or two on the A319/A320 may be inoperative or missing provided: 1. Departures to a country other than the United States or Canada are Not Authorized, 2. Operations solely within the state of Alaska over uninhabited areas are Not Authorized, and 3. Dispatch is prohibited for flights to and from Alaska. Note: An expired item must be removed from the aircraft. Note: A321 Fixed ELT is deferred per MEL 25-61-01E.

name: Emergency Locator Transmitter – Survival (Portable) Type ELTs (ADT 406 S Plastic Cover)
rem: (O) One or both may be damaged or missing. Note: An expired item must be removed from the aircraft.

name: Emergency Locator Transmitter - Fixed ELTs (With Survival [Portable] Type ELT [ADT 406 S or Safran Orlia Ultima S] Operative)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative or missing provided: 1. System is deactivated if not missing, 2. Repairs are made within 90 consecutive calendar-days, and 3. Placard stating “FIXED ELT NOT INSTALLED” is placed in view of the pilot. 4. Survival (Portable) ELT transmitter is operative, and 5. Departures to a country other than the United States or Canada are Not Authorized. Note: An expired item must be removed from the aircraft. PAGE

name: Emergency Locator Transmitter - Fixed ELTs (With Survival [Portable] Type ELT [ADT 406 S or Safran Orlia Ultima S] Inoperative)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. System is deactivated if not missing, 2. Repairs are made within 90 consecutive calendar-days, 3. Placard stating “FIXED ELT NOT INSTALLED” is placed in view of the pilot, 4. Departures to a country other than the United States or Canada are Not Authorized, 5. Operations solely within the state of Alaska over uninhabited areas are Not Authorized, and 6. Dispatch is prohibited for flights to and from Alaska. Note: An expired item must be removed from the aircraft. Note: Survival (Portable) ELT is deferred per MEL 25-61-01B.

name: Emergency Locator Transmitter – Remote ELT Switch
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Remote ELT switch is deactivated, and 2. ELT switch is placed in the ARMED mode.

name: Emergency Locator Transmitter – ELT Indicator Light
rem: One or more may be inoperative.

name: Emergency Locator Transmitter – ELT Aural Alert
rem: One or more may be inoperative.

name: Emergency Locator Transmitter – Low Frequency Under Water Locator Beacon (LF-ULB) (For A321CEO Ships 1001-1029 and 3033-3098) (For All A321NEO)
rem: May be inoperative provided operations do not require its use.

name: Equipment for Location of an Aircraft in Distress (ELT-
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. System is deactivated, and 2. Repairs are made within 90 consecutive calendar-days.

name: Equipment for Location of an Aircraft in Distress (ELT-
rem: (M) May be missing provided: 1. Placard stating “ELT not installed” is placed in view of the pilot, and 2. Repairs are made within 90 consecutive calendar-days. Note: An expired item must be removed from the aircraft. PAGE

name: Equipment for Location of an Aircraft in Distress (ELT-
placard: LOCATOR TRANSMITTER INOPERATIVE or MISSING LOCATOR TRANSMITTERS INOPERATIVE or MISSING LOCATOR TRANSMITTER(S) PLASTIC DAMAGED or MISSING FIXED ELT NOT INSTALLED FIXED ELT NOT INSTALLED REMOTE ELT SWITCH INOP ELT INDICATOR LIGHT INOP ELT AURAL ALERT INOP NOT INSTALLED OR INOP ELT-DT INOP ELT-DT INOP ELT-DT INOP
rem: May be inoperative provided that at least one automatic ELT is operative.
oproc: 1. Captain to brief Lead Flight Attendant/Purser on the overwater equipment that is inoperative or missing. 25-61-01B, 25-61-01E 1. Captain to brief Lead Flight Attendant/Purser on the equipment that is inoperative or missing. 2. Operations solely within the state of Alaska over uninhabited areas are Not Authorized, and 3. Dispatch is prohibited for flights to and from Alaska. 25-61-01C Captain to brief Lead Flight Attendant/Purser to inspect the affected ELT to ensure the ON/OFF/ARMED switch is in the ARMED position.
mproc: 1. If the ELT is not missing, deactivate the fixed ELT by complying with AMM Task 25-65-35-000-001-A. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE
ptext: LOCATOR TRANSMITTER INOPERATIVE or MISSING LOCATOR TRANSMITTERS INOPERATIVE or MISSING LOCATOR TRANSMITTER(S) PLASTIC DAMAGED or MISSING FIXED ELT NOT INSTALLED FIXED ELT NOT INSTALLED REMOTE ELT SWITCH INOP ELT INDICATOR LIGHT INOP ELT AURAL ALERT INOP NOT INSTALLED OR INOP ELT-DT INOP ELT-DT INOP ELT-DT INOP

name: Fireproof Gloves
rem: May be damaged or missing.

name: Printed Supplemental Safety Information
rem: (O) (M) May be missing or damaged provided: 1. Safety Information Card is located in convenient locations for use of each passenger, 2. Cards cannot be missing from each exit seat, and 3. Any seat(s) or row(s) of seats must be blocked and deferred per MEL 25-21-01A where a Safety Information Card is not located in convenient locations for use of each passenger.
oproc: 1. Captain to brief Lead Flight Attendant/Purser on the location of missing or damaged Safety Information Card(s).
mproc: 1. Safety Information Cards must be available at each exit seat. 2. Safety Information Cards must be placed in a convenient location for use by each passenger. 3. Defer any passenger seat per 25-21-01A if a Safety Information Card cannot be placed in a convenient location for use by passenger occupying the seat.

name: PED Mount
rem: Reconfigure cockpit for two (2) operative PED mounts. See TOPP 40-40-05 for instructions.

name: PED Mount
rem: Reconfigure cockpit for one (1) operative PED mount. See TOPP 40-40-05 for instructions.

name: PED Mount
rem: Reconfigure cockpit for zero (0) operative PED mounts. See TOPP 40-40-05 for instructions.

name: Fault(s) indicated by SDCU
rem: Note: Dispatch with maintenance status message displayed on ECAM is permitted without CFDS interrogation.

name: Fault(s) indicated by SMOKE
placard: STATUS MESSAGE “SDCU” DISPLAYED STATUS MESSAGE “SMOKE” DISPLAYED
rem: Note: Dispatch with maintenance status message displayed on ECAM is permitted without CFDS interrogation.
ptext: STATUS MESSAGE “SDCU” DISPLAYED STATUS MESSAGE “SMOKE” DISPLAYED

name: Loop A
rem: (O) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS) beyond 120 minutes, one may be inoperative on each engine provided: 1. Associated Loop B is operative, 2. Upon initial MEL application, review associated operative loop history with MCC, and 3. The engine fire test is performed PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Loop B
rem: (O) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS) beyond 120 minutes, one may be inoperative on each engine provided: 1. Associated Loop A is operative, 2. Upon initial MEL application, review associated operative loop history with MCC, and 3. The engine fire test is performed PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE: 1. Engine Fire Detection Loop A inoperative on Engine 1 A. In case of one engine taxi, the APU generator must be used. B. The engine fire test is made before before each flight. Note: Disregard the FIRE light 1 located on the ENG MASTER panel (pedestal) C. One engine taxi at departure: i. After first engine start: a. APU is left ON Note: APU generator must be used. ii. After second engine start: a. APU set as required. D. One engine taxi on arrival: i. Before shutting down the first engine: a. APU is ON. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: FIRE Lights On ENG Control Panel
rem: One or both may be inoperative.

name: Bulbs/LEDs
rem: Four bulbs/LEDs in each pushbutton switch may be inoperative.

name: APU Fire Detection System
rem: (O) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS) beyond 120 minutes, detection loop (B) may be inoperative provided BEFORE EACH APU START APU fire test is performed. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: APU Fire Detection System
rem: (O) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS) beyond 120 minutes, detection loop (A) may be inoperative provided: 1. BEFORE EACH APU START APU fire test is performed, and 2. During ground operations, APU condition is monitored in the cockpit. Note: APU Auto Fire Extinguishing feature not available. When the flight crew leaves the aircraft, maintenance must monitor the APU. If maintenance is not available, the flight crew must shut down the APU. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: APU Fire Detection System
rem: (O) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS) beyond 120 minutes, both loops (A and B) may be inoperative provided: 1. APU is not used, and 2. Repairs are made within four flights. Note: Two air carts required for engine start, unless a single cart is high volume output.

name: APU Fire Detection System
rem: (O) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS), both loops (A and B) may be inoperative provided APU is not used. Note: Two air carts required for engine start, unless a single cart is high volume output. PAGE

name: Bulbs/LEDs
rem: Four bulbs/LEDs in push button may be inoperative.

name: Bulbs/LEDs
rem: (O) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS), more than four bulbs/LEDs may be inoperative provided APU is not used. Note: Two air carts required for engine start, unless a single cart is high volume output.
oproc: 1. Do not use APU. 2. APU MASTER switch – OFF. 3. External electrical and pneumatic power are required for normal starting. 4. Reference AOM VOL I Supplementary Procedures section for External Air/CrossBleed Start procedures. 5. If an APU takeoff is specified on the WDR: A. Select both pack pb-sw OFF prior to takeoff B. At the THR RED altitude, place PACK 1 pb-sw ON C. When flaps are selected UP, place PACK 2 pb-sw ON Note: Expect to receive PACK 1(2) OFF ECAM message after T.O. INHIBIT is removed at 1,500 feet AFE.

name: Avionic Smoke Detection System
rem: (O) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS), may be inoperative for three landings only.
oproc: 1. Disregard: A. On the VENTILATION overhead panel: the BLOWER pb-sw FAULT light and the EXTRACT pb-sw FAULT light. B. On the EMER ELEC PWR overhead panel: the GEN 1 LINE pb-sw SMOKE light. 2. In the case of an electrical smoke: Apply the AVNCS SMOKE emergency procedure. Refer to QRH/ABN-27 Smoke / Fumes / AVNCS Smoke.

name: Smoke Detectors in FWD Cargo Compartment
rem: (O) Any or all may be inoperative provided PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE procedures are used to ensure the FWD cargo compartment remains empty, or is verified to contain only empty cargo handling equipment, ballast, and/or fly-away kits (see MEL Instructions Section). Note: Failure of a single detector in each cavity is indicated by a MAINTENANCE STATUS message. Note: When this MEL is applied, on A321 NEO aircraft ONLY, restricting use of the entire FWD Cargo Compartment prohibits carrying Motorized Wheelchairs and Sharp, Dense, Heavy (SDH) items on-board aircraft. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, Captain is to ensure that no cargo is loaded in the FWD cargo compartment. Note: When this MEL is applied, on A321 NEO aircraft ONLY, restricting use of the entire FWD Cargo Compartment prohibits carrying Motorized Wheelchairs and Sharp, Dense, Heavy (SDH) items on-board aircraft.

name: Smoke Detectors in AFT Cargo Compartment
rem: (O) (M) Any or all may be inoperative provided PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE procedures are used to ensure the AFT cargo compartment remains empty, or is verified to contain only empty cargo handling equipment, ballast, and/or fly-away kits (see MEL Instructions Section). Note: Failure of a single detector in each cavity is indicated by a MAINTENANCE STATUS message. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, Captain is to ensure that no cargo is loaded in the AFT cargo compartment.
mproc: 1. Verify AOG Parts Stowage Container in aft cargo compartment is empty. 2. Place a strip of Red Colored OM2 UNSERVICEABLE – DO NOT USE tape (or equivalent*) across the container. Note: An acceptable alternate to the OM2 tape is a section of 2 inch wide masking tape with the words “UNSERVICEABLE – DO NOT USE” printed legibly in 1 inch black letters.

name: Lavatory Smoke Detection System
rem: (O) (M) For each lavatory, the lavatory smoke detection system may be inoperative provided: 1. Lavatory waste receptacle is empty, 2. Lavatory door is locked closed and placarded, UNSERVICEABLE – DO NOT USE, and 3. Lavatory is used only by crewmembers. Note: These provisos are not intended to prohibit lavatory use or inspections by crewmembers. Ensure lavatory door is locked closed.
oproc: 1. Captain to brief Lead Flight Attendant/Purser that the affected lavatory door is to remain locked and not entered except for use or inspection by crewmembers. 2. Captain is to ensure the lavatory door is locked closed if crewmember inspection has been performed or if the lavatory is used by a crewmember.
mproc: 1. Check that the lavatory waste container is empty. 2. Close and lock the Lavatory door as follows: A. Lift the LAVATORY sign on the outer face of the lavatory door. B. Move the latch under the sign until the indicator shows the door is locked (OCCUPIED). C. Check that the door will not open. 3. Place a strip of Red Colored OM2 UNSERVICEABLE – DO NOT USE tape (or equivalent*) ON the lavatory door. DO NOT tape shut door. This allows crew members to access lavatory. Note: An acceptable alternate to the OM2 tape is a section of 2 inch wide masking tape with the words “UNSERVICEABLE – DO NOT USE” printed legibly in 1 inch black letters. 4. Place a strip of 2 inch wide masking tape with the words “CREW USE ONLY” printed legibly in 1 inch black letters ON the lavatory door. DO NOT tape door shut. This allows crew members to access lavatory.

name: Video Control Center Standalone Smoke Detection System
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided the Video Control Center is deactivated.
oproc: 1. Captain to brief Lead Flight Attendant/Purser that it will be necessary to do the pre–takeoff briefings and emergency equipment demonstrations verbally. 2. Ensure PAX SYS pb switch or PAX PERSONAL ELEC SPLY pb switch on overhead panel is OFF. Note: Selecting the PAX SYS pb or PAX PERSONAL ELEC SPLY pb to OFF deactivates the electric seat control for all business class seats.
mproc: 1. Deactivation of the VCC Smoke Detection System: A. With aircraft electrical circuits energized, in the cockpit, on the overhead panel 56VU, put the PAX SYS pb switch or PAX PERSONAL ELEC SPLY pb switch to the OFF position. Note: Selecting the PAX SYS pb or PAX PERSONAL ELEC SPLY pb to OFF deactivates the electric seat control for all business class seats. Figure 26-1: 56VU Panel

name: Engine AGENT 1 & 2 DISCH Light Systems
rem: (M) One may be inoperative for each engine provided associated bottle(s) is verified properly charged before the FIRST FLIGHT OF EACH DAY (Log Book Entry Required). Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
mproc: 1. DAILY CHECK (BEFORE FIRST FLIGHT OF EACH DAY): A. Perform AMM Task 26-21-00-040-001.

name: Engine AGENT 1 & 2 SQUIB Light Systems
rem: (M) Any or all may be inoperative provided it is verified that the failure is in the test circuit only.
mproc: 1. Perform AMM Task 26-21-00-040-003.

name: ENG FIRE Test Systems
rem: (M) One test function may be inoperative provided: 1. The fault is in the test system only, 2. System is tested ONCE EACH FLIGHT DAY (Log Book Entry Required), and 3. All other functions of fire detection systems operate normally. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
mproc: 1. DAILY CHECK (Once Each Flight Day): A. Engine Fire Extinguisher System i. Perform AMM Task 26-21-00-040-002.

name: APU Fire Extinguisher System
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided the APU is not used. Note: Two air carts required for engine start, unless a single cart is high volume output.
oproc: 1. Do not use APU. 2. APU MASTER switch – OFF. 3. External electrical and pneumatic power are required for normal starting. 4. Reference Operations Manual Volume 1 Supplementary Procedures section for APU Inoperative (Bleed and Generator) Engine Start procedures. If an APU Takeoff is specified on the WDR, reference Operations Manual, Volume 1, Packs Off for Takeoff.

name: APU Agent DISCH Light
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided bottle is verified properly charged before the FIRST FLIGHT OF EACH DAY (Log Book Entry Required). Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: APU Agent DISCH Light
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided APU is not used. Note: Two air carts required for engine start, unless a single cart is high volume output.
oproc: 1. Do not use APU. 2. APU MASTER switch – OFF. 3. External electrical and pneumatic power are required for normal starting. 4. Reference AOM VOL I Supplementary Procedures section for External Air/CrossBleed Start procedures. 5. If an APU takeoff is specified on the WDR: A. Select both pack pb-sw OFF prior to takeoff B. At the THR RED altitude, place PACK 1 pb-sw ON C. When flaps are selected UP, place PACK 2 pb-sw ON Note: Expect to receive PACK 1(2) OFF ECAM message after T.O. INHIBIT is removed at 1,500 feet AFE.
mproc: 1. DAILY CHECK (BEFORE FIRST FLIGHT OF EACH DAY): A. Perform AMM Task 26-22-00-040-004.

name: APU SQUIB Light
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided APU extinguishing system firing circuit is verified operative before the FIRST FLIGHT OF EACH DAY (Log Book Entry Required). Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: APU SQUIB Light
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided APU is not used. Note: Two air carts required for engine start, unless a single cart is high volume output
oproc: 1. Do not use APU. 2. APU MASTER switch – OFF. 3. External electrical and pneumatic power are required for normal starting. 4. Reference Operations Manual Volume 1Supplementary Procedures section for APU Inoperative (Bleed and Generator) Engine Start procedures. 5. If an APU takeoff is specified on the WDR: A. Select both pack pb-sw OFF prior to takeoff B. At the THR RED altitude, place PACK 1 pb-sw ON C. When flaps are selected UP, place PACK 2 pb-sw ON Note: Expect to receive PACK 1(2) OFF ECAM message after T.O. INHIBIT is removed at 1,500 feet AFE.
mproc: 1. DAILY CHECK (BEFORE FIRST FLIGHT OF EACH DAY): A. Perform AMM Task 26-22-00-040-005.

name: APU Ground Automatic Fire Extinguisher System
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided APU is continuously monitored in the flight deck during all APU ground operations. Note: When the flight crew leaves the aircraft, maintenance must monitor the APU. If maintenance is not available, the flight crew must shut down the APU.

name: APU Ground Automatic Fire Extinguisher System
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided APU is not used. Note: Two air carts required for engine start, unless a single cart is high volume output
oproc: 1. Flight crew or maintenance personnel to monitor APU on the flight deck during ground operations. 2. When the flight crew leaves the aircraft, maintenance must monitor the APU. If maintenance is not available, the flight crew must shut down the APU. 26-22-03B 1. Do not use APU. 2. APU MASTER switch – OFF. 3. External electrical and pneumatic power are required for normal starting. 4. Reference Operations Manual Volume 1Supplementary Procedures section for APU Inoperative (Bleed and Generator) Engine Start procedures. 5. If an APU takeoff is specified on the WDR: A. Select both pack pb-sw OFF prior to takeoff B. At the THR RED altitude, place PACK 1 pb-sw ON C. When flaps are selected UP, place PACK 2 pb-sw ON Note: Expect to receive PACK 1(2) OFF ECAM message after T.O. INHIBIT is removed at 1,500 feet AFE.

name: APU Fire Test System
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided firing circuit and bottle low pressure detection systems are verified operative before the FIRST FLIGHT OF EACH DAY (Log Book Entry Required). Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: APU Fire Test System
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided APU is not used. Note: Two air carts required for engine start, unless a single cart is high volume output.
oproc: 1. Do not use APU. 2. APU MASTER switch – OFF. 3. External electrical and pneumatic power are required for normal starting. 4. Reference Operations Manual Volume 1Supplementary Procedures section for APU Inoperative (Bleed and Generator) Engine Start procedures. 5. If an APU takeoff is specified on the WDR: A. Select both pack pb-sw OFF prior to takeoff B. At the THR RED altitude, place PACK 1 pb-sw ON C. When flaps are selected UP, place PACK 2 pb-sw ON Note: Expect to receive PACK 1(2) OFF ECAM message after T.O. INHIBIT is removed at 1,500 feet AFE.
mproc: 1. DAILY CHECK (BEFORE FIRST FLIGHT OF EACH DAY): A. Perform AMM Task 26-22-00-040-012.

name: APU Fire Extinguisher Overpressure Indication (Red Disc)
rem: (M) May be missing provided: 1. Squib test is used to verify squib integrity, and 2. Bottle pressure switch is verified operative before the FIRST FLIGHT OF EACH DAY (Log Book Entry Required). Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: APU Fire Extinguisher Overpressure Indication (Red Disc)
rem: (O) May be missing provided APU is not used. Note: Two air carts required for engine start, unless a single cart is high volume output.
oproc: 1. Do not use APU. 2. APU MASTER switch – OFF. 3. External electrical and pneumatic power are required for normal starting. 4. Reference Operations Manual Volume 1Supplementary Procedures section for APU Inoperative (Bleed and Generator) Engine Start procedures. 5. If an APU takeoff is specified on the WDR: A. Select both pack pb-sw OFF prior to takeoff B. At the THR RED altitude, place PACK 1 pb-sw ON C. When flaps are selected UP, place PACK 2 pb-sw ON Note: Expect to receive PACK 1(2) OFF ECAM message after T.O. INHIBIT is removed at 1,500 feet AFE.
mproc: 1. Verify squib integrity by performing AMM Task 26-22-00-040-008. 2. Verify bottle pressure switch by performing AMM Task 26-22-00-040-007. 3. DAILY CHECK (BEFORE FIRST FLIGHT OF EACH DAY): A. Verify bottle pressure switch by performing AMM Task 26-22-00-040-007.

name: APU FIRE Light on External Power Panel (APU Automatic Fire Extinguishing System Verified Operative)
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided the APU Automatic Fire Extinguishing System is verified operative.

name: APU FIRE Light on External Power Panel (APU Automatic Fire Extinguishing System NOT Verified Operative)
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided APU is continuously monitored in the flight deck during all APU ground operations. Note: When the flight crew leaves the aircraft, maintenance must monitor the APU. If maintenance is not available, the flight crew must shut down the APU.
oproc: 1. Flight crew or maintenance personnel to monitor APU on the flight deck during ground operations. 2. When the flight crew leaves the aircraft, maintenance must monitor the APU. If maintenance is not available, the flight crew must shut down the APU.
mproc: 1. Place a warning notice in the flight deck in position on overhead panel 25VU to tell person(s) not to operate the APU. 2. Accomplish the following Operational Test of the Test Circuit. ACTION RESULT 1. On the APU/AUTO EXTING section of panel 50 VU: On the APU/AUTO EXTING section of panel 50 VU: ● Push the TEST ON pushbutton during the test. ● The OK legend in the TEST pushbutton switch illuminates. Note: When the OK legend in the TEST pushbutton switch illuminates, all components operate correctly. 2. On the APU/AUTO EXTING section of panel On the APU/AUTO EXTING section of panel 50VU: 50 VU: ● Push the RESET pushbutton switch. ● The test circuit is reset. 3. Put aircraft back to original configuration.

name: Bottle 1
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE procedures are used to ensure all compartments remain empty, or is verified to contain only empty cargo handling equipment, ballast, and/or fly-away kits (see MEL Instructions Section). Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Bottle 2
rem: (O) Bottle 2 may be inoperative (and cargo compartment used) provided airplane remains within 60 minutes of landing at a suitable airport.

name: Bottle 2
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE procedures are used to ensure all compartments remain empty, or is verified to contain only empty cargo handling equipment, ballast, and/or fly-away kits (see MEL Instructions Section). Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Squib of Cargo Bottle 1
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE procedures are used to ensure all compartments remain empty, or is verified to contain only empty cargo handling equipment, ballast, and/or fly-away kits (see MEL Instructions Section). Note: When a squib of cargo bottle 1 linked to AFT Cargo Compartment is inoperative, both AFT and BULK cargo compartments remain empty or are verified to contain only empty cargo handling equipment, ballast (ballast may be loaded in ULDs), and/or Fly Away Kits. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Squib of Cargo Bottle 2
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided Bottle 2 is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 26-23-01B or 26-23-01C as applicable. PAGE

name: FWD Cargo Compartment DISCH/BTL Light
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided an acceptable test procedure is used ONCE EACH FLIGHT DAY (Log Book Entry Required) to verify that bottle is properly charged. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: FWD Cargo Compartment DISCH Light
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE procedures are used to ensure FWD cargo compartment remains empty, or is verified to contain only empty cargo handling equipment, ballast, and/or fly-away kits (see MEL Instructions Section). Note: When this MEL is applied, on A321 NEO aircraft ONLY, restricting use of the entire FWD Cargo Compartment prohibits carrying Motorized Wheelchairs and Sharp, Dense, Heavy (SDH) items on-board aircraft. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: AFT Cargo Compartment DISCH Light
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided an acceptable test procedure is used ONCE EACH FLIGHT DAY (Log Book Entry Required) to verify that bottle is properly charged. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: AFT Cargo Compartment DISCH Light
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE procedures are used to ensure the AFT cargo compartment remains empty, or is verified to contain only empty cargo handling equipment, ballast, and/or fly-away kits (see MEL Instructions Section). Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, Captain is to ensure that no cargo is loaded in the FWD cargo compartment. Note: When this MEL is applied, on A321 NEO aircraft ONLY, restricting use of the entire FWD Cargo Compartment prohibits carrying Motorized Wheelchairs and Sharp, Dense, Heavy (SDH) items on-board aircraft. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Lavatory Waste Bin Fire Extinguisher System
rem: (M) For each lavatory, the fire extinguisher system may be inoperative provided lavatory smoke detection system operates normally.

name: Lavatory Waste Bin Fire Extinguisher System
rem: (O) (M) For one lavatory, the fire extinguisher system may be inoperative provided: 1. Lavatory waste receptacle is empty, 2. Lavatory door is locked and placarded, UNSERVICEABLE – DO NOT USE, and 3. Lavatory is used only by crewmembers. Note: These provisos are not intended to prohibit lavatory use or inspections by crewmembers. Ensure lavatory door is locked closed after crewmember inspection.
oproc: 1. Captain to brief Lead Flight Attendant/Purser that the affected lavatory door is to remain locked and not entered except for use or inspection by crewmembers. 2. Captain is to ensure the lavatory door is locked closed if crewmember inspection has been performed or if the lavatory is used by a crewmember.
mproc: 1. Verify the Lavatory Smoke Detector is operational by performing AMM Task 26-17-00-710-001. 26-25-01B: 1. Close and lock the Lavatory door as follows: A. Make sure lavatory waste receptacle is empty. B. Close the lavatory door and lock by placing a slotted screwdriver blade in the slot in the VACANT/OCCUPIED sign on the door and move to OCCUPIED position. Check that door will not open. C. Place a strip of Red Colored OM2 UNSERVICEABLE – DO NOT USE tape (or equivalent*) ON the lavatory door. DO NOT tape door shut. This allows crew members to access lavatory. Note: An acceptable alternate to the OM2 tape is a section of 2 inch wide masking tape with the words “UNSERVICEABLE – DO NOT USE” printed legibly in 1 inch black letters. D. Place a strip of 2 inch wide masking tape with the words “CREW USE ONLY” printed legibly in 1 inch black letters ON the lavatory door. DO NOT tape door shut. This allows crew members to access lavatory.

name: Portable Fire Extinguishers
rem: (M) One forward passenger compartment fire extinguisher may be inoperative or removed provided: 1. Inoperative fire extinguisher remains in a certified location until removed from the aircraft at the next suitable maintenance facility, 2. Location placarding is removed or obscured, and 3. Required distribution is maintained. Note: An expired item must be removed from the aircraft. Note: Inoperative fire extinguishers, removed from a certified location or removed from the aircraft, are subject to 49 CFR dangerous goods regulations.

name: Portable Fire Extinguisher Tamper Seals or Tags
placard: FIRE EXTINGUISHER MISSING FIRE EXTINGUISHER TAMPER SEALS OR INOP/DAMAGED/MISSING
rem: (O) May be inoperative, damaged, or missing provided proper installation and servicing is verified PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: 1. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE: A. Captain to brief Lead Flight Attendant/Purser that one or more Portable Fire Extinguisher Tamper Seal or Tag is inoperative, damaged, or missing and proper installation and servicing has to be verified.
mproc: 1. Inoperative fire extinguisher remains in a certified location until removed from the aircraft at the next suitable maintenance facility, 2. Location placarding is removed or obscured with tape.
ptext: FIRE EXTINGUISHER MISSING FIRE EXTINGUISHER TAMPER SEALS OR INOP/DAMAGED/MISSING

name: Fault(s) Indicated by F/CTL
rem: Note: Dispatch with maintenance status message displayed on ECAM is permitted without CFDS interrogation.

name: Fault(s) Indicated by SFCS
rem: Note: Dispatch with maintenance status message displayed on ECAM is permitted without CFDS interrogation.

name: Fault(s) Indicated by SLAT
rem: Note: Dispatch with maintenance status message displayed on ECAM is permitted without CFDS interrogation.

name: Fault(s) Indicated by FLAP
placard: STATUS MESSAGE “F/CTL” DISPLAYED STATUS MESSAGE “SFCS” DISPLAYED STATUS MESSAGE “SLAT SYNCH 1(2)” DISPLAYED STATUS MESSAGE “FLAP SYNCH 1(2)” DISPLAYED
rem: Note: Dispatch with maintenance status message displayed on ECAM is permitted without CFDS interrogation.

name: Aileron Servo Controls (Associated with ELAC 2)
rem: (O) (M) Two associated with ELAC 2 (left green and right blue) may be inoperative provided: 1. Servos remain mechanically connected and hydraulically supplied (damping function is not affected), 2. All roll spoilers (spoilers 2 thru 5 are roll spoilers) operate normally, 3. Aileron Servo Controls associated with ELAC 1 operates normally, and 4. Repairs are made within three flight days. Note: Disregard F/CTL/AILERON SERVO FAULT. Note: If F/CTL Class II Message is displayed see MEL 27-00-00A.

name: Aileron Servo Controls (Associated with ELAC 1)
rem: (O) (M) Two associated with ELAC 1 (left blue and right green) may be inoperative provided: 1. Servos remain mechanically connected and hydraulically supplied (damping function is not affected), 2. All roll spoilers (spoilers 2 thru 5 are roll spoilers) operate normally, 3. Aileron Servo Controls associated with ELAC 2 operate normally, 4. TR1 and TR2 operate normally, 5. DC TIE contactor 1 is verified closed PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, and 6. Repairs are made within three flight days. Note: Disregard F/CTL/AILERON SERVO FAULT. Note: If F/CTL Class II Message is displayed see MEL 27-00-00A. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: 1. The F/CTL ELAC2 FAULT alert may display on the EWD after the first engine start. 2. Check that the F/CTL ELAC2 FAULT alert is not displayed after the second engine start. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: ECAM Aileron Position Indications
rem: (O) One Or both may be inoperative provided capability to move affected aileron through each servo control is verified visually PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: ECAM Aileron Actuator Indications
placard: POSITION INDICATION INOP ACTUATOR INDICATION INOP
rem: Any or all may be inoperative.
oproc: PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, and before engine start, visually verify that each servo control is capable of moving the affected aileron by: 1. Verify ailerons are clear. 2. YELLOW ELEC PUMP– ON. 3. PTU – Check AUTO. 4. BLUE ELEC PUMP –Check AUTO. 5. BLUE PUMP OVRD –ON. 6. ELAC 1 and 2 – ON, A. Move the stick from left stop to right stop and check visually the full travel of ailerons. If the expected aileron movement does not occur, maintenance action is required. 7. ELAC 1 – OFF, A. Move the stick from left stop to right stop and then from forward stop to aft stop. Check visually the full travel of ailerons. If the expected aileron movement does not occur, maintenance action is required. 8. ELAC 1 – ON. 9. YELLOW ELEC PUMP– OFF. 10. BLUE ELEC PUMP OFF then AUTO (Resets Blue Pump OVRD).
ptext: POSITION INDICATION INOP ACTUATOR INDICATION INOP

name: ECAM Rudder Position Indication
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. A visual verification of rudder movement is made PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required), and 2. RUD TRIM indication on pedestal is operative and is verified at zero PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE 1. Verify the rudder trim indication on the pedestal is set to zero.
mproc: PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE: MAKE SURE THAT THE TRAVEL RANGES OF THE FLIGHT CONTROLS ARE CLEAR. MOVEMENT OF FLIGHT WARNING CONTROLS CAN CAUSE INJURY TO PERSONS AND/OR DAMAGE. 1. Energize the aircraft electrical circuits. 2. Establish communications – flight deck to ground. 3. Set YELLOW ELEC PUMP to ON. A. Perform a visual check of rudder surface movement as follows: FLIGHT DECK (Flight Crew if available) GROUND OBSERVATION ● Apply full left rudder. Rudder moves to full left. ● Apply full right rudder. Rudder moves to full right. ● Release rudder pedals. Rudder goes to the neutral position. 4. Set YELLOW ELEC PUMP to OFF.

name: Rudder Hydraulic System Pressure Indication Symbol on ECAM F/CTL Page
rem: Any or all may be inoperative.

name: Rudder Pedal Adjustment System
rem: (O) CAPT and/or F/O may be inoperative provided: 1. Associated rudder pedals can be adjusted to a position which is acceptable to the affected crewmember, and 2. Full and unrestricted movement of rudder pedals and brake pedal deflection is possible at both pilot stations.
oproc: During flight deck preparation: 1. At affected crewmember(s) position simultaneously press both rudder pedals to ensure they are secure in the desired position. Note: The pedal force used to satisfactorily accomplish the above test should approximate the force normally used for individual rudder pedal movement. 2. At both crewmember positions accomplish full rudder pedal and brake pedal movement to ensure full and unrestricted movement/deflection.

name: System No.1
rem: (O) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS), may be inoperative provided: 1. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, and 2. System No.2 is operative.

name: System No.2
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, and 2. System No.1 is operative.
oproc: 1. CAT III - Not Authorized.

name: Rudder Manual Trim Reset Function
rem: May be inoperative provided one rudder position indication is available.

name: Rudder Trim Position Indications
rem: (O) One indicator on ECAM or pedestal may be inoperative.

name: Rudder Trim Position Indications
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Rudder trim is verified to operate normally, 2. Rudder position is verified at zero PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, and 3. Rudder pedals are verified in a neutral position. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: 1. Use operative indication to check rudder trim position. 27-22-03B PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE 1. Rudder position is checked at zero on the F/CTL SD page.
mproc: 1. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE: MAKE SURE THAT THE TRAVEL RANGES OF THE FLIGHT CONTROLS ARE CLEAR. MOVEMENT OF WARNING FLIGHT CONTROLS CAN CAUSE INJURY TO PERSONS AND/OR DAMAGE. A. Energize the aircraft electrical circuits. B. Establish communications – flight deck to ground. C. Set YELLOW ELEC PUMP to ON. D. Verify rudder pedals are in neutral position. E. Verify rudder trim operates as follows: FLIGHT DECK (Flight Crew if available) GROUND OBSERVATION ● Turn RUD TRIM selector switch to the NOSE/L Rudder moves to the left. position. ● Turn RUD TRIM selector switch to the NOSE/R Rudder moves to the right. position. ● Push the RUD TRIM/RESET pushbutton. Rudder goes to the neutral position. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Rudder Travel Limiter Systems
rem: One may be inoperative. Note: “AUTO FLT RUD TRV LIM SYS” fault on ECAM indicates a failure of both rudder travel limiter systems and is a “NO DISPATCH” condition (see ECAM Messages table this manual)

name: ECAM Rudder Travel Limiter Position Indication
rem: May be inoperative.

name: ECAM Elevator Position Indications
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided a visual verification of affected elevator movement is made PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required). Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: ECAM Elevator Actuator Indications (HYD POWER)
placard: POSITION INDICATION(S) INOP ACTUATOR INDICATION ___ INOP
rem: May be inoperative.
oproc: PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, verify logbook entry has been made by maintenance for MEL/CDL REQUIRED FOLLOW UP ACTION as stated on the (yellow) MEL Placard placed in the logbook.
mproc: 1. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE: MAKE SURE THAT THE TRAVEL RANGES OF THE FLIGHT CONTROLS ARE CLEAR. MOVEMENT OF WARNING FLIGHT CONTROLS CAN CAUSE INJURY TO PERSONS AND/OR DAMAGE. A. Energize the aircraft electrical circuits. B. Establish communications – flight deck to ground. C. Set YELLOW ELEC PUMP to ON. D. Check that BLUE ELEC PUMP and PTU are set to AUTO. E. Set BLUE PUMP OVRD to ON. F. Verify elevator position indicator(s) operates normal as follows: FLIGHT DECK (Flight Crew if available) GROUND OBSERVATION ● Apply maximum side stick nose down position. Elevators move down. ● Apply maximum side stick nose up position. Elevators move up. ● Release side stick. Elevators go to the neutral position. G. Set YELLOW ELEC PUMP to OFF. H. Set BLUE ELEC PUMP to OFF, then AUTO.
ptext: POSITION INDICATION(S) INOP ACTUATOR INDICATION ___ INOP

name: Elevator Servo Control Position Transducers
rem: (M) One per servo control must operate normally.
mproc: Note: MEL relief for an ELEV SERVO FAULT warning message is only possible if the associated failure message contains ELEV POS XDCR: USE STBY XDCR. After swapping the servo control position transducer, the servo control must operate normally on the spare transducer (no ECAM warning and no amber box around the servo control on the ECAM F/CTL page). 1. Perform AMM Task 27-34-00-040-001.

name: ECAM Pitch Trim Position Indication
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided a check of pitch trim handwheel and stabilizer verifies synchronous movement PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required). Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: 1. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, verify logbook entry has been made by maintenance for MEL/CDL REQUIRED FOLLOW UP ACTION as stated on the (yellow) MEL Placard placed in the logbook. 2. Pitch trim – Set as required. Note: If requested to assist in the maintenance stab trim check, refer to DDG item below.
mproc: PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE: 1. Perform AMM Task 27-40-00-040-001.

name: Stabilizer Actuator Electrical Motors (Motor 3)
rem: Motor 3 only may be inoperative.

name: Stabilizer Actuator Electrical Motors (Motor 2)
rem: (O) (M) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS), motor 2 only may be inoperative provided: 1. ELAC 1 is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 27-93-01, and 2. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements.
oproc: Comply with MEL item 27-93-01 (O) procedure.
mproc: Defer ELAC 1 inoperative per MEL item 27-93-01.

name: Pitch Trim Hydraulic System Pressure Indication Symbol on ECAM F/CTL Page
placard: TRIM HYDRAULIC SYSTEM PRESSURE SYMBOL(S) INOP
rem: One or both may be inoperative.
ptext: TRIM HYDRAULIC SYSTEM PRESSURE SYMBOL(S) INOP

name: Flap Channels
rem: (O) (M) SFCS 2 flap channel may be inoperative provided: 1. Slats and Flaps operate normally on SFCC 1, 2. Operation of SFCC 1 WTBs are confirmed by tests PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required), 3. Electrical supply to SFCC 2 flap channel is inhibited, 4. ELAC, SEC, ADIRS, LGCIU, FAC, and RA systems operate normally, 5. Spoiler surfaces 2 and 4 operate normally, 6. RNAV operations do not require SFCS use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements. and 7. Minimum idle on ground is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 73-20-03A, 73-20-03B, or 73-20-03C as applicable. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Flap Channels
rem: (O) (M) SFCS 2 flap channel may be inoperative provided: 1. Slats and Flaps operate normally on SFCC 1, 2. Operation of SFCC 1 WTBs are confirmed by tests PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required), 3. Electrical supply to SFCC 2 flap channel is inhibited, 4. ELAC, SEC, ADIRS, LGCIU, FAC, and RA systems operate normally, 5. Spoiler surfaces 2 and 4 operate normally, 6. RNAV operations do not require SFCS use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements. 7. Minimum idle on ground is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 73-20-03D, and 8. OAT is below ISA+35°C. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard) PAGE

name: Slat Channel
rem: (O) (M) SFCS 2 slat channel may be inoperative provided: 1. Slats and Flaps operate normally on SFCC 1, 2. Operation of SFCC 1 WTBs are confirmed by tests PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required), 3. Electrical supply to SFCC 2 slat channel is inhibited, 4. ELAC, SEC, ADIRS, LGCIU, FAC, and RA systems operate normally, 5. Takeoff using FLAP 1 (CONF 1+F) is prohibited, 6. Departure weather is 1000 ft./3 mi. or greater, 7. Destination (and alternate if required) station weather is forecast to be 1000 ft./3 mi. or greater at the time of arrival, 8. If, upon arrival, the actual destination/alternate weather is below 1000 ft./3 mi. landing is authorized provided SFCC 1 is operational, and 9. RNAV operations do not require SFCS use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: 1. Disregard and clear the F/CTL FLAP SYS 2 FAULT caution displayed on ECAM EWD. 2. Comply with MEL item 73-20-03A, 73-20-03B, or 73-20-03C (O) procedure, as applicable. Note: Status messages ENG 2 APPR IDLE ONLY and FLAPS SLOW are displayed. 27-51-01B 1. Disregard and clear the F/CTL FLAP SYS 2 FAULT caution displayed on ECAM EWD. 2. Comply with MEL 73-20-03D (O) procedure. 3. OAT is below ISA+35°C. Note: Status messages ENG 2 APPR IDLE ONLY and FLAPS SLOW are displayed. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Flap Wing Tip Brake Solenoids
rem: (O) (M) Solenoids associated with SFCC 2 may be inoperative provided: 1. Operation of SFCC 1 WTBs are confirmed by tests PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required), and 2. RNAV operations do not require Flap Wing Tip Brake Soleniod use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: RNP AR Operations - Not Authorized
mproc: 1. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE: A. Confirm the operation of the SFCC 1 WTBs by performing AMM Task 27-51-00-040-004.

name: Flap Hydraulic Motors (Green Motor)
rem: Green motor may be inoperative provided blue slat motor operates normally.

name: Flap Hydraulic Motors (Yellow Motor)
placard: FLAP HYDRAULIC MOTOR INOP FLAP HYDRAULIC MOTOR INOP
rem: Yellow motor may be inoperative provided blue slat motor operates normally.
ptext: FLAP HYDRAULIC MOTOR INOP FLAP HYDRAULIC MOTOR INOP

name: Flap PCU Valve Blocks
rem: (O) (M) SFCS 2 Flap PCU Valve Block may be inoperative provided: 1. Slats and Flaps operate normally on SFCC 1, 2. Operation of SFCC 1 WTBs are confirmed by tests PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required), 3. Electrical supply to SFCC 2 flap channel is inhibited, 4. ELAC, SEC, ADIRS, LGCIU, FAC, and RA systems operate normally, 5. Spoiler surfaces 2 and 4 operate normally, 6. Minimum idle on ground is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 73-20-03A, 73-20-03B, 73-20-03C or 73-20-03D, as applicable, and 7. RNAV operations do not require Flap BCU Valve Blocks use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: 1. Disregard and clear the F/CTL FLAP SYS 2 FAULT caution displayed on ECAM EWD. 2. Comply with MEL item 73–20–03A, 73-20-03B, 73-20-03C or 73-20-03D (O) procedure, as applicable. Note: Status messages ENG 2 APPR IDLE ONLY and FLAPS SLOW are displayed.
mproc: 1. Deactivate the SFCC No.2 Flap Channel by performing AMM Task 27-51-00-040-001 2. Defer Minimum Idle on Ground inoperative per MEL item 73-20-03A, 73-20-03B, 73-20-03C or 73-20-03D as applicable 3. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE: A. Confirm operation of SFCC 1 WTB/POB by performing AMM Task 27-51-00-710-001.

name: Spoiler Surfaces (No.1 or 3)
rem: (O) (M) One pair of symmetrical surfaces 1 or 3 may be inoperative in the retracted position provided: 1. SECs associated with operative spoilers operate normally, and 2. Applicable performance corrections for 2 Pair Spoilers Inoperative are applied for takeoff and landing.

name: Spoiler Surfaces (No.5)
rem: (O) (M) One pair of symmetrical surfaces 5 may be inoperative in the retracted position provided SECs associated with operative spoilers operate normally.

name: Spoiler Surfaces (No.2 or 4)
rem: (O) (M) One pair of symmetrical surfaces 2 or 4 may be inoperative in the retracted position provided: 1. SECs associated with operative spoilers operate normally, 2. SFCC 2 flap channel operates normally, and 3. Applicable performance corrections for 2 Pair Spoilers Inoperative are applied for takeoff and landing.

name: Spoiler Surfaces (No.1 and 2)
rem: (O) (M) Two pair of symmetrical surfaces 1 and 2 may be inoperative in the retracted position provided: 1. SECs associated with operative spoilers operate normally, 2. SFCC 2 flap channel operates normally, 3. Spoiler surfaces 3 and 4 operate normally, 4. Applicable performance corrections for 2 Pair Spoilers Inoperative are applied for takeoff and landing, 5. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, and 6. RNAV operations do not require Spoiler Surfaces use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements. PAGE

name: Spoiler Surfaces (No.3 and 4)
rem: (O) (M) Two pair of symmetrical surfaces 3 and 4 may be inoperative in the retracted position provided: 1. SECs associated with operative spoilers operate normally, 2. TR 1 and TR 2 operate normally, 3. DC TIE contactor 1 is verified closed PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, 4. SFCC 2 flap channel operates normally, 5. Do not use speedbrakes, 6. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, and 7. Applicable performance corrections for 2 Pair Spoilers Inoperative are applied for takeoff and landing. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: 1. After engines start, check on ECAM HYD page for the absence of amber data. 2. Disregard the F/CTL SPOILER FAULT message. 3. Select the MEL item "GROUND SPOILERS 1 PAIR INOPERATIVE" in the Airbus EFB Performance Applications. 27-64-01A SPOILER SURFACES (NO. 1 OR 3) EFFECTIVITY All FLIGHT PLANNING None REQUIREMENTS NOTES AWABS Takeoff data is provided for DRY or WET runway conditions only. Takeoffs using ICY, QCTR or HCTR data are not authorized. AWABS Performance corrections have been applied. TAKEOFF Manual WDR not permitted with MEL 27-64-01A. PERFORMANCE WORKSHEET CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Spoilers Hydraulic System Pressure Indication Symbol on F/CTL Page
rem: Any or all may be inoperative.

name: Slat Wing Tip Brake Solenoids
rem: (O) (M) Solenoids associated with SFCC 2 may be inoperative provided: 1. SFCC 1 WTBs is checked operative normally PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required), 2. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, and 3. RNAV operations do not require Slat Wing Tip Brake Soleniod use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: 1. RNP-AR operations - Not Authorized.
mproc: 1. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE: A. Perform AMM Task 27-81-00-040-001

name: Slats Hydraulic Motors
rem: If blue slat hydraulic motor is inoperative, the green flap hydraulic motor must be operative.

name: Slat PCU Valve Blocks
rem: (O) (M) SFCS 2 Slat PCU Valve Block may be inoperative provided: 1. Slats, Flaps, and associated monitoring and protection systems operate normally on SFCC 1, 2. Operation of SFCC 1 WTB's are confirmed by tests PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required), 3. Electrical supply to SFCC 2 slat channel is inhibited, 4. ELAC, SEC, ADIRS, LGCIU, FAC, and RA systems operate normally, 5. Takeoff using FLAP 1 (CONF 1+F) is prohibited, 6. Departure weather is 1000 ft./3 mi. or greater, 7. Destination (and alternate if required) station weather is forecast to be 1000 ft./3 mi. or greater at the time of arrival, 8. If, upon arrival, the actual destination/alternate weather is below 1000 ft./3mi., landing is authorized provided SFCC 1 is operational, and 9. RNAV operations do not require Slat PCU Valve Blocks use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: 1. Verify that: A. Departure weather is 1000 ft./3 mi. or greater. B. Destination (and alternate if required) station weather is forecast to be 1000 ft./3 mi. or greater at the time of arrival. 2. If, upon arrival, the actual destination/alternate weather is below 1000 ft./3 mi., landing is authorized provided SFCC 1 is operational. 3. Disregard and clear the F/CTL SLAT SYS 2 FAULT caution displayed on ECAM EWD. 4. Takeoff using FLAP 1 (CONF 1+F) configuration is prohibited. Note: Status message SLATS SLOW is displayed. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: All Speed Brakes
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided applicable performance corrections for All Spoilers Inoperative are applied for takeoff and landing.

name: Speed Brake No. 2
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Speed brake No. 2 is considered inoperative in the retracted position, 2. Spoiler surfaces No.2 are considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 27-64-01C, and 3. Speed brakes No. 3 and 4 are operative.

name: Speed Brake No. 3 and 4
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Speed brake Nos. 3 and 4 are considered inoperative in the retracted position, 2. Spoiler surfaces No.3 and 4 are considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 27-64-01E, 3. Speed brake No. 2 is operative, and 4. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements.
oproc: 1. AUTO/BRK function is not available. Do not select or use the AUTO/BRK function. Note: When T.O MEMO is displayed on ECAM EWD, disregard the line: AUTO BRK........MAX (blue). Note: One or both thrust reversers may not deploy at landing. 2. Select the MEL item 'GROUND SPOILERS ALL INOPERATIVE' in the Airbus EFB Performance Applications. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: All Spoilers
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. A check of the thrust reverser system is performed to ensure that both thrust reversers operate normally PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required), 2. Autobrake function is not used, 3. Applicable performance corrections for All Spoilers Inoperative are applied for takeoff and landing, 4. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, and 5. Repairs are made within three landings. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Spoiler 5
rem: The pair of spoilers 5 may be inoperative.

name: Spoilers 1 and 2
rem: (O) The pair of spoilers 1 and 2 may be inoperative provided: 1. Spoilers 3 and 4 are operative, and 2. Applicable performance corrections for 2 Pair Spoilers Inoperative are applied for takeoff and landing.

name: Spoilers 3 and 4
rem: (O) The pair of spoilers 3 and 4 may be inoperative provided: 1. Spoilers 1 and 2 are operative, and 2. Applicable performance corrections for 2 Pair Spoilers Inoperative are applied for takeoff and landing.
oproc: 1. AUTO/BRK function is not available. Do not select or use the AUTO/BRK function. A. When T.O MEMO is displayed on ECAM EWD, disregard the line: AUTO BRK ................. MAX (blue) Note: One or both thrust reversers may not deploy at landing. 2. Select the MEL item "GROUND SPOILERS ALL INOPERATIVE" in the Airbus EFB Performance Applications. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Spoiler/Speedbrake Indications on ECAM F/CTL and WHEEL Page (Spoilers Nos. 1 or 3; 2 or 4; or 5 Inoperative)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative for an associated inoperative spoiler deferred per MEL 27-64-01A, 27-64-01B or 27-64-01C as appropriate.

name: Spoiler/Speedbrake Indications on ECAM F/CTL and WHEEL Page (Spoilers Nos.1 and 2 Inoperative)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative for associated inoperative spoiler deferred per MEL 27-64-01D. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements.

name: Spoiler/Speedbrake Indications on ECAM F/CTL and WHEEL Page (Spoilers Nos.3 and 4 Inoperative)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative for associated inoperative spoiler deferred per MEL 27-64-01E. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements.

name: Spoiler/Speedbrake Indications on ECAM F/CTL and WHEEL Page (Spoilers operative)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided a visual check of affected surface movement is made PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required). Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: Comply with MEL Item 27-64-01A, 27-64-01B, or 27-64-01C (O) procedure, as appropriate. 27-92-05B Comply with MEL Item 27-64-01D (O) procedure. 27-92-05C Comply with MEL Item 27-64-01E (O) procedure. 27-92-05D PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, verify logbook entry has been made by maintenance for MEL/CDL REQUIRED FOLLOW UP ACTION as stated on the (yellow) MEL Placard placed in the logbook. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Elevator Aileron Computers
rem: (O) (M) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS), ELAC 1 or any ELAC 1 function may be inoperative provided: 1. Both accelerometers associated with ELAC 2 operate normally, 2. All sidestick transducers associated with ELAC 2 and the three SECs operate normally, 3. ELAC 2, SECs, ADIRs, SFCCs, LGCIUs, FACs, and RAs operate normally, 4. TR 1 and TR 2 operate normally, 5. DC TIE contactor 1 is verified closed PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, 6. All roll spoilers (spoilers 2 thru 5 are roll spoilers) operate normally, 7. Elevators and roll spoilers control through the SECs is verified operative PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required), 8. Above FL200, the use of speed brakes lever is limited to its half position, 9. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, and 10. RNAV operations do not require ELAC use refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements. Note: When the ELAC 1 FAULT alert is displayed (ELAC 1 is not electrically supplied), F/O Take-Over pb cannot disengage AP1. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: 1. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, verify logbook entry has been made by maintenance for MEL/CDL REQUIRED FOLLOW UP ACTION as stated on the (yellow) MEL Placard placed in the logbook. 2. IN FLIGHT Above FL200, limit the use of the speedbrakes lever to its half position. 3. APPROACH ● If the AP 1 is used for the approach: The captain must be the PF. Note: Due to the fact that the ELAC 1 is not electrically supplied, the F/O take-over pb cannot disengage the AP 1 CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: FAULT Lights
rem: One may be inoperative provided: 1. Both Flight Warning Computers (FWCs) operate normally, and 2. ELAC indications operate normally.

rem: One or both may be inoperative.
ptext: P.B. SWITCH FAULT LIGHT INOP P.B. SWITCH OFF LIGHTS INOP

name: ECAM ELAC Indications
rem: One may be inoperative for an inoperative ELAC 1.

name: ECAM ELAC Indications
placard: ECAM ELAC INDICATION INOP ECAM ELAC INDICATION INOP

ptext: ECAM ELAC INDICATION INOP ECAM ELAC INDICATION INOP

name: Spoiler Elevator Computers
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. SEC 3 is deactivated, 2. SEC 1 and SEC 2 are operative, 3. SFCC No. 2 flap channel is operative, 4. All aileron servo channels are operative, 5. All roll spoilers associated with operative SECs (spoilers 3 thru 5) are operative, 6. Spoiler Surfaces (No.1 and 2) are considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 27-64-01D, 7. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, and 8. RNAV operations do not require SEC 3 use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements.
oproc: 1. SEC 3 – OFF. 2. Comply with MEL Item 27-64-01D (O) procedure.
mproc: 1. Defer Spoilers 1 and 2 inoperative per MEL Item 27-64-01D 2. Deactivate the SEC 3 by: A. Pull and secure FLIGHT CONTROLS/SEC 3/SPLY circuit breaker located on the 121VU panel at Q19.

name: FAULT Lights
rem: One may be inoperative provided associated SEC caution operates normally.

name: FAULT Lights
rem: One or more may be inoperative provided: 1. Flight warning computers (FWC’s) operate normally, and 2. ECAM SEC indications operate normally.

name: OFF Lights
placard: ___ SEC FAULT LIGHT INOP ___ SEC FAULT LIGHT INOP SEC ___ OFF LIGHT INOP

ptext: ___ SEC FAULT LIGHT INOP ___ SEC FAULT LIGHT INOP SEC ___ OFF LIGHT INOP

name: SEC Indication on ECAM F/CTL Page
rem: One or more may be inoperative provided SEC pb switch fault light system operates normally.

name: Flight Control Data Concentrators (FCDC)
rem: (M) FCDC 2 ONLY may be inoperative.
mproc: On C/B panel 121VU: pull and secure circuit breaker FLIGHT CONTROLS/FCDC2/SPLY location Q20.

name: Fault(s) Indicated by FUEL
rem: Note: Dispatch with this MAINT STS message displayed on ECAM is permitted without CFDS interrogation.

name: Between Inner and Outer Tanks
rem: (O) One or both may be damaged or missing provided the inner tank fuel temperature is monitored.

name: Between Inner and Outer Tank
rem: (O) (M) One or both may be damaged or missing provided: 1. Associated transfer valves are latched in open position, and 2. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, associated transfer valve is verified in the open position. Note: After transfer valves have been electrically latched open; any refueling, repowering or opening of refuel door will cause the transfer valve to unlatch and close requiring transfer valve to be electrically latched open again. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: In Vent Surge Tank
rem: (O) (M) One or both may be damaged or missing.

name: In the Center Tank for ACT Vent Line (A321NEO with ACT)
rem: One or both may be damaged or missing provided there is no fuel in any ACT(s) and in the center tank.
oproc: 1. This dispatch condition results in an unusual distribution of fuel quantity: Outer tank of affected wing will be partially empty with the transfer valves normally closed. 2. Fuel management should be performed with care. 3. After a long taxi or if outside air temperature is high, FUEL OUTER TK HI TEMP caution may be displayed on ECAM EWD. If this occurs, disregard FUEL OUTER TK HI TEMP caution and apply the following procedure: A. The Inner Tank Fuel temperature during taxi and in flight is to be monitored to ensure that it decreases and remains within temperature limitations. B. In the event of the Inner Tank Fuel temperature exceeding temperature limitations, i. Before takeoff, delay takeoff and shutdown engine of affected side. ii. In flight, increase engine fuel flow of affected side and if necessary use APU GEN and switch off IDG of affected side if opposite GEN is available. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: LH Wing (For A319/A320 Aircraft)
rem: (O) One or both may be inoperative open.

name: LH Wing (For A319/A320 Aircraft)
rem: (O) One or both may be inoperative closed provided: 1. LH outer tank is full of fuel, and 2. LH outer tank fuel is considered unusable and included in ZFW and CG calculations.

name: LH Wing (For A319/A320 Aircraft)
rem: (O) (M) One may be inoperative closed provided: 1. Operative LH wing outer to inner tank transfer valve is latched in open position, and 2. Operative LH wing outer to inner tank transfer valve is checked to be in open position PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE. Note: After transfer valves have been electrically latched open; any refueling, repowering or opening of refuel door will cause the transfer valve to unlatch and close. This will require Transfer Valves to be electrically latched open again. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: RH Wing (For A319/A320 Aircraft)
rem: (O) One or both may be inoperative open.

name: RH Wing (For A319/A320 Aircraft)
rem: (O) One or both may be inoperative closed provided: 1. RH outer tank is full of fuel, and 2. RH outer tank fuel is considered unusable and included in ZFW and CG calculations.

name: RH Wing (For A319/A320 Aircraft)
rem: (O) (M) One may be inoperative closed provided: 1. Operative RH wing outer to inner tank transfer valve is latched in open position, and 2. Operative RH wing outer to inner tank transfer valve is checked to be in open position PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE. Note: After transfer valves have been electrically latched open; any refueling, repowering or opening of refuel door will cause the transfer valve to unlatch and close. This will require Transfer Valves to be electrically latched open again. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard) PAGE

name: Automatic Fuel Feed System (A319/A320 Aircraft)
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided alternate procedures are used.
oproc: ON GROUND 1. If center tank is empty for flight: A. FUEL MODE SEL pb-sw – MAN B. CTR TK PUMP 1 and 2 pb-sw – OFF 2. If center tank is not empty for the flight: A. Before Engine Start i. FUEL MODE – MAN before engines start. WITH THE MODE SEL IN THE MAN POSITION, THE CENTER TANK PUMPS WILL RUN. IN CAUTION MANUAL MODE THE CTR TK PUMP PB-SW MUST BE SELECTED OFF, WHEN THE CENTER TANK IS EMPTY. B. 3 minutes after engines start: i. OPERATIVE CTR TK PUMPS (1 and/or 2) –OFF. IN FLIGHT 3. If the center tank is not empty for the flight: A. After slats retraction and when the fuel in one wing is less than 11,000 lbs:: i. OPERATIVE CTR TK PUMPS (1 and/or 2) – ON. B. If the FUEL CTR TK PUMPS LO PR alert is displayed on the EWD, or if the center tank is not empty at slat extension: i. OPERATIVE CTR TK PUMPS (1 and/or 2) –OFF.

name: Wing Tank Pumps
rem: (O) (M) One pump may be inoperative provided JP– 4/Jet B is not used. Note: If JP–4/Jet B is used, refer to MEL item 28–21–01B. Note: This item is AD related.

name: Wing Tank Pumps (JP-4/Jet B fuel only)
rem: (O) (M) One pump 2 may be inoperative when JP4/ Jet B is used provided: 1. Fuel return valve is verified to operate normally PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required), and 2. Takeoff ECAM fuel temperature is less than +30°C. Note: This item is AD related. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: Ensure inoperative fuel pump is OFF. 28-21-01B 1. Ensure inoperative fuel pump is OFF. 2. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, verify logbook entry has been made by maintenance for MEL/CDL REQUIRED FOLLOW UP ACTION as stated on the (yellow) MEL Placard placed in the logbook.
mproc: IF THE FOLLOWING CIRCUIT BREAKERS ARE TRIPPED, THE ASSOCIATED WING FUEL PUMP WIRING CAUTION MUST BE CHECKED PRIOR TO THE RESET OF THAT CIRCUIT BREAKER (PER AD 2000–24–08, EM 2821– 01068). 1. Pull and secure the circuit breaker for the affected pump as follows: A. Left Wing Tanks (21QA): i. On C/B panel 121VU: pull and secure circuit breaker FUEL/L WING TK/PUMP1 SPLY location N23. ii. On C/B panel 121VU: pull and secure circuit breaker FUEL/L WING TK/PUMP2 SPLY location N26. B. Right Wing Tanks (22QA): i. On C/B panel 121VU: pull and secure circuit breaker FUEL/R WING TK/PUMP1 SPLY location Q23. ii. On C/B panel 121VU: pull and secure circuit breaker FUEL/R WING TK/PUMP2 SPLY location Q26. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Center Tank Systems Pump
rem: (O) (M) One may be inoperative provided (when center tank fuel is required) a suitable alternate airport exists within range of wing tanks fuel loading. Note: This item is AD related.

name: Center Tank Systems Pump
rem: (O) (M) One or both may be inoperative provided fuel in center tank is considered unusable and is included in ZFW and C.G. calculations. Note: This item is AD related.

name: Center Tank Systems Transfer Valves (A321CEO and A321NEO)
rem: (O) One may be inoperative in closed position provided (when center tank fuel is required) a suitable alternate airport exists within range of wing tanks fuel loading.

name: Center Tank Systems Transfer Valves
rem: (O) One or both may be inoperative in closed position provided fuel in center tank is considered unusable and is included in ZFW and C.G. calculations.

name: Center Tank Systems Transfer Valves
rem: (O) One or both may be inoperative in closed position provided: 1. Fuel in center tank is considered unusable and is included in ZFW and C.G. calculations, and 2. Fuel in ACTs is considered unusable and is included in ZFW and C.G. calculations.
oproc: When center tank has available fuel: 1. On Ground: A. X-FEED pb-sw – Check OFF B. Affected CTR TK PUMP pb-sw – OFF C. Operative CTR TK PUMP pb-sw – OFF 2. In Flight: A. If the fuel quantity in the center tank is 440 lbs or less: i. Operative CTR TK PUMP pb-sw – Maintain OFF B. If the fuel quantity in the center tank is greater than 440 lbs: CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Wing Tank Pump Sequence Valves (A319/A320 Aircraft)
rem: (O) One may be inoperative provided the associated pump is switched off when center tank is feeding.
oproc: Turn corresponding pump off when center tank is feeding.

rem: May be inoperative provided X FEED indication on ECAM FUEL page operates normally.

name: OPEN Light
placard: PB SWITCH ON LIGHT INOP PB SWITCH OPEN LIGHT INOP
rem: May be inoperative provided X FEED indication on ECAM FUEL page operates normally.
