name: Engine LP Fuel Valve Electrical Motor
rem: (M) One motor on each engine LP Fuel Valve may be inoperative provided: 1. Affected valve motor is deactivated, and 2. Remaining valve motor is checked operative.
mproc: Perform AMM Task 28-24-00-040-002-A.

name: Fuel Quantity Preselector System
rem: May be inoperative.

name: Fuel Quantity Indicator (Refueling Panel)
rem: (O) (M) One or more indications may be inoperative provided PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE alternate means of refueling are used (see Fueling Procedures Manual) Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: 1. Ensure receipt of a completed paper FSR. Note: Refer to Volume 1 A320 5.12.6 Inoperative Fuel Quantity Indication and FOM - Flight Ops Manual 14.4.1.7 Inoperative Gauge Refueling.
mproc: 1. Place a placard on refuel/defuel panel as follows: A. Obtain a Placard. B. Print on placard DISCREPANCY in bold letters “____ FUEL QUANTITY PANEL IND INOP USE ALTERNATE PROCEDURES.” C. Trim placard so only the DISCREPANCY portion remains. 2. Affix trimmed placard adjacent to the tank indicators at the refueling panel 3. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, service tank to required level per Fueling Procedures Manual. Note: Flight deck ECAM FUEL QTY indications (if operative) may be used to refuel affected tanks per Fueling Procedures Manual.

name: High Level Fuel Detection System (For A319 and A320 aircraft)
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided an acceptable means of monitoring fuel loading is used PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: High Level Fuel Detection System - Wing Tank (For A321CEO and A321NEO aircraft)
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided an acceptable means of monitoring fuel loading is used PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: High Level Fuel Detection System - Center Tank (For A321CEO and A321NEO aircraft)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. An acceptable means of monitoring fuel loading is used PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, 2. Manual transfer from ACT(s) to center tank is verified to operate normally, and 3. ACT(s) and center fuel indications on ECAM FUEL page are operative. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: High Level Fuel Detection System - Center Tank (For A321CEO and A321NEO aircraft)
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. An acceptable means of monitoring fuel loading is used PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, and 2. There is no fuel in ACT. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: High Level Fuel Detection System - Center Tank (For A321CEO and A321NEO aircraft)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. An acceptable means of monitoring fuel loading is used PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, 2. Fuel in any ACT is considered unusable and included in ZFW and CG calculations, and 3. ACT transfer valve is secured closed. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: High Level Fuel Detection System - Additional Center Tank (For A321CEO and A321NEO aircraft)
placard: DETECTION SYS INOP USE ALTERNATE OF MONITORING FUELING TANK HI LVL FUEL DETECTION INOP TANK HI LVL FUEL DETECTION INOP TANK HI LVL FUEL DETECTION INOP TANK HI LVL FUEL DETECTION INOP HI LVL FUEL DETECTION INOP
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided an acceptable means of monitoring fuel loading is used PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
ptext: DETECTION SYS INOP USE ALTERNATE OF MONITORING FUELING TANK HI LVL FUEL DETECTION INOP TANK HI LVL FUEL DETECTION INOP TANK HI LVL FUEL DETECTION INOP TANK HI LVL FUEL DETECTION INOP HI LVL FUEL DETECTION INOP

name: Refuel Valves
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided affected valve(s) is operated manually PRIOR TO EACH REFUELING (Log Book Entry Required). Note: LOG BOOK ENTRY IS REQUIRED ONLY IF VALVE(S) IS ACCESSED (SEE (M) PROCEDURE). Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
mproc: 1. Place a second placard in a conspicuous place at the fueling panel. 2. PRIOR TO EACH REFUELING A. Manually operate affected refuel valve(s) using appropriate procedures in AMM Task 28-25-00-040-005-A. Log book entry required for accessing the fill valves and close up per AMM Task 28-25-00-040-005-A. B. Service tank with inoperative refueling valve(s) to required level per Fueling Procedures Manual. The following identifies the location of the right wing tank refuel valve panel 622JB and left wing tank refuel valve panel 522JB: Figure 28-2: Wing Refuel Valve Access CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Transfer Defuel Valve
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided valve is secured in the closed position.
mproc: WARNING PUT THE SAFETY DEVICES AND DISPLAY WARNING NOTICES BEFORE YOU START A TASK ON OR NEAR: ● THE FLIGHT CONTROLS ● THE FLIGHT CONTROL SURFACES ● THE LANDING GEAR AND RELATED DOORS ● COMPONENTS THAT MOVE WARNING MAKE SURE THAT THE GROUND SAFETY–LOCKS ARE IN POSITION ON THE LANDING GEAR 1. TRANSFER DEFUEL VALVE – DEACTIVATE CLOSED A. On C/B panel 121 VU: pull and secure circuit breaker FUEL/RFL/DFL/XFR/DEFUEL/VALVE location L23, or L22 for A321NEO. B. Open right MLG door. C. Open Fueling Control access panel 622KB on the right hand wing leading edge. D. Close the Transfer Defuel Valve: i. On Fueling Control panel (800VU) make sure that MODE SELECT SWITCH is in the OFF and guarded position. ii. If the actuator (11QU) shows the transfer defuel valve (11QM) OPEN (See feel indicator is in line with the step). a. Remove the actuator from the drive shaft of the transfer defuel valve. b. Manually turn the drive shaft to the valve–closed position (indicator groove vertical). c. Make sure that the actuator shaft is in the correct position to align with the valve drive shaft. d. If necessary, on panel 121VU: remove secure and close circuit breaker FUEL/RFL/DFL/XFR/DEFUEL/ VALVE location L23, and operate the actuator to get the correct position. Pull and re-secure circuit breaker. e. Disconnect the electrical connector from the actuator. f. Cap and stow. g. Install the actuator, but leave electrically disconnected. OR iii. If the position indicator on the actuator shows closed (see–feel indicator is crossline 90 degrees to the step) a. Disconnect the electrical connector from the actuator. b. Cap and stow. OR iv. If the actuator 11U unserviceable: a. Disconnect the electrical connector from the actuator. b. Cap and stow electrical connectors. c. Remove the actuator. d. Make sure that the transfer defuel valve is in the closed position (indicator vertical e. Install the VALVE LOCKING TOOL (T720456) onto the face plate of the drive shaft to lock the drive shaft so that it will not turn. E. Close the right MLG door. F. Close access panel opened in step 1. A. (3). CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Exterior Control Panel
rem: (M) One or more indications may be inoperative provided PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE alternate means of refueling are used (see Fueling Procedures Manual) Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
mproc: 1. Place a placard on refuel/defuel panel as follows: A. Obtain a Placard. B. Print on placard DISCREPANCY in bold letters “REFUEL/DEFUEL CONTROL PANEL INOP USE ALTERNATE PROCEDURES.” C. Trim placard so only the DISCREPANCY portion remains. D. Affix trimmed placard adjacent to the tank indicators at the refueling panel. 2. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, service tank to required level per Fueling Procedures Manual. Note: Flight deck ECAM FUEL QTY indications (if operative) may be used to refuel affected tanks per Fueling Procedures Manual.

name: Auto Transfer System Additional Center Tank (A321NEO with ACT)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided no fuel is verified in the ACT PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard) OR (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Manual transfer from ACT to the center tank is checked PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required), and 2. Verify ACT and center tank fuel quantity indications on ECAM FUEL page are operative PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE Note: Coordination between flight crew or maintenance personnel and fueling personnel is required to ensure test is performed prior to center tank being filled. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE: 1. If the manual transfer from ACT to the center tank check is required, verify the logbook entry has been made for MEL/CDL REQUIRED FOLLOW UP ACTION as stated on the (yellow) MEL Placard in the logbook. 2. FSR or eFSR has been received. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Transfer Valve Additional Center Tank (A321NEO with ACT)
rem: May be inoperative in closed position provided there is no fuel in ACT.

name: Transfer Valve Additional Center Tank (A321NEO with ACT)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative in open position provided an alternate procedure is used for refueling ACT.
oproc: Ensure an FSR or eFSR is received.
mproc: PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, service tank to required level per Fueling Procedures Manual.

name: Transfer Pump Additional Center Tank
rem: May be inoperative.

name: Air Shutoff Valve Additional Center Tank (A321NEO with ACT)
rem: May be inoperative closed provided there is no fuel in ACT.

name: Air Shutoff Valve Additional Center Tank (A321NEO with ACT)
rem: (O) May be inoperative closed provided: 1. Manual transfer from ACT is to center tank is verified to operate normally PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, and 2. ACT and center tank fuel quantity indications on ECAM FUEL page are operative. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE 1. Manually transfer a small amount of fuel from the ACT to the center tank (this can be seen by observing the fuel quantity on the ECAM FUEL page). Note: Before refueling, coordinate with fueling personnel if there is no fuel in ACT. A. Place ACT XFR selector to AFT 1 B. Place ACT XFR MODE SEL to MAN C. Monitor fuel transferring from ACT D. Place ACT XFR selector AS REQUIRED E. Place ACT XFR MODE SEL to AUTO

name: Inward Pressure Relief Valve Additional Center Tank (A321NEO with ACT)
rem: May be inoperative open provided there is no fuel in ACT.

name: Vent Valve Additional Center Tank (A321NEO with ACT)
rem: May be inoperative provided there is no fuel in ACT.

name: Vent Valve Additional Center Tank (A321NEO with ACT)
rem: (M) May be inoperative in CLOSED position provided an alternate procedure is used for refueling ACT.
mproc: Service ACT to required level per Fueling Procedures Manual.

name: Refuel Valve Additional Center Tank (A321NEO with ACT)
rem: (M) May be inoperative in the CLOSED position. Note: (M) PROCEDURE ONLY NEEDS TO BE ACCOMPLISHED AT EACH ACT REFUELING.

name: Refuel Valve Additional Center Tank (A321NEO with ACT)
placard: REFUEL VALVE INOP CLOSED REFUEL VALVE INOP OPEN
rem: May be inoperative in the OPEN position.
mproc: This procedure is only accomplished if ACT is being refueled: 1. Open ACT refuel valve using manual plunger override per AMM Task 28-25-00-040-005-A. 2. Close ACT refuel valve after ACT has been fueled.
ptext: REFUEL VALVE INOP CLOSED REFUEL VALVE INOP OPEN

name: Inlet Valve Additional Center Tank (A321NEO with ACT)
rem: May be inoperative in CLOSED position provided there is no fuel in ACT.

name: ACT XFR MODE SEL – FAULT Light (A321NEO with ACT)
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided the fuel quantity indications of the ACT are operative on the ECAM FUEL page.

name: ACT XFR MODE SEL – MAN Light (A321NEO with ACT)
rem: May be inoperative.

name: ALL ACT pb-sw – OVFL Light (A321NEO with ACT)
rem: May be inoperative provided the fuel quantity indication of the center tank is operative on the ECAM FUEL page.

name: ACT XFR Selector (A321NEO with ACT)
placard: MODE SEL FAULT LIGHT INOP XFR MODE SEL MAN LIGHT INOP ACT PB-SW OVFL LIGHT INOP ACT XFR SEL INOP
rem: (O) May be inoperative.
oproc: Before Takeoff 1. Place ACT XFR selector to AFT 1 2. Place ACT XFR MODE SEL to MAN 3. Monitor fuel transferring from ACT Note: When ACT contains less than 330 lbs of fuel, continue the manual transfer of ACT for 5 minutes. After 5 minutes consider the ACT as transferred. After fuel in ACT is transferred 4. Place ACT XFR selector to OFF 5. Place ACT XFR MODE SEL to AUTO 28-28-11D Check that the auto transfer from ACT to center tank has started 40 minutes after takeoff. Note: If FUEL ACT XFR FAULT displays during flight, consider fuel remaining in ACT as unusable.
ptext: MODE SEL FAULT LIGHT INOP XFR MODE SEL MAN LIGHT INOP ACT PB-SW OVFL LIGHT INOP ACT XFR SEL INOP

name: Low Level Detection Systems - Wing
rem: One may be inoperative provided all flight deck fuel quantity indicators are operative.

name: Low Level Detection Systems – Additional Center Tank (A321NEO with ACT)
placard: LEVEL DETECTION SYSTEM INOP LEVEL DETECTION SYSTEM INOP
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided ACT auto transfer system is considered inoperative and deferred per 28-28-01.
mproc: Defer ACT Auto Transfer System per 28-28-01.

name: Blue System Electric Pump (Automatic Control)
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. Pump can be manually operated, and 2. Indications of blue hydraulic system are verified normal.
oproc: 1. On the ground, if blue electric pump runs continuously: A. At electrical power supply application: BLUE ELEC PUMP – OFF. B. After engines start: BLUE ELEC PUMP –AUTO. C. After engines shutdown: BLUE ELEC PUMP – OFF. OR 2. On the ground, if blue electric pump does not start automatically: A. After engines start: i. BLUE ELEC PUMP – AUTO. ii. BLUE PUMP OVRD – ON. iii. Check blue hydraulic pressure available on ECAM HYD page. iv. After engines shutdown: BLUE ELEC PUMP – OFF

name: Hydraulic Reservoir Quantity Indicator (Green Servicing Panel)
placard: INDICATOR INOP ON THE GREEN SERVICING PANEL
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided hydraulic fluid quantity is monitored during servicing of the hydraulic reservoir using the visual quantity gauge.
mproc: 1. When servicing the hydraulic reservoir with an inoperative quantity indicator at the Green Servicing Panel, accomplish the following: A. Energize the aircraft electrical circuits: (Ref. AMM TASK 24–41–00–861–002). B. Open access door 197CB. C. On C/B panel 121VU: verify that circuit breaker HYDRAULIC/HYD/QTY/IND location P35 is closed. D. On the service panel of the Green hydraulic system: i. Turn on the reservoir–filling manual selector–valve (1134CQ) to the applicable position for each hydraulic system. E. If the quantity indicator gives an incorrect indication and it is necessary to fill one or more of the reservoirs, fill the applicable reservoir(s) as follows: i. Gain access to the applicable hydraulic reservoir: a. For the Green hydraulic reservoir: ● Open the RH main landing gear door (Ref. AMM TASK 32–12–00–010–001). b. For the Blue hydraulic reservoir: ● Open access door 197FB c. For the Yellow hydraulic reservoir: ● Open access door 196BB ii. Have a person in position at the reservoir to be filled. This person will monitor the contents indicator of the reservoir during the filling procedure. Make sure this person lets the filler know when the reservoir is full. iii. Fill the reservoir (Ref. AMM TASK 12–12–29–611–001) or (Ref. AMM TASK 12–12–29–611–001). iv. Stop the supply of hydraulic fluid when the reservoir is full. F. De–energize the ground service network. G. Close–up i. For the Green hydraulic reservoir: a. Close the main door of the landing gear (Ref. AMM TASK 32–12–00–410–001). ii. For the Blue hydraulic reservoir: a. Close the access door 197FB. iii. For the Yellow hydraulic reservoir: a. Close the access door 196BB. H. Restore aircraft to required configuration CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE
ptext: INDICATOR INOP ON THE GREEN SERVICING PANEL

name: Hydraulic Reservoir Four Way Selector Valve on Ground Service Panel
placard: FOUR WAY SELECTOR INOP ON GROUND SERVICE PANEL
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided associated system is serviced using the HP ground connection.
mproc: Service the affected hydraulic reservoir as follows: Note: Each hydraulic reservoir system must be serviced separately. 1. Connect the ground cart to the HP ground connector of the affected system to be filled. 2. Slowly increase the pressure until a level increase can be observed directly at the reservoir gauge. 3. Keep this pressure until the correct reservoir level is reached. 4. Do a check of the manual selector valve (1134GQ) for leaks. If necessary repair the leaks. 5. Disconnect the ground cart. 6. Return aircraft to required service.
ptext: FOUR WAY SELECTOR INOP ON GROUND SERVICE PANEL

name: Automatic Activation Function
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative (PTU runs continuously) provided: 1. System pressure indication on ECAM operates normally, 2. Power transfer can be stopped when PTU p.b. switch is placed OFF, and 3. Operation of the PTU in both directions is verified before FIRST FLIGHT OF EACH DAY (Log Book Entry Required). Note: PTU must be operated during takeoff and landing to provide gear and flap retraction in the event of an engine failure during takeoff/go–around. It is advisable to make a PA announcement to the passengers indicating PTU noise is normal during these phases of flight. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: 1. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, verify logbook entry has been made by maintenance for MEL/CDL REQUIRED FOLLOW UP ACTION as stated on the (yellow) MEL Placard placed in the logbook. Note: The above logbook entry is only required before the first flight of each day. 2. Before Takeoff select PTU – AUTO (Lights out). 3. After gear and flaps are retracted, select PTU –OFF. 4. Before landing, select PTU – AUTO (Lights out). Note: For passenger comfort, PTU may be switched OFF during the taxi after landing provided both hydraulic engine pumps are operative and running. PTU must be selected to AUTO before leaving the aircraft.
mproc: 1. DAILY CHECK (Before first flight of Each Day): Note: The PTU pressurizes the system to a nominal value of 3000 PSI+/– 200 PSI, but if no systems are operated the pressure is not constant. The PTU operates in a cycle which keeps the pressure in the hydraulic system between 2100 and 3200 PSI. A. Verify PTU pb switch is in the AUTO (Lights out) position. B. Verify Power Transfer Unit operates in both directions. C. After both engines start: i. GREEN ENG 1 PUMP – OFF a. Check that green system pressure is correct (3000 PSI _ 200 PSI) and check the PTU arrow indication. ii. GREEN ENG 1 PUMP – ON iii. PTU – OFF CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Yellow System Electric Pump
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided associated pb sw is selected OFF. Note: The AFT and FWD cargo doors must be operated manually. Note: Two agents required to operate cargo doors.
oproc: 1. Yellow system electric pump pb sw – OFF. 2. Start ENG 2 first, then start ENG 1. Note: This start sequence ensures that the yellow system accumulator is pressurized for parking brake operation. 3. Single engine taxi Not Authorized. Note: Without engines running, if yellow system accumulator pressure is bled off, parking brakes will not be available.
mproc: 1. Deactivate the yellow system electric pump as follows: A. Place the Yellow system electric pump p.b. sw – OFF. B. On C/B panel 122VU: pull and secure circuit breaker DOORS/CARGO location T12. C. If pump continues to run, Pull and secure the following circuit breakers: D. On A/C 3209–3238 i. On C/B panel 123VU: pull and secure circuit breaker Y HYD/ELEC PUMP/NORM location AB03. E. On A/C 3239–3278, all A319 aircraft, and all A321 aircraft i. On C/B panel 123VU: pull and secure circuit breaker Y HYD/ELEC/ELEC PUMP/NORM location AB03. F. On All A/C i. On C/B panel 123VU: pull and secure circuit breaker Y HYD/ELEC/PUMP location AB06. Note: If the pump runs continuously on the ground, there is a potential of an overheat situation. Note: The resetting of the circuit breakers should be limited to the time required to open or close the cargo doors.

In this case, the ENG OIL LO PRESS warning is inoperative (refer to MEL 79-33-02A or 79-33-02B).

name: Ram Air Turbine (RAT) Fault

If any part of the RAT FAULT warning system is deactivated, the deactivated component must be reactivated and the (M) procedure accomplished PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required). Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE and after both engines are started: 1. PTU – OFF. 2. Yellow Elec Pump – ON. 3. Check that the blue and yellow hydraulic systems pressure is correct (3000 PSI +/– 200 PSI) on the ECAM HYD page. 4. PTU – AUTO. 5. Yellow Elec Pump – OFF. Note: PTU will indicate running on the ECAM HYD page. 29-30-04F PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE and after both engines are started: 1. PTU – OFF. 2. Check that green and yellow hydraulic systems pressure is correct (3000 PSI +/– 200 PSI) on the ECAM HYD page. 3. PTU – AUTO. Note: PTU will indicate running on the ECAM HYD page. name: Wing Anti-Ice Control Valves - RH Valve OPEN (For A319/A320/A321CEO) (For A321NEO Ships 5027, 5033, and 5036-Subsequent) (For A321NEO Ships
rem: (O) (M) RH valve may be inoperative provided: 1. Affected valve is secured in OPEN position, 2. ENG 1 is started first, 3. X BLEED selector is shut when starting ENG 1, 4. “CROSS BLEED START” procedure is used when starting the ENG 2, 5. Alternate procedures are used, and 6. Performance corrections are applied. Note: This item is AD related.

name: Wing Anti-Ice Control Valves
rem: (O) (M) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS) beyond 120 minutes, may be inoperative provided: 1. Affected valves are secured in CLOSED position, 2. Aircraft is not operated in known or forecast structural icing conditions, and 3. Ships 5001-5026, 5028-5032, and 5034-5035, if Pre-AA 3611-01130, are restricted from all Extended Operations (ETOPS). Note: This item is AD related. Theater Restrictions May Apply
oproc: 1. Check that the anti–ice arrow is displayed on the ECAM BLEED page (may be displayed amber or green). 2. Before engines start: Maintain X BLEED selector at SHUT before engines start when APU bleed is used. 3. Engines start: A. ENG 1 must be started first. B. Maintain X BLEED selector at SHUT during ENG 1 start. C. Use “CROSS BLEED ENG START” procedure to start ENG 2. 4. After engines start: A. WING ANTI ICE pb switch – OFF B. ENG 2 BLEED pb switch – OFF C. X BLEED selector –SHUT Note: PACK 2 is displayed in the INOP SYS column. Note: RH engine will be in Hi–Idle Mode. Note: Deicing must be accomplished with Engines Not Running. Note: When “APU” is annotated on the WDR, takeoff performance prohibits using bleed air from engines. Use procedures in Vol 1 Supplementary Procedures 5.2.1 or 5.2.2 as desired maintaining X BLEED selector at SHUT. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

ANTI ICE WING pb switch – OFF. 30-11-02B Do not operate airplane in known or forecast structural icing conditions.

Note: Selecting switch to ON is to have the NAI command consistent with system status and allow thrust adaptation. 30-21-03A ANTI-ICE ENG 1 (2) CTL FAULT CAUTIONS ON ECAM EWD EFFECTIVITY A321NEO FLIGHT PLANNING Reduce ENROUTE LIMIT WT by 2,000 lbs. rem: (O) (M) One or more may be inoperative provided: 1. Associated lavatory wash basin and galley water supplies are secured off, 2. Associated galley sink and lavatory washbasin drains are blocked to prevent their use, and 3. Associated galley sinks and washbasins are periodically monitored to ensure they remain blocked. Note: CIDS Caution may be illuminated on FAP.
oproc: 1. Captain to brief Lead Flight Attendant/Purser to periodically monitor associated galley sinks and associated washbasins to ensure they remain blocked. Note: Rinse water is still available in the associated lavatory for toilet flushing only.

name: Clock System (Indicator On Center Instrument Panel)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Time base from CFDIU is available on ECAM, and 2. Chrono indication is available on one Navigation Display (ND). Note: For aircraft equipped with P/N APE5100-1, during the entire month of February, intermittent erroneous indications (wrong, over-range or missing digits) of the elapsed time and chronometer functions may occur if the clock is in GPS mode. In that case, the flight crew must set the clock selector to internal (INT) mode in order to recover the elapsed time and chronometer functions. The clock is considered operative. When the clock is in INT mode, the flight crew must synchronize the clock with the GPS at least one time per day to comply with the time precision required for ATC data link communication. Refer to FCOM DCS-31-55-20 Operation in Internal Mode..
oproc: After any electrical power interruption (loss of UTC on ECAM lower display), initialize UTC and date through the MCDU as follows: 1. Select CFDS on F/O’s MCDU. 2. Select next page. 3. Enter GMT and Date. Note: If clock cannot be reset, contact MCC.
mproc: Note: GMT/DATE init only appears on the MCDU when there is a clock fault and a power cutoff longer than 200 minutes. In this case, pulling of circuit breaker(s) is not sufficient due to the clock having a built in battery. 1. To remove the clock: A. On C/B panel 49VU: pull and tag circuit breaker CLOCK/NORM/SPLY location F11. B. On C/B panel 121VU: pull and tag circuit breaker CLOCK/STBY/SPLY location N02. C. Remove the thermoformed cover from the center instrument panel. D. Loosen the two screws. Do not remove them. E. Disengage the clock from the housing. F. Disconnect the electrical connector. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Centralized Fault Display system (CFDS) (including CFDS Clock)
rem: May be inoperative provided: 1. A CFDS system is available when required for specified maintenance tasks, and 2. Panel Clock must operate normally. Note: CFDS time base may not be available on lower screen.

name: Flight Data Recorder (FDR)
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Cockpit Voice Recorder (CVR) operates normally, 2. Airplane is not dispatched from ATL, MSP or DTW unless: A. The FDR failure occurs after pushback but prior to takeoff, OR B. The FDR repair was attempted but was not successful, 3. In those cases where repair is attempted but not successful, the aircraft may be dispatched on a flight or series of flights until the next arrival at ATL, MSP or DTW where repair must be accomplished, 4. It is required that the MCC initiate a Corrective Action Required (CAR) for the initial repair location at the time of the deferrfal and issue a second CAR for the next designated airport if the initial repair attempt was not successful, and 5. Repairs are made within three flight days.

name: Flight Data Recorder (FDR) DFDR Recording Parameters Required by 14 CFR
rem: Up to three (3) parameters may be inoperative provided: 1. Cockpit Voice Recorder (CVR) operates normally, and 2. Repairs are made within 20 calendar days. Note: Refer to TOPP 40-20-40 for handling FDR.

name: Flight Data Recorder (FDR) DFDR Recording Parameters Not Required by 14 CFR
placard: DATA RECORDER INOP RECORDER REQUIRED PARAMETER(S) ___ NOT BEING RECORDED NOT REQUIRED PARAMETER(S) ___ NOT BEING RECORDED
rem: May be inoperative provided repairs are made prior to completion of the next scheduled (C/H/M) maintenance check. Note: Refer to TOPP 40-20-40 for handling FDR.
mproc: Note: A321NEO aircraft has Airbus MOD 165565 embodied which incorporate two CVDR's for the Cockpit Voice Recorder (CVR) and the Digital Flight Data Recorder (DFDR) functions. Delta is not authorized by the FAA to consider the two CVDR's as independent systems, currently both units are consided as one system. If the RECORDER FDR FAULT is displayed the CVR function is considered to be operative as long as the RECORDER CVR FAULT is not displayed.
ptext: DATA RECORDER INOP RECORDER REQUIRED PARAMETER(S) ___ NOT BEING RECORDED NOT REQUIRED PARAMETER(S) ___ NOT BEING RECORDED

name: Flight Data Interface Unit (FDIU) or Flight Data Interface Function of the Flight Data Interface and Management Unit (FDIMU)
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. Cockpit Voice Recorder (CVR) operates normally, 2. Airplane is not dispatched from ATL, MSP or DTW unless: A. The FDR failure occurs after pushback but prior to takeoff, OR B. The FDR repair was attempted but was not successful, 3. In those cases where repair is attempted but not successful, the aircraft may be dispatched on a flight or series of flights until the next arrival at ATL, MSP or DTW where repair must be accomplished prior to dispatch, and 4. Repairs are made within three flight days.
oproc: If FDIU failure occurs after pushback but prior to takeoff (including at ATL, MSP or DTW), contact MCC for deferral instructions.

rem: May be inoperative.

rem: QAR INOP

name: Digital AIDS Recorder System
rem: May be inoperative.

name: Data Management Recorder (DMU) or Data Management Function of the FDIMU
rem: (O) May be inoperative.
oproc: Manually record engine data onto Engine Performance Report Forms. Refer to: ● Operations Manual, Volume 1, Supplementary Procedures, Communications, Engine Condition Monitoring (ECM) Recording . Ensure that a minimum of ten (10) Engine Performance Report Forms (OM-851) are installed in the aircraft logbook. If not, contact MCC. (forms can be obtained from the TechOps Home Page) Note: If an Engine Performance Report Form is not available and MCC determines that there is no requirement for ENCON readings during that flight segment, the Engine Performance Report Form may be boarded at the destination. It is desired to have the ENCON data available once per flight day.

rem: May be inoperative.

name: Flight Warning Computer (FWC) - FWC 2 Only
rem: (O) (M) FWC 2 ONLY may be inoperative provided enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements.
oproc: CAT III – Not Authorized.
mproc: 1. Verify that the following circuit breakers are closed: A. On C/B panel 122VU: verify that circuit breaker LIGHTING/ANN LT SPLY/XFMR/BUS 2 location X05 is closed. B. On C/B panel 122VU: verify that circuit breaker LIGHTING/ANN LT SPLY/XFMR/STAT INV location X03 is closed. C. On C/B panel 122VU: verify that circuit breaker LIGHTING/TST/BOARD/SPLY location X06 is closed. D. On C/B panel 49VU: verify that circuit breaker LIGHTING/XFMR/115V.5V/ESS BUS location H03 is closed. 2. Pull and tag the following circuit breakers: A. On A/C 3209–3211 i. On C/B panel 49VU: pull circuit breaker FWS/FWC1/SPLY location E02. B. On A/C 3212–3278, 3101–3171, and A321 aircraft i. On C/B panel 49VU: pull circuit breaker FWS/FWC1/SPLY location F1. C. On All A/C i. On C/B panel 121VU: pull circuit breaker EIS/FWC2/SPLY location Q07. 3. Make certain that aircraft is in the following configuration: A. LANDING GEAR down locked, confirmed on panel 402VU by the fact that the lower light (triangle) of the 3 LANDING GEAR POSITION INDICATING LIGHT UNITS IS ON (Green color). B. ELAC 1 (2), SEC 1 (2) (3) engaged, confirmed on panel 23VU for ELAC 1 and SEC 1, and on panel 24VU for ELAC 2, SEC 2 and SEC 3 by the fact that FAULT and OFF legends of associated FLT CTL pushbutton switch are extinguished. 4. On panel 25VU, place the ANN LT switch in the TEST position: A. On panel 402VU, make sure that the upper light (UNLK legend) of the 3 LANDING GEAR POSITION INDICATING LIGHT UNITS comes on (Red color) and that the lower light (triangle) remains on (Green color). B. On panel 23VU and panel 24VU, make sure that for ELAC 1 (2), SEC 1 (2) (3), that FAULT and OFF legends of the associated FLT CTL pushbutton switch come on. 5. On panel 25VU, place the ANN LT switch in the BRT position: A. On panel 402VU, make sure that the upper light (UNLK legend) of the 3 LANDING GEAR POSITION INDICATING LIGHT UNITS goes OFF and that the lower light (triangle) remains on (Green color). B. On panel 23VU and panel 24VU, make sure that, for ELAC 1 (2), SEC 1 (2) (3), the FAULT and OFF legends of the associated FLT CTL pushbutton switch go off. 6. On A/C 3209–3211 A. On C/B panel 49VU: close circuit breaker FWS/FWC1/SPLY location E02. 7. On A/C 3212–3278, 3101–3171, and A321 aircraft A. On C/B panel 49VU: close circuit breaker FWS/FWC1/SPLY location F1. 8. On All A/C A. On C/B panel 121VU: close circuit breaker EIS/FWC2/SPLY location Q07. 9. After few seconds, some warnings will appear on upper ECAM display unit. Press CLEAR until MEMO page appears. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: CLR pb

name: T/O CONFIG pb
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided T/O configuration is verified PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: STS pb
placard: PAGE MANUAL CALL PUSHBUTTON INOP ___ CLR PUSHBUTTON(S) INOP T/O CONFIG PB INOP ___ STS PUSHBUTTON(S) INOP
rem: (O) May be inoperative.
oproc: PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE: Check that the aircraft is in the correct takeoff configuration by: 1. FLAPS/SLATS A. On EWD, check that the FLAPS position corresponds to the position inserted in the MCDU PERF TAKE OFF Page. 2. PITCH TRIM A. Check that the selected pitch trim is in the green band of the pitch trim wheel and corresponds to the value inserted in the MCDU PERF TAKE OFF Page. 3. RUDDER TRIM A. CHECK ZERO 4. GROUND SPOILERS A. CHECK ARMED. 5. SIDESTICK PRIORITY light A. CHECK NO RED. 6. On the WHEEL SD page A. Check that the brakes temperature is below 300 °C. 7. DOORS A. CHECK 8. On the DOOR/OXY SD page A. Check that all doors are indicated CLOSED. 9. Deselect the DOOR/OXY SD page after the check. 31-56-01D When the STS reminder appears at the bottom of the EWD, or whenever the flight crew wants to check the STATUS SD page: 1. Press the RCL pb to display the cleared alerts. 2. Press the CLR pb as many times as necessary in order to automatically display the STATUS SD page. 3. Press the CLR pb when the STATUS SD page is checked to clear the STATUS SD page.
ptext: PAGE MANUAL CALL PUSHBUTTON INOP ___ CLR PUSHBUTTON(S) INOP T/O CONFIG PB INOP ___ STS PUSHBUTTON(S) INOP

name: PFD / ND XFR
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. Captain and F/O PFD and ND units are operative, and 2. PFD to ND automatic switching is checked operative on the flying pilot’s side PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: 1. During flight deck preparation: A. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE on the inoperative PFD/ND XFR pb: i. PFD Brightness control knob – OFF. ii. Check that the PFD image is automatically transferred on ND. iii. PFD Brightness control knob – ON. IF THE PFD IMAGE IS NOT AUTOMATICALLY TRANSFERRED ON THE ASSOCIATED ND CAUTION UNIT, THE AFFECTED SIDE CANNOT BE USED BY THE PILOT FLYING. B. If applicable, on the side where PFD to ND automatic switching is inoperative: CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: PFDU 2 Only
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. PFDU1, NDU1, EWDU, SDU, NDU2 and FCU Channels are operative, 2. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, and 3. RNAV operations do not require PFDU use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements.

Repairs are made within three flight days. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard) PAGE

rem: One or both may be inoperative provided repairs are made within seven landings.

rem: (M) One or both may be broken or missing.

name: Landing Gear Control and Interface Units No.2
rem: (O) (M) LGCIU No.2 may be inoperative provided: 1. Both radio altimeter systems operate normally, 2. Both FCU channels operate normally, 3. All ELAC’s, SEC’s, ADIR’s, SFCC’s, and FAC’s operate normally, 4. Flex takeoff is not used, 5. LGCIU No.2 is deactivated, 6. CVR Erase pb is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 23-71-02E, 7. CVR TEST pb is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 23-71-02F, 8. Predictive Windshear Detection and Avoidance is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 34-41-01F, 9. Fuel Tank Inerting System is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 47-10-01, 10. No.2 engine minimum idle on ground is inoperative and deferred per MEL 73-20-03A, 73-20-03B, 73-20-03C, or 73-20-03D as applicable, 11. Ground external horn is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 23-42-01, 12. External Power Panel ADIRU/AVNCS Vent Caution Light is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 24-30-09, 13. Skin Air Outlet Valve is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL MEL 21-26-04, 14. Skin Air Inlet Valve is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 21-26-05A or MEL 21-26-05B as applicable, 15. Avionic Equipment Ventilation Computer (AEVC) is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 21-26-10, 16. Brake Fan System is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 32-48-01A, 17. Cargo door opening electrical control system is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 52-30-04B, 18. Reverser No.2 is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 78-30-01A, or 78-30-01B as applicable, 19. Runway turn-off light system is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 33-40-03, 20. Taxi and takeoff light system is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 33-40-04, 21. Engine Bump Function is not used, 22. No.2 engine AUTOSTART controls are considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 80-11-05, and 23. Repairs are made within one flight day. Note: As a result of a deactivated LGCIU 2 multiple systems are affected. Note: This item is AD related. PAGE

name: Landing Gear Control and Interface Units No.2
rem: (O) (M) LGCIU No.2 may be inoperative provided: 1. Both radio altimeter systems operate normally, 2. Both FCU channels operate normally, 3. All ELAC’s, SEC’s, ADIR’s, SFCC’s, and FAC’s operate normally, 4. Flex takeoff is not used, 5. LGCIU No.2 is deactivated, 6. The OAT is below ISA +35°C, 7. CVR Erase pb is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 23-71-02E, 8. CVR TEST pb is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 23-71-02F, 9. Predictive Windshear Detection and Avoidance is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 34-41-01F, 10. Fuel Tank Inerting System is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 47-10-01, 11. No.2 engine minimum idle on ground is inoperative and deferred per MEL 73-20-03A, 73-20-03B, 73-20-03C, or 73-20-03D as applicable, 12. Ground external horn is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 23-42-01, 13. External Power Panel ADIRU/AVNCS Vent Caution Light is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 24-30-09, 14. Skin Air Outlet Valve is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL MEL 21-26-04, 15. Skin Air Inlet Valve is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 21-26-05A or MEL 21-26-05B as applicable, 16. Avionic Equipment Ventilation Computer (AEVC) is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 21-26-10, 17. Brake Fan System is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 32-48-01A, 18. Cargo door opening electrical control system is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 52-30-04B, 19. Reverser No.2 is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 78-30-01A, or 78-30-01B as applicable, 20. Runway turn-off light system is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 33-40-03, 21. Taxi and takeoff light system is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 33-40-04, 22. Engine Bump Function is not used, 23. No.2 engine AUTOSTART controls are considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 80-11-05, and 24. Repairs are made within one flight day. Note: As a result of a deactivated LGCIU 2 multiple systems are affected. Note: This item is AD related. PAGE

name: Landing Gear Retraction System
rem: (O) (M) Except for Extended Operations(ETOPS), may be inoperative provided: 1. MCC must provide authorization prior to use, 2. Inoperative components are properly secured by an acceptable procedure, 3. No revenue passengers are carried, 4. A maximum of 19 persons arecarried as authorized by 14 CFR for non-passenger carrying operations, 5. The airplane is operated with landing gear down in accordance with the ODM Ferry section Flight with Landing Gear Down, and 6. Performance corrections are applied.
oproc: Revenue Passengers Not Authorized 1. The captain will conduct a safety briefing on smoking, seat belts, emergency exits, oxygen, and if applicable, flotation equipment for EOW operations 2. Refer to ODM Ferry section, Flight with Landing Gear Down. 3. Verify with maintenance that VMO/MMO switch is in the L/G DOWN position. 4. Comply with applicable procedures in the ODM as listed below: A. A319 ODM - Ferry, 5-1 B. A320 ODM - Ferry, 5-1 C. A321CEO ODM - Ferry 5-1 D. A321NEO ODM- Ferry, 5-1 5. Select the MEL item "LANDING GEAR POSITION EXTENDED" in the Airbus EFB Performance Applications. 32-31-02 LANDING GEAR RETRACTING SYSTEM EFFECTIVITY All FLIGHT PLANNING When Automated Flight Plan System is used, use Performance Code LGD (if available), or When REQUIREMENTS Automated Flight Plan System is NOT available, use conservative factors in ODM to compute the flight planning data. NOTES AWABS Takeoff data is provided for DRY or WET runway conditions only. Takeoffs using ICY, QCTR or HCTR data are not authorized. AWABS Performance corrections have been applied. TAKEOFF Manual WDR not permitted with MEL 32-31-02. PERFORMANCE WORKSHEET
mproc: 1. Perform AMM Task 32-31-00-040-803-A. 2. Install safety devices on the nose and both main landing gear per AMM Task 32-00-00-481-001-A. 3. Inoperative components are properly secured by an acceptable procedure.

name: Landing Gear Gravity Extension System
rem: (O) (M) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS), may be inoperative provided: 1. MCC must provide authorized prior to use, 2. Inoperative components are properly secured by an acceptable procedure, 3. No revenue passengers are carried, 4. A maximum of 19 persons are carried as authorized by 14 CFR for non-passenger carrying operations, and 5. The airplane is operated with landing gear down in accordance with the AFM supplement for gear down flight.
oproc: Revenue Passengers Not Authorized 1. The captain will conduct a safety briefing on smoking, seat belts, emergency exits, oxygen, and if applicable, flotation equipment for EOW operations 2. Refer to ODM Ferry section, Flight with Landing Gear Down. 3. Verify with maintenance that VMO/MMO switch is in the L/G DOWN position. 4. Comply with applicable procedures in the ODM as follows: A. A319 ODM - Ferry, 5-1 B. A320 ODM - Ferry, 5-1 C. A321CEO ODM - Ferry, 5-1 D. A321NEO ODM - Ferry, 5-1 5. Select the MEL item "LANDING GEAR POSITION EXTENDED" in the Airbus EFB Performance Applications. 32-33-01 LANDING GEAR GRAVITY EXTENSION SYSTEM EFFECTIVITY All FLIGHT PLANNING When Automated Flight Plan System is used, use Performance Code LGD (if available), or REQUIREMENTS When Automated Flight Plan System is NOT available, use conservative factors in ODM to compute the flight planning data. NOTES AWABS Takeoff data is provided for DRY or WET runway conditions only. Takeoffs using ICY, QCTR or HCTR data are not authorized. AWABS Performance corrections have been applied. TAKEOFF Manual WDR not permitted with MEL 32-33-01. PERFORMANCE WORKSHEET CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Main Wheel Braking Systems (Associated Tachometer Operative)
rem: (O) (M) One brake may be inoperative provided: 1. Minimum runway width is 148 ft (45 meters), 2. Anti–skid system operates normally, 3. Nose wheel steering operates normally, 4. Affected brake is removed or deactivated, 5. Both reversers operate normally, 6. Green and yellow systems on operative brakes operate normally, 7. Applicable performance corrections for One Brake Inoperative are applied for takeoff and landing, 8. The AUTO/BRK Function is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 32-42-04A, and 9. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements. KJAC (Jackson Hole Airport) operations Authorized on dry runways only.

name: Main Wheel Braking Systems (Associated Tachometer Inoperative)
rem: (O) (M) One brake may be inoperative provided: 1. Minimum runway width is 148 ft (45 meters), 2. Anti–skid system operates normally, 3. Nose wheel steering operates normally, 4. Affected brake is removed or deactivated, 5. Both reversers operate normally, 6. Green and yellow systems on operative brakes operate normally, 7. Applicable performance corrections for One Brake Inoperative are applied for takeoff and landing, 8. The AUTO/BRK Function is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 32-42-04A, and 9. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements. KJAC (Jackson Hole Airport) operations Authorized on dry runways only

name: Green Braking System
rem: (O) (M) Braking on one wheel may be inoperative provided: 1. Minimum runway width is 148 ft (45 meters), 2. Green hydraulic supply to affected brake is deactivated, 3. Anti–skid system operates normally, 4. Nose wheel steering operates normally, 5. Both reversers operate normally, 6. Wheel tachometers are operative, 7. Applicable performance corrections for One Brake Inoperative are applied for takeoff and landing, and 8. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements. KJAC (Jackson Hole Airport) operations Authorized on dry runways only.
oproc: 1. Do not use autobrakes. Note: In case of engine failure at takeoff, retract landing gear when in positive climb. 2. If both engines are operative, keep landing gear down for one minute and observe max gear retract speed 220 kts). The fuel burn increase is negligible. Note: Vibrations may be present during 1 or 2 minutes after landing gear retraction. 3. Verify takeoff and landing runways are at least 148 ft wide. Note: Takeoff memo will not be all green. 4. Select the MEL item ‘BRAKES CONFIGURATION 1 BRAKE INOPERATIVE’ in the Airbus EFB Performance Applications. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Braking/Steering Control Unit (BSCU) Channel 1 / System 1 (All A319 aircraft) (All A320 aircraft) (All A321CEO aircraft) (A321NEO ships 5001-5002)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Alternate brake system is verified to operate normally PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, 2. Brake pressure indicators operate normally, 3. The affected channel/system is deactivated, 4. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, the CFDS does not indicate a L/G SYS DISAGREE caution (Log Book Entry Required), 5. Channel 2/System 2 is operative, 6. The AUTO/BRK (LO, MED, MAX) pb DECEL light is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 32-42-04E, 7. The AUTO/BRK Panel Mode Lights (LO, MED, MAX) ON light is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 32-42-04B, and 8. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements. Note: This item is AD related. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Braking/Steering Control Unit (BSCU) Channel 1 / System 1 (A321NEO ships 5003- Subsequent)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Alternate brake system is verified to operate normally PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, 2. Brake pressure indicators operate normally, 3. The affected channel/system is deactivated, 4. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, the CFDS does not indicate a L/G SYS DISAGREE caution (Log Book Entry Required), 5. Channel 2/System 2 is operative, 6. The AUTO/BRK (LO, MED, MAX) pb DECEL light is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 32-42-04E, 7. The AUTO/BRK Panel Mode Lights (LO, MED, MAX) ON light is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 32-42-04B, 8. Landing gear i kept extended for one minute after takeoff, and 9. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements. Note: This item is AD related. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard) PAGE

name: Braking/Steering Control Unit (BSCU) Channel 2 / System 2 (All A319 aircraft) (All A320 aircraft) (All A321CEO aircraft) (A321NEO ships 5001-5002)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Alternate brake system is verified to operate normally PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, 2. Brake pressure indicators operate normally, 3. The affected channel/system is deactivated, 4. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, the CFDS does not indicate a L/G SYS DISAGREE caution (Log Book Entry Required), 5. Channel 1/System 1 is operative, and 6. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements. Note: This item is AD related. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Braking/Steering Control Unit (BSCU) Channel 2 / System 2 (A321NEO ships 5003- Subsequent)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Alternate brake system is verified to operate normally PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, 2. Brake pressure indicators operate normally, 3. The affected channel/system is deactivated, 4. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, the CFDS does not indicate a L/G SYS DISAGREE caution (Log Book Entry Required), 5. Channel 1/System 1 is operative, 6. Landing gear is kept extended for one minute after takeoff, and 7. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements. Note: This item is AD related. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: 1. Comply with MEL 32-42-04B and MEL 32-42-04E (O) procedures, as applicable. 2. Verify a log book entry stating “repetitive check completed” has been made. 3. For A321NEO ships 5003 and subsequent: CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: AUTO/BRK Panel Mode Lights (LO, MED) ON
rem: (O) One or both may be inoperative provided: 1. LO and/or MED autobrake mode(s) is not used, and 2. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements. Note: If the ECAM WHEEL page operates normally, use MEL item 32–42–04B. KJAC (Jackson Hole Airport) operations Authorized on dry runways only.

name: AUTO/BRK Panel Mode Lights (LO, MED, MAX)
rem: (O) Any or all may be inoperative provided Auto Brake indications on ECAM WHEEL page operate normally.

name: AUTO/BRK Panel Mode Lights (LO, MED) DECEL
rem: (O) One or both may be inoperative provided: 1. Associated autobrake mode(s) is not used, and 2. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements. Note: If the ECAM WHEEL page operates normally, use MEL item 32–42–04E KJAC (Jackson Hole Airport) operations Authorized on dry runways only.
oproc: Do not select or use the AUTO/BRK function. Note: When T.O MEMO is displayed on ECAM EWD, disregard the line: AUTO BRK........MAX (blue) 32-42-04B, 32-42-04E Utilize Auto Brake Indications on ECAM WHEEL page. 32-42-04C, 32-42-04F 1. Do not arm autobrakes for takeoff. 2. Use manual braking during Rejected Takeoff. Note: When T.O MEMO is displayed on ECAM EWD, disregard the line: AUTO BRK........MAX (blue) 32-42-04D, 32-42-04G Do not use associated autobrake mode(s) for landing.

name: Tachometer
rem: (O) One may be inoperative provided: 1. Both LGCIU are operative, 2. All SECs are operative, 3. All ground spoilers are operative, 4. Crosswind component is below 10 kt at departure airport and below 15 kt at arrival airport, 5. Takeoff runway state is no more than wet, 6. Performance penalties are applied, 7. BSCU L4.9B (P/Ns E21327006 / E213271106) has not been installed by interchangeability, and 8. Affected brake is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 32-42-01B. Note: BSCU standard P/N can be checked by BITE Test of the BSCU – LRU Identification.
oproc: 1. Before takeoff check that no other amber brake released indication is displayed on the WHEEL SD page. 2. Select the MEL item "WHEEL TACHOMETERS 1 TACHOMETER INOPERATIVE" in the Airbus EFB Performance Applications. Do not additionally select the brake inoperative MEL item in the application, as its performance impact is already accounted for within the tachometer inoperative scenario. 3. Comply with MEL 32-42-01B (O) Procedure. 32-42-05 TACHOMETER EFFECTIVITY All FLIGHT PLANNING None REQUIREMENTS NOTES Crosswind for Takeoff must be below 10 knots Crosswind for Landing must be below 15 knots AWABS Takeoff data is provided for DRY or WET runway conditions only. Takeoffs using ICY, QCTR or HCTR data are not authorized. AWABS Performance corrections have been applied. TAKEOFF Manual WDR not permitted with MEL 32-42-05. PERFORMANCE WORKSHEET

name: Yellow Brake System
placard: BRAKE SYSTEM INOP AND ON ___ BRAKE INOP
rem: (M) Braking on one wheel may be inoperative provided: 1. Yellow hydraulic supply of affected brake is deactivated, and 2. Both reversers operate normally. Note: This item is AD related.
mproc: THE YELLOW HYDRAULIC BRAKE CONNECTION IS ALWAYS BEHIND THE LDG GEAR. MAKE CERTAIN THAT THE LANDING GEAR SAFETY LOCKS AND THE WHEEL CHOCKS ARE IN POSITION. CAUTION MAKE CERTAIN THAT PARKING BRAKE CONTROL SWITCH IS IN OFF (RELEASED) POSITION BEFORE DEACTIVATION. 1. Depressurize yellow hydraulic systems and install warning tag. 2. Loosen knurled sleeve on hydraulic self–sealing coupling (located behind the ldg gear) and turn to disconnect. 3. Wipe hydraulic half–coupling (hose side) clean and apply 2 or 3 turns of 3/4 to 1 inch wide self adhesive fabric tape around sleeve. 4. Tightly wind 3 or 4 turns of lockwire around mid–area of tape–lagged sleeves 5. Twist the lockwire and put it through the nearest locking hole in the hexagonal head of the half coupling. Tighten the lockwire and twist it. 6. Remove the bleed screw plug (2) from the bleed screw (1) (reference Figure 32-1 Yellow Brake System Disconnect). 7. Connect a vinyl hose to the bleed screw and put other end in a container. 8. Slowly loosen the bleed screw to release hydraulic residual pressure from the brake. 9. Tighten the bleed screw, disconnect the vinyl hose and install the bleed screw plug on the bleed screw. 10. Remove warning tag. 11. Pressurize the yellow hydraulic system and then the hydraulic line associated to deactivated alternate brake and check for leakage with related pedals fully pushed. Figure 32-1: Yellow Brake System Disconnect
ptext: BRAKE SYSTEM INOP AND ON ___ BRAKE INOP

name: ACCU PRESS Indicator (Accumulator) (For A319/A320 Aircraft)
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Both brake pressure indicators operate normally, and 2. Pressure on both brake pressure indicators is verified with parking brake on.

name: ACC PRESS Indicator (Accumulator)
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Both brake pressure indicators operate normally, and 2. Pressure on both brake pressure indicators is verified with parking brake on.

name: ACCU PRESS Indicator (Accumulator)
placard: PRESS INDICATOR INOP PRESS INDICATOR INOP PRESS INDICATOR INOP
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. ACCU PRESS/ACCU ONLY indication is available on ECAM WHEEL page, and 2. Hydraulic pressure of the brake accumulator is checked on ECAM PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE: 1. Place the YELLOW ENG 2 PUMP pb-sw to the OFF position. 2. Place the YELLOW ELEC PUMP pb-sw to the OFF position. 3. Move a side stick in the pitch and roll until the yellow system pressure indication on the HYD SD page indicates 0. 4. Place the PARK BRK handle to the ON position. Note: BRAKES BRK Y ACCU LO PR will be replaced by BRAKES PARK BRK LO PR when the PARK BRK handle is set to the ON position and the yellow system pressure is low. 5. On both BRAKES pressure indicators, check that the BRAKES indication is in the upper part of the white zone (around 2,500 PSI). If required use the electric pump on the hydraulic system to recharge the brake accumulators. YELLOW AND GREEN SYSTEMS ARE PRESSURIZED FROM THE YELLOW ELECTRIC PUMP. GET WARNING GROUND CREW CLEARANCE BEFORE USING THE ELECTRIC PUMP. 6. Restore aircraft to normal configuration. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE
ptext: PRESS INDICATOR INOP PRESS INDICATOR INOP PRESS INDICATOR INOP

name: BRAKES Pressure Indicators
rem: (O) (M) One may be inoperative provided both BSCU channels/systems operate normally. Note: This item is AD related.
oproc: Check ECAM status page to verify that neither BRAKES SYS 1 (2) appear under INOP SYS.
mproc: 1. Assure that all hydraulic systems are depressurized. 2. Assure that the brake accumulator is pressurized. Note: If the alternate brake accumulator is depleted, it will be necessary to pressurize the yellow hydraulic system momentarily prior to this check. 3. Establish contact with ground observer. 4. Place PARKING BRK to OFF. 5. Step on both brake pedals. 6. Have the ground observer check that the wear indicators on associated brake(s) retract. 7. Release Pressure on brake pedals. 8. Have ground observer check that the wear indicators on associated brake(s) extend. 9. Place Parking Brake to ON.

name: Parking Brake External Light
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided parking brake status is verified before moving aircraft.
oproc: Ensure parking brake is off before pushback or taxi.

name: Brake Temperature Monitoring Unit
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided brake ground cooling time is applied.
oproc: Determine the minimum quick turnaround time from the table found in MEL Item 32-60-03H. Note: Pulling the CB per the (M) Procedure instructions disables the entire brake temperature indication system.
mproc: On C/B panel 121VU: pull and secure circuit breaker HYDRAULIC/BRK/TEMP/DET/UNIT location M37.

name: Brake Fan System
rem: May be inoperative.

name: Brake Fan
placard: BRAKE FAN SYSTEM INOP BRAKE FAN INOP
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided affected pair of fans is deactivated.
mproc: 1. To deactivate the Brake Fans 1 and 2: A. Pull and secure circuit breaker HYDRAULIC/BRK FAN/WHEELS/1 AND 2 on the 121VU located at L32 2. To deactivate the Brake Fans 3 and 4: A. Pull and secure circuit breaker HYDRAULIC/BRK FAN/WHEELS/3 AND 4 on the 121VU panel located at L35.
ptext: BRAKE FAN SYSTEM INOP BRAKE FAN INOP

name: PEDALS DISC pb

oproc: Accomplish the rudder check portion of the before takeoff flight control check with the aircraft stopped.

name: Nose Wheel Steering Control system Deactivation Device (Failed In No-Towing Mode)
rem: (O) May be inoperative (no towing mode when lever in TOWING position).

name: Nose Wheel Steering Control System Deactivation Device
placard: STEERING CONTROL SYSTEM DEACTIVATION DEVICE INOP STEERING CONTROL SYSTEM DEACTIVATION DEVICE INOP
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided the NWS electrical deactivation box is deactivated. Note: With the Steering Activation/Deactivation Lever in the NON-TOWING position, the pin the lever clips to (see Figure 32-2) may be missing as long as the Nose Wheel Steering Control System Deactivation Device is deactivated and deferred per MEL 32-51-03B.
oproc: 1. Before pushback: A/SKID and NOSE WHEEL – OFF. 2. After pushback: A/SKID and NOSE WHEEL – ON. 32-51-03B 1. Before pushback: A/SKID and NOSE WHEEL – OFF. 2. After pushback: A/SKID and NOSE WHEEL – ON. 3. Nose gear parking brake light and NW STRG DISC memo will be inoperative. Parking brake setting and tow bar disconnect must be verbally confirmed between flight crew and ground personnel.
mproc: WARNING MAKE SURE THAT THE GROUND SAFETY–LOCKS ARE IN POSITION ON THE LANDING GEAR. MAKE SURE THAT THE TRAVEL RANGES OF THE FLIGHT CONTROL SURFACES ARE CLEAR BEFORE YOU WARNING PRESSURIZE/DEPRESSURIZE A HYDRAULIC SYSTEM. MAKE SURE THAT THE CONTROLS AGREE WITH THE POSITION OF THE ITEMS THEY OPERATE BEFORE WARNING YOU PRESSURIZE A HYDRAULIC SYSTEM. PRESSURIZE ONLY THE HYDRAULIC SYSTEM(S) THAT IS (ARE) NECESSARY FOR THE TASK. ISOLATE THE WARNING OTHER HYDRAULIC SYSTEM(S) BEFORE YOU SUPPLY HYDRAULIC POWER TO THE AIRCRAFT. 1. On C/B panel 121VU: pull and tag circuit breaker HYDRAULIC/BRAKING AND STEERING/SYS2/SPLY location M36. 2. On C/B panel 121VU: pull and tag circuit breaker HYDRAULIC/BRAKING AND STEERING/SYS2/CTL location M35. 3. On C/B panel 121VU: pull and tag circuit breaker HYDRAULIC/BRAKING AND STEERING/SYS1/CTL location M34. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE
ptext: STEERING CONTROL SYSTEM DEACTIVATION DEVICE INOP STEERING CONTROL SYSTEM DEACTIVATION DEVICE INOP

name: Faults Indicated by BRAKES N/WS MINOR FAULT Caution on ECAM EWD
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Caution BRAKES N/WS MINOR FAULT is confirmed to be caused by a fault on an alternate braking system pressure transducer by troubleshooting, and 2. BSCU system 1 and system 2 operate normally.

name: Faults Indicated by BRAKES N/WS MINOR FAULT Caution on ECAM EWD
placard: MINOR FAULT DISPLAYED ON ECAM MINOR FAULT DISPLAYED ON ECAM
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. BRAKES pressure indicators operate normally, 2. Alternate braking system is checked operative PRIOR TO THE FIRST FLIGHT OF THE DAY (log book entry required), 3. It is checked PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE that the following CFDS failure message related to BRAKES N/WS MINOR FAULT is not displayed: ● A fault on an Alternate Pressure Transducer, or ● A fault on a Normal Pressure Transducer. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: 1. The ACCU PRESS indication must be in the green band. A. If ACCU PRESS indication is not in the green band: i. Y ELEC PUMP pb-sw ON until pressure is in the green band then OFF 2. CHOCKS – Check in place. 3. PARKING BRAKE handle – OFF 4. PARKING PEDALS – PRESS A. Apply maximum pressure on both pedals 5. BRAKE PRESSURE (on BRAKES pressure indicators) – CHECK A. Pressure must increase without delay symmetrically on left and right sides for the same application simultaneously applied on left and right pedals. With full pedal deflection, the pressure must be between 900 PSI and 1 100 PSI. 6. BRAKES PEDALS – RELEASE A. Check that no residual pressure in the brakes remains. 7. PARKING BRAKE handle – ON
mproc: Confirmed the BRAKES N/WS MINOR FAULT is caused by an alternate braking system pressure transducer by performing BSCU Ground Scan per AMM Task 32-46-00-740-002-A. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE
ptext: MINOR FAULT DISPLAYED ON ECAM MINOR FAULT DISPLAYED ON ECAM

name: Cockpit and Instrument Panel Lighting System
rem: Individual lights may be inoperative provided: 1. Remaining lights are sufficient to clearly illuminate all required instruments, controls, and other devices for which they are provided, 2. Remaining lights are positioned so that direct rays are shielded from flight crewmembers eyes, 3. Lighting configuration and intensity is acceptable to the flight crew, 4. RH dome light is operative, and 5. The left section of the center main panel flood light operates normally. Note: Incandescent RH Dome light is considered operative with a minimum of three bulbs illuminated in each assembly. The bulbs can be checked by selecting the DOME toggle switch from DIM to BRT and ensure both functions operate. Note: Individual buttons/switch lights and/ or annunciations and indications are excluded from this relief.

name: Cockpit and Instrument Panel Lighting System
placard: COCKPIT LIGHT(S) IS INOP COCKPIT LIGHT(S) IS INOP
rem: Individual lights may be inoperative provided: 1. Remaining lights are sufficient to clearly illuminate all required instruments, controls, and other devices for which they are provided, 2. Remaining lights are positioned so that direct rays are shielded from flight crewmembers eyes, 3. Lighting configuration and intensity is acceptable to the flight crew, and 4. RH dome light is operative. Note: LED RH Dome light is considered operative when all bulbs/segments are operative and BRT, DIM, and OFF functions operate normally. Note: Individual buttons/switch lights and/ or annunciations and indications are excluded from this relief.
ptext: COCKPIT LIGHT(S) IS INOP COCKPIT LIGHT(S) IS INOP

rem: May be inoperative.

name: Cabin Light System
rem: (O) Individual lights may be inoperative provided remaining lighting is sufficient for cabin attendants to perform their duties. Note: POD Lighting is not considered part of the cabin illumination lighting system. Use NEF 33-24-09 for POD Lighting deferrals.

name: Flight Deck Door Security Lighting System
placard: ___ CABIN LIGHT(S) IS INOP DOOR SECURITY LIGHTING SYSTEM INOP
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided alternate procedures are used to visually observe personnel requesting flight deck entry.
oproc: Ensure remaining lights are sufficient for cabin attendants to perform their duties. 33-20-01B Captain to brief Lead Flight Attendant/Purser to utilize a flashlight to illuminate the area outside the flight deck door to allow visual verification.
ptext: ___ CABIN LIGHT(S) IS INOP DOOR SECURITY LIGHTING SYSTEM INOP

name: Passenger Lighted Information Signs (Inoperative PA)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Associated passenger seat or lavatory is not occupied from which a passenger lighted information sign is not readily legible, 2. Associated passenger seat or lavatory is blocked and placarded UNSERVICEABLE – DO NOT USE, 3. If placarded, lavatory is used only by crewmembers, and 4. A minimum of two lavatories must operate normally. Note: These conditions are not intended to prohibit lavatory use or inspections by crewmembers. Ensure lavatory door is locked closed after crewmember inspection.

name: Passenger Lighted Information Signs (Operative PA)
placard: LIGHTED INFORMATION SIGN(S) IS INOP SEATS AND/OR LAVATORY ARE BLOCKED LIGHTED INFORMATION SIGN(S) IS INOP
rem: (O) May be inoperative and associated passenger seat or lavatory may be occupied provided: 1. PA system operates normally, and 2. PA system is used to notify passengers and cabin crew when associated sign(s) are placed on or off.
oproc: 1. Captain to brief Lead Flight Attendant/Purser on the following applicable items: A. Which passenger seat(s) is blocked. B. If a lavatory is blocked, that the affected lavatory door is to remain locked and not entered except for use or inspection by crewmembers. 2. Captain is to ensure the lavatory door is locked closed if crewmember inspection has been performed or if the lavatory is used by a crewmember. 33-20-02B 1. Ensure that PA system operates normally and can be heard through out cabin during flight. 2. Captain is to brief the Lead Flight Attendant/ Purser to use PA system to alert the passengers. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE
ptext: LIGHTED INFORMATION SIGN(S) IS INOP SEATS AND/OR LAVATORY ARE BLOCKED LIGHTED INFORMATION SIGN(S) IS INOP

rem: (O) (M) Must be operative between sunset and sunrise.

rem: (M) NAV 1 or NAV 2 system may be inoperative.

name: Forward Navigation Lights – Aircraft Equipped With Two Navigation Lights System (UTC – Goodrich light assembly)
rem: (O) (M) Must be operative between sunset and sunrise. PAGE

name: Landing Lights
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative for operations between sunrise and sunset. Theater Restrictions May Apply

name: Runway Turn- Off Light System
rem: (M) One or both may be inoperative. Note: Assemblies with LED’s are considered inoperative if more than 2 LEDs are inoperative. Theater Restrictions May Apply
mproc: 1. Deactivate the inoperative light by pulling and securing the appropriate circuit breaker: A. For the left runway turnoff light; i. LIGHTING/EXT LT/RWY TURN OFF/L on panel 122VU at location W06 B. For the right runway turnoff light; i. LIGHTING/EXT LT/RWY TURN OFF/R on panel 122VU at location W05

name: Taxi and Takeoff Light System
rem: (M) One or both may be inoperative. Note: Assemblies with LED’s are considered inoperative if more than 4 LEDs are inoperative. Theater Restrictions May Apply
mproc: 1. Deactivate the inoperative light(s) by pulling and securing the appropriate circuit breaker: A. To deactivate the taxi light pull and secure the LIGHTING/EXT LT/TAXI AND TAKE OFF/TAXI circuit breaker on the 122VU panel located at W08. B. To deactivate the takeoff light pull and secure the LIGHTING/EXT LT/TAXI AND TAKE OFF/TO circuit breaker on the 122VU panel located at W07.

name: Logo Lights
rem: (M) One or both may be inoperative. Note: Assemblies with LED’s are considered inoperative if more than 5 LEDs are inoperative.
mproc: 1. Deactivate the inoperative light(s) by pulling and securing the appropriate circuit breaker: A. To deactivate the right logo light pull and secure LIGHTING/LOGO R circuit breaker on the 122VU panel located at V02. B. To deactivate the left logo light pull and secure LIGHTING/LOGO L circuit breaker on the 122VU panel located at V03.

name: Beacon Lights
rem: (O) (M) One or both may be inoperative provided: 1. Strobe lights operate normally, and 2. Alternate procedures are used.

name: Strobe Lights
placard: COLLISION LIGHTS IS INOP COLLISION LIGHTS IS INOP
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Beacon lights operate normally, and 2. Alternate procedures are used.
oproc: 1. If the bottom beacon is inoperative, the Captain and Ramp Agent must coordinate engine start using FOM Ramp Operations Communication procedures for verbal or hand signals. 33-40-06B 1. Place the STROBE switch to the OFF position.
mproc: 1. Deactivate the inoperative light(s) by pulling and securing the appropriate circuit breaker: A. For the upper anti-collision light pull and secure LIGHTING/EXT LT/BEACON/UPPER on the 122VU panel located at U06. B. For the lower anti-collision light pull and secure LIGHTING/EXT LT/BEACON/LOWER on the 122VU panel located at U03. 33-40-06B 1. Deactivate the inoperative wing/tail strobe light by pulling and securing the following circuit breaker: A. Pull and secure LIGHTING/WING/STROBE circuit breaker on panel 122VU located at V05.
ptext: COLLISION LIGHTS IS INOP COLLISION LIGHTS IS INOP

name: Wing Scan Lights
rem: (O) (M) One or both may be inoperative provided when a pre–takeoff contamination check is necessary: 1. Adequate lighting exists, OR 2. A return to gate or de–icing site is accomplished. Note: Assemblies with LED’s are considered inoperative if more than 2 LEDs are inoperative.
oproc: 1. When a pre–takeoff contamination check is necessary and lighting conditions are not adequate: ● Return to gate or de–icing site. 2. Reference Operations Manual Volume I, Supplementary Procedures, Adverse Weather for Cold Weather Operation section for Exterior Inspection requirements.
mproc: 1. Deactivate the inoperative wing scan light(s) by pulling and securing the appropriate circuit breaker(s): A. For the right wing scan light pull and secure circuit breaker LIGHTING/EXT LT/WING/AND ENG SCAN/R on the 122VU panel located at W03. B. For the left wing scan light pull and secure circuit breaker LIGHTING/EXT LT/WING/AND ENG SCAN/L on the 122VU panel located at W02.

name: Floor Proximity Emergency Escape Path Marking System (A319 Aircraft with AA
rem: Individual lights may be inoperative as indicated. 1. Up to 50% of the bulbs in the cabin may be inoperative provided no two consecutive bulbs are inoperative, 2. One of the two red bulbs at each overwing exit group may be inoperative, and 3. One of the two white bulbs at each first class seat may be inoperative. Note: All interior Emergency Exit sign light covers must be installed. Note: LED EXIT Identifiers must operate normally.

name: Floor Proximity Emergency Escape Path Marking System (All A321CEO Aircraft) (All A321NEO Aircraft)
rem: Individual lights may be inoperative as indicated. 1. Seat mounted emergency light modules in the vicinity of the doors and emergency exits (red) must operate normally, 2. First seat mounted emergency light module in first class section must be operative, 3. Last seat mounted emergency light module in tourist class section must be operative, 4. For the A321CEO, 18 individual, non-adjacent, seat-mounted emergency lights in the main aisles may be inoperative, 5. For the A321NEO, 14 individual, non-adjacent, seat-mounted emergency lights in the main aisles may be inoperative, and 6. Floor proximity light covers must be installed on all operative lights. Note: All interior Emergency Exit sign light covers must be installed. Note: LED EXIT Identifiers must operate normally.

name: Floor Proximity Emergency Escape Path Marking System (A319 Aircraft without AA
rem: Individual lights may be inoperative as indicated. 1. Seat mounted emergency lights in the vicinity of emergency exits (red) must be operative, 2. First seat mounted emergency light in first class section must be operative, 3. Last seat mounted emergency light in tourist class section must be operative, 4. 8 of the remaining seat mounted emergency lights seats may be inoperative provided no two consecutive bulbs are inoperative, and 5. Floor proximity light covers must be installed on all operative lights. Note: All interior Emergency Exit sign light covers must be installed. Note: LED EXIT Identifiers must operate normally. PAGE

name: BAT TEST pb on the PTP
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided that the batteries are verified correctly charged. Note: Battery tests required by the maintenance program cannot be exceeded.
mproc: 1. On the overhead panel 25VU make sure the EMER EXIT LT switch 4WL is in the OFF position. 2. On the overhead panel 25VU make sure the NO SMOKING switch 190RH is in the OFF position. 3. On the Forward Attendant Panel (FAP) 120RH, make sure the LIGHT EMER pushbutton switch and its integral light are off. 4. For the A319 and A320 aircraft perform the following: A. On the PTP 110RH, press the TEST EMER LIGHT/SYS membrane switch for not less than 3 seconds. B. On the PTP display 110RH: ● The EMER SYSTEM ATA 33-51-36 TEST OK status comes on. ● The SYS OK light goes off after approximately 60 seconds. Note: If the SYS OK LED comes on, there are no limitations in the operation of the emergency light system (REF AMM 33-51-00-710-001). 5. For the A321 aircraft perform the following: A. In the cockpit on the MCDU : ● Push the line select key CIDS 1 displaying the menu page. ● Push the line select key SYSTEM TESTS displaying the tests on the menu page. ● Push the line select key EPSU SYS TEST. ● TEST IN PROGRESS shows for 60 seconds then TEST OK shows

name: LLM Category II/III Status (Lower Landing Minimums)
rem: (M) Aircraft restricted from CAT II/III approaches, autoland not permitted due to AMT is non-AFQ authorized. CAT II/III Not Authorized
mproc: 1. The AMT or flight crew will be instructed by MCC to create a new log item in the aircraft logbook using MEL Item Number 34-00-00L (contact MCC for instructions). 2. The deferral code LLM must also be entered on the log page and the placard when deferring this item. 3. MCC authorization is required prior to its usage or removal.

name: IR 1 (IR 1 NAV Mode)
rem: (O) NAV Mode of IR 1 may be inoperative provided: 1. IR 1 is operated in ATT mode, 2. IR 2 and IR 3 are operative, 3. GPS 1 is operative, 4. EGPWS/TAWS is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 34-48-01F, 5. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, and 6. RNAV operations do not require IR 1 NAV Mode use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements. Theater Restrictions May Apply

The FMGC 1 is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 22-83-01, 7. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, and 8. Repairs are made within three flight legs. Theater Restrictions May Apply

name: Ground Speed Indications on ND

oproc: If indication is inoperative on one side: 1. For aircraft with the RNP AR capability, RNP AR operations are not permitted. If indication is inoperative on both sides: 1. RNAV 10 operations are not permitted. 2. RNAV 5 operations are not permitted. 3. RNAV 1 / RNAV 2 operations are not permitted. 4. RNP 4 operations are not permitted. 5. RNP 2 operations are not permitted. 6. RNP 1 operations are not permitted. 7. RNP APCH operations are not permitted. 8. For aircraft with the RNP AR capability, RNP AR operations are not permitted.

RNAV 10 operations are not permitted. 2. RNAV 5 operations are not permitted. 3. RNAV 1 / RNAV 2 operations are not permitted. 4. RNP 4 operations are not permitted. 5. RNP 2 operations are not permitted. 6. RNP 1 operations are not permitted. 7. RNP APCH operations are not permitted. 8. For aircraft with the RNP AR capability, RNP AR operations are not permitted.

name: Standby Horizon (Attitude)
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. Operations are conducted in Day VMC only, and 2. Operations are not conducted into known or forecast over–the–top conditions. Note: “Over–the–top” means above the layer of clouds or other obscuring phenomena forming the ceiling.
oproc: Limit operation to Day VMC only.

name: Non-Stabilized Magnetic Compass (Standby)
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. Any combination of two IRs are operative, and 2. Airplane is operated with dual independent navigation capability and under positive radar control by ATC on enroute portion of flight.

name: Non-Stabilized Magnetic Compass (Standby) Lighting
placard: STAND-BY COMPASS INOP STAND-BY COMPASS INOP STAND-BY COMPASS INOP STAND-BY COMPASS LIGHTING INOP
rem: May be inoperative.
oproc: During flight deck preparation: 1. ATT HDG sel – CAPT 3 Check normal display on CAPT PFD, ND and RMI VOR. A. ATT HDG sel – F/O 3. Check normal display on F/O PFD, ND. B. ATT HDG sel – NORM. C. EIS DMC – CAPT 3. Check normal display on CAPT PFD, ND. D. EIS DMC – F/O 3. Check normal display on F/O PFD, ND. E. EIS DMC – NORM. F. CAPT PFD/ND – XFR. Check that CAPT PFD and ND images are interchanged. G. CAPT PFD/ND – XFR. H. F/O PFD/ND – XFR. Check that F/O PFD and ND images are interchanged. I. F/O PFD/ND – XFR. 34-22-02B Utilize DDRMI for compass information. 34-22-02C Maintain positive radar control by ATC during enroute portion of flight.
ptext: STAND-BY COMPASS INOP STAND-BY COMPASS INOP STAND-BY COMPASS INOP STAND-BY COMPASS LIGHTING INOP

name: GPWS – G/S pb or PULL UP – GPWS pb-sw or PULL UP – GS pb-sw
rem: (M) Both may be inoperative provided: 1. GPWS is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 34-48-01A, 2. EGPWS/TAWS (Terrain Awareness and Warning System) is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 34-48-01F, and 3. Repairs are made within two flight days.
oproc: 1. With the aircraft on the ground, press and hold the operational GPWS pb until all warnings are checked. Figure 34-1: GPWS pb
mproc: 1. Defer GPWS per MEL 34-48-01A. 2. Defer EGPWS/TAWS (Terrain Awareness and Warning System) per MEL 34-48-01F.
