name: Radio Altimeter (RA) Systems - (RA 1)
rem: (O) (M) RA 1 may be inoperative provided: 1. RA 2 operates normally, 2. Both FCU channels operate normally, 3. All ELACs, SECs, ADIRS, SFCC, LGCIU, and FACs operate normally, 4. Defer GPWS per MEL 34-48-01A, 5. Defer EGPWS per MEL 34-48-01F, 6. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, 7. RNAV operations do not require RA 1 use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements, and 8. Repairs are made within two flight days. Theater Restrictions May Apply

name: Radio Altimeter (RA) Systems - (RA 2)
rem: (O) (M) RA 2 may be inoperative provided: 1. RA 1 operates normally, 2. Both FCU channels operate normally, 3. All ELACs, SECs, ADIRS,SFCC, LGCIU, and FACs operate normally, 4. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, 5. RNAV operations do not require RA 2 use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements, and 6. Repairs are made within three flight days.
oproc: 1. If RA 1 is inoperative comply with MEL Item 34-48-01A and 34-48-01F (O) procedures. 2. F/CTL MAINTENANCE message maybe displayed on the STATUS SD page. 3. During an ILS (or MLS, or GLS) approach with AP engaged, the flight crew must closely monitor all primary flight parameters and FMA indications. An early/untimely FLARE and THR IDLE mode engagement may occur in the case of an erroneous RA height indication from the remaining RA. In the event of an early/untimely FLARE and THR IDLE mode engagement: ● Immediately perform an automatic go-around (thrust levers set to TOGA), OR ● Immediately disconnect AP and set both FDs to OFF. If external visual references are sufficient, the approach may be continued manually. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Automatic Call Out System
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use; refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements.
oproc: 1. CAT II – Not Authorized. 2. CAT III – Not Authorized. 3. Autoland – Not Authorized.

name: Altitude Alerting System
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. Autopilot with altitude hold and altitude capture is operative, 2. Enroute operations do not require it use, 3. Aircraft cannot depart from ATL, DTW, or MSP, 4. Repairs are made within three flight days, and 5. The airplane remains at or below FL280. RVSM Not Authorized

name: Altitude Alerting System Aural Alert
rem: May be inoperative provided: 1. Visual alert operates normally, and 2. Autopilot with altitude hold and altitude capture is operative.

name: Altitude Alerting System Visual Alert
placard: ALERTING SYSTEM INOP ALERTING SYSTEM AURAL ALERT INOP ALERTING SYSTEM VISUAL ALERT INOP
rem: May be inoperative provided: 1. Aural alert operates normally, and 2. Autopilot with altitude hold and altitude capture is operative.
oproc: 1. RVSM operations – Not Authorized. Limit flight altitude to FL280 or below. 2. Utilize altitude hold function of A/P. 3. Use standard altitude awareness during climbs and descents.
ptext: ALERTING SYSTEM INOP ALERTING SYSTEM AURAL ALERT INOP ALERTING SYSTEM VISUAL ALERT INOP

name: Traffic Alert and Collision Avoidance System (TCAS System)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. System is deactivated and secured, 2. Routing restrictions for TCAS are observed, 3. For A321 aircraft, defer EGPWS/TAWS per MEL 34-48-01F, 4. For A321 aircraft defer GPWS per MEL 34-48-01A 5. For A321 aircraft defer ADS-B Out Extended Squitter Transmissions per MEL 34-57-02B, 6. For A321NEO aircraft defer ATSAW Function per MEL 34-40-13, 7. RNAV operations do not require TCAS use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements, and 8. Repairs are made within 3 flight cycles. Note: TCAS must be operative on routes outbound from the Continental United States to destinations outside the Continental United States, except Canada. Theater Restrictions May Apply

name: DME

PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, dispatch has obtained approval from ATC facilities having jurisdiction over the planned route of flight, 3. TCAS is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 34-43-01A, 4. Example: compass locator, precision radar, surveillance radar, or fix from NDB, VOR or DME. Refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimum Requirements, OR May be inoperative for VMC flight conditions. Note: May be required as noted on approach plate.

name: ADS-B Out Extended Squitter Transmissions
rem: (O) One may be inoperative provided RNAV operations do not require ADS-B use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements.

name: ADS-B Out Extended Squitter Transmissions
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. Alternate procedures are used, and 2. Prior to flight, authorization is obtained from ATC facilities having jurisdiction over planned route of flight using an approved authorization process. Note: Any ADS-B function that operates normally may be used.
oproc: For one inoperative ADS-B Out, select transponder to operative side. 34-57-02B Ensure the Flight Dispatch Release indicates that ATC approval has been obtained along the planned route. If ATC approval cannot be confirmed, contact Flight Control for confirmation and an amended Flight Dispatch Release if required.

name: Global Positioning System
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Destination and alternate airports do not require a RNAV (GPS) approach, 2. Defer ADS-B Out Extended Squitter Transmissions per MEL 34-57-02A or 34-57-02B as applicable, 3. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimums Requirement, and 4. RNAV operations do not require GPS use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements. Note: GLS AUTOLAND and/or GLS2 may be displayed when GPS is inoperative. Refer to MEL 34-30-03. Theater Restrictions May Apply

name: Global Positioning System
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Destination and alternate airports do not require a RNAV (GPS) approach, 2. Defer ADS-B Out Extended Squitter Transmissions per MEL 34-57-02A or 34-57-02B as applicable, 3. One DME is operative, 4. Enroute operations and approach minimums do not require its use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for Lower Landing Minimums Requirement, and 5. RNAV operations do not require GPS use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements. Note: GLS AUTOLAND and/or GLS2 may be displayed when GPS is inoperative. Refer to MEL 34-30-03. Theater Restrictions May Apply
oproc: Ensure destination and alternate airports do not require a RNAV (GPS) approach. 34-58-01B 1. RNAV procedures that require the use of GPS – Not Authorized. 2. Ensure destination and alternate airports do not require a RNAV (GPS) approach. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Navigation Database Out of Currency
rem: (O) (M) May be out of currency provided: 1. Current Aeronautical Charts or current Nav Database is used to verify Navigation Fixes PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, 2. Pilot or Flight Control use charts or RVAL Tool to verify status and suitability of Navigation Facilities used to define route of flight, 3. Approach Navigation Radios are manually tuned and identified, and 4. Dispatch will not file a flight plan requiring RNAV data that is identified as changed within the current database. (Refer to Navigation Equipment Tab [this manual] for RNAV Minimum Requirements). Theater Restrictions May Apply Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE: 1. Select the route to be flown from the (non-current) data base. 2. Check the intended route against current Jeppesen pages for agreement of all navigation data, using applicable departure, enroute and arrival charts, or request Flight Control to validate the database using the RVAL Tool. 3. If all information agrees, the FMS may be used normally on the route which was checked. Each new route must be checked in the same manner. 4. Any time the FMS data does not agree with current Jeppesen information, manual tuning and course selection must be accomplished.
mproc: 1. Verify database to be within twenty eight (28) days of the effective and active date range. 2. Enter a statement in the log book such as, “NAV Database out of currency.” 3. MCC will CAR the log for update at next available RON. 4. MCC will close the applicable FMS Database Load forecast to prevent over-run. 5. MCC or Maintenance will create a MON log.

name: Group 3 Radio Altimeter (RA) Installed
rem: (O) Aircraft restricted from Autoland / SA CAT II / CAT II / CAT III in the 48 Contiguous United States due to 5G related limitations. Note: This item is AD Related.
oproc: The following operations are NOT AUTHORIZED in the 48 Contiguous United States (Reference 5G Fleet Bulletin): ● Autoland (including practice Autolands) ● SA CAT II ILS Approach ● CAT II ILS Approach ● CAT III ILS Approach

name: Exterior Oxygen Overpressure Indicator Disc (Green Disc)
rem: (O) May be damaged or missing provided PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE the oxygen pressure is verified not to be decreasing. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE verify that oxygen pressure is not decreasing by checking the OXY pressure indication on ECAM DOOR/OXY page twice with a 15 minute interval between each check. Figure 35-1: Overboard Discharge Indicators

name: OXY High Pressure Indication
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. The oxygen pressure is verified by direct reading PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required), 2. The oxygen pressure is sufficient for the intended flight, and 3. The REGUL LO PR indication is operative. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: REGUL LO PR Indication
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided the oxygen pressure is verified PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required). Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: 1. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, verify logbook entry has been made by maintenance for MEL/CDL REQUIRED FOLLOW UP ACTION as stated on the (yellow) MEL Placard placed in the logbook. 2. Ensure that the oxygen pressure is sufficient for the intended flight. 35-13-01B 1. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, verify logbook entry has been made by maintenance for MEL/CDL REQUIRED FOLLOW UP ACTION as stated on the (yellow) MEL Placard placed in the logbook. 2. Disregard the REGUL LO PR indication and associated OXY indication on ECAM DOOR/OXY page.
mproc: PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE: 1. Check the OXYGEN pressure on direct reading pressure gauge, access through door 812. Note: For the A321NEO, pressure must be checked on both the Captain System and First Officer System. 2. Review Log/Sceptre history to ensure NO history of leakage. 3. Record cylinder pressure(s) in logbook. 4. Accomplish required FOLLOW UP ACTION (Yellow Placard) for logbook entry. 35-13-01B PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE: 1. Perform AMM Task 35-10-00-040-002-A.

name: OFF Light
rem: May be inoperative.

name: Passenger Oxygen Modules
rem: (M) Modules may be inoperative provided associated seats are placarded UNSERVICEABLE – DO NOT USE. Note: MCC must contact Flight Control for coordinating with ROC to remove seat from inventory until repaired.

name: Passenger Oxygen Modules
rem: (O) (M) Modules may be inoperative provided airplane remains at or below FL250.

name: Galley Modules
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative and associated galley occupied provided airplane remains at or below FL250.

name: Galley Modules
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative and associated galley occupied provided a portable oxygen bottle and mask are available for the associated galley occupant.

name: Flight Attendant Oxygen Module
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided the associated flight attendant seat is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 25-22-01A, 25-22-01B, or 25-22-01C as applicable.

name: Flight Attendant Oxygen Module
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided the airplane remains at or below FL 250.

name: Lavatory Oxygen Module
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided lavatory is placarded “UNSERVICABLE – DO NOT USE”.
mproc: 1. Close and lock the Lavatory Door as follows: A. Lift the LAVATORY sign on the outer face of the lavatory door. B. Move the latch under the sign until the indicator shows the door is locked (OCCUPIED). C. Check that the door will not open. 2. Place a strip of Red colored OM2 “UNSERVICEABLE – DO NOT USE” tape (or equivalent) across the lavatory door and across the toilet lid to prevent use. Note: An acceptable alternate to the OM2 tape is a section of 2 inch wide masking tape with the words “UNSERVICEABLE – DO NOT USE” printed legibly in one inch black letters.

name: Passenger Oxygen Manual Control System
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided airplane remains at or below FL250.
oproc: Operate aircraft at or below FL250.

name: Passenger Oxygen AUTO Control System
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Airplane remains at or below FL300, and 2. Passenger oxygen manual control system is verified to operate normally.

name: Passenger Oxygen AUTO Control System
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided airplane remains at or below FL250.
oproc: 1. Operate aircraft at or below FL300. 35-23-02B 1. Operate aircraft at or below FL250.
mproc: 1. Verify the passenger oxygen manual control system is operational by performing AMM Task 35-23-00-040-802.

name: Portable Oxygen Bottles or Units (Including Masks and Hoses)
rem: (O) (M) Any in excess of those required by 14 CFR may be inoperative or removed provided: 1. An inoperative or not properly serviced portable oxygen bottle/unit remains in a certified location until removed or serviced at the next suitable maintenance facility, 2. Location placarding is removed or obscured, and 3. Required distribution of serviceable bottles is maintained throughout the aircraft. Note: An expired item must be removed from the aircraft. Note: Inoperative portable oxygen bottles or units, removed from a certified location or removed from the aircraft, are subject to 49 CFR dangerous goods regulations. Note: Medical equipment installed in the aircraft as part of an EMS operations is not considered part of the normal complement of equipment. No MEL relief applies to that equipment and 14 CFR maintenance and inspection requirements do not apply.

name: Protective Breathing Equipment (PBE) Units
rem: (M) One forward passenger compartment PBE unit may be inoperative provided: 1. Inoperative PBE remains in a certified location until removed from the aircraft at the next suitable maintenance facility, 2. Location placarding is removed or obscured, and 3. Required distribution is maintained. Note: Inoperative PBE units removed from a certified location, or is removed from the aircraft, are subject to dangerous goods regulations. Note: An expired item must be removed from the aircraft.

name: Protective Breathing Equipment (PBE) - Tamper Seals or Tags
placard: ___ PBE IS INOP/MISSING PBE TAMPER SEALS/TAGS INOP/DAMAGED/MISSING
rem: (O) May be inoperative, damaged, or missing provided proper installation and servicing is verified PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: 1. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE: A. Captain to brief Lead Flight Attendant/Purser that one or more PBE Tamper Seal or Tag is inoperative, damaged, or missing and proper installation/servicing has to be verified: i. If one tamper seal or tag is missing, PBE is servicable and no further action is required. ii. If both tamper seals or tags are missing: a. Remove PBE and inspect for obvious damage (punctures, rips in pouch, etc.). b. Reinstall PBE and ensure proper installation. Note: If PBE is damaged or installation is in question, maintenance is required.
mproc: 1. Inoperative PBE is located in its container or removed from aircraft. 2. Cover up the PBE location placard or remove it. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE
ptext: ___ PBE IS INOP/MISSING PBE TAMPER SEALS/TAGS INOP/DAMAGED/MISSING

name: Fault(s) Indicated by AIR
rem: (M) May be displayed provided it is verified that the CFDS does not report a fault on APU Leak detection loop.

name: Fault(s) Indicated by AIR
rem: (M) May be displayed provided APU Leak detection loop is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 36-22-17A, 36-22-17B, or 36-22-17C as applicable. Note: Dispatch with this maintenance status message displayed on ECAM is permitted without CFDS interrogation.

name: Fault(s) Indicated by AIR
rem: (M) May be displayed provided it is verified that the CFDS does not report a fault on BMC, pylon leak detection loop, or APU Leak detection loop.

name: Fault(s) Indicated by AIR
rem: (M) May be displayed provided: 1. Verify CFDS does not report faults on BMC 1, LH pylon leak detection loop or the APU Leak detection loop, 2. If CFDS reports a fault on BMC 2, BMC 2 is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 36-11-08F, or 36-11-08H as applicable, and 3. If CFDS reports a fault on the RH pylon leak detection loop, RH pylon leak detection loop is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 36-22-15B, 36-22-15C or 36-22-15D as applicable.

name: Fault(s) Indicated by AIR
rem: (M) May be displayed provided: 1. Verify CFDS does not report faults on BMC 2, RH pylon leak detection loop, 2. If CFDS reports a fault on BMC 1, BMC 1 is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 36-11-08E, or 36-11-08G as applicable, 3. If CFDS reports a fault on the LH pylon leak detection loop, LH pylon leak detection loop is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 36-22-15B, 36-22-15C or 36-22-15D as applicable, and 4. If CFDS reports a fault on APU leak detection loop, APU leak detection loop is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 36-22-17A, 36-22-17B, 36-22-17C, or 36-22-17D as applicable.

name: Fault(s) Indicated by AIR
rem: Note: Dispatch with maintenance status message displayed on ECAM is permitted without CFDS interrogation. PAGE

name: Bleed Air Supply Systems
rem: (O) (M) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS), one may be inoperative provided: 1. The associated ENG BLEED p.b. sw is selected OFF, 2. Upon initial MEL application, MCC shall review the 7 day history, including Post Flight Reports, of the operative pneumatic bleed system to ensure no known conditions exist that could prevent an adequate supply of bleed air for cabin pressurization, 3. Airplane is not operated in known or forecast structural icing conditions, 4. Airplane remains at or below FL310, 5. The X-BLEED valve selector switch is selected OPEN, and 6. No live animals or temperature sensitive cargo are carried in the AFT cargo compartment. Note: This item is ETOPS CMP related. Theater Restrictions May Apply

name: Bleed Air Supply Systems (All A320 Aircraft) (All A321CEO/A321NEO Aircraft)
rem: (O) (M) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS), one may be inoperative provided: 1. The associated ENG BLEED p.b. sw is selected OFF, 2. Upon initial MEL application, MCC shall review the 7 day history, including Post Flight Reports, of the operative pneumatic bleed system to ensure no known conditions exist that could prevent an adequate supply of bleed air for cabin pressurization, 3. Airplane is not operated in known or forecast structural icing conditions, 4. The speed brakes are operative, 5. The X-BLEED valve selector switch is selected OPEN, and 6. No live animals or temperature sensitive cargo are carried in the AFT cargo compartment. Note: This item is ETOPS CMP related. Theater Restrictions May Apply PAGE

name: Bleed Air Supply Systems (For A319 Aircraft)
rem: (O) (M) One may be inoperative provided: 1. The associated ENG BLEED p.b. sw is selected OFF, 2. Upon initial MEL application, MCC shall review the 7 day history, including Post Flight Reports, of the operative pneumatic bleed system to ensure no known conditions exist that could prevent an adequate supply of bleed air for cabin pressurization, 3. Airplane is not operated in known or forecast structural icing conditions, 4. Airplane remains at or below FL 370, 5. The speed brakes are operative, 6. The X-BLEED valve selector switch is selected OPEN, and 7. No live animals or temperature sensitive cargo are carried in the AFT cargo compartment. Theater Restrictions May Apply
oproc: Note: Pneumatic air pressure will be low with throttles at idle. Disregard ECAM messages related to low pneumatic system pressure. 1. Ensure live animals or temperature sensitive cargo are not loaded in the AFT Cargo compartment. Note: Temperature sensitive cargo are materials sensitive to important temperature variations or sensitive to lack of ventilation. 2. WING ANTI ICE pb – OFF. 3. Do not operate airplane in known or forecast structural icing conditions. 4. Operate aircraft at or below FL310. 5. For the A319/A320 aircraft: PACK FLOW – LO. 6. For the A321 aircraft: ECON FLOW – ON. 7. AFT CARGO HOT AIR – OFF. 8. Affected ENG BLEED – OFF. 9. Select Cross Bleed valve to OPEN. 10. For the A319/A320 Aircraft: BLEED Page – SELECT and MONITOR (throughout the flight) If the pre cooler outlet temperature of the remaining bleed exceeds 240 degrees C within 2 minutes after X BLEED valve opening: ● PACK (on the first affected bleed side) – OFF. 11. For takeoff – Perform a packs off takeoff per Volume 1 A320 5.2.2 Packs Off for Takeoff. 12. A319, A320, and A321CEO ships 3001-3018: A. In flight, in the case of failure of the remaining engine bleed air supply system, or in the case of failure of the associated engine: i. Apply the associated ECAM procedure ii. Refer to QRH/ABN-11 AIR ENG 1+2 BLEED FAULT. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

mproc: 1. Defer the associated bleed air supply system per MEL 36-11-01A, 36-11-01B or 36-11-01C as applicable. 2. Deactivate the associated bleed pressure regulator valve by: A. For the A319/A320/A321CEO, perform AMM Task 36-11-00-040-001. B. For the A321NEO, perform AMM Task 36-11-00-040-803-A. Learning Video

name: High Pressure Valves (HPV)
rem: (O) (M) One may be inoperative provided: 1. Affected HP valve is secured closed, 2. Opposite bleed air supply system is operative, and 3. On Ships 5001-5026, 5028-5032, and 5034-5035, if Pre-AA 3611-01130, the affected bleed valve (PRV) is considered inoperative and must be deferred per MEL 36-11-02. Note: This item is AD related.
oproc: 1. Comply with (O) procedure for MEL 36-11-01A, 36-11-01B or 36-11-01C as applicable. 36-11-07B Note: Pneumatic air pressure will be low with throttles at idle. Disregard ECAM messages related to low pneumatic system pressure such as AIR ENG 1(2) BLEED ABNORM PR (refer to FCOM-PRO-ABN-AIR – AIR ENG 1(2) BLEED ABNORM PR - ANNUNCIATION) 1. During flight deck preparation: A. Affected ENG 1(2) BLEED pb – OFF. B. X BLEED – AUTO. 2. After engine start, but prior to taxi: A. Affected ENG 1(2) BLEED pb – OFF. B. X BLEED – OPEN. C. One PACK pb - OFF. 3. Before takeoff and until descent: A. Affected ENG 1(2) BLEED pb – ON. B. X BLEED – AUTO. C. PACK pb - as required. 4. If wing anti ice is required : A. ONE PACK – OFF. 5. Before descent and until parked at the gate: A. Affected ENG 1(2) BLEED pb – OFF. B. X BLEED – OPEN. C. One PACK pb - OFF. 6. At the gate: A. X BLEED – AUTO. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Bleed Monitoring Computers – BMC 1

name: APU Bleed Air Supply System
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. APU BLEED switch remains OFF, and 2. An alternate air source is available for engine starts. Note: APU can be used for electrical power. Note: Two air carts required for engine start, unless a single cart is high volume output.
oproc: 1. Set APU BLEED pb switch to OFF. 2. Reference Operations Manual Volume 1 Supplementary Procedures section for APU Bleed Inoperative Engine Start procedures. If an APU Takeoff is specified on the WDR, reference Operations Manual, Volume 1, Packs Off for Takeoff.

name: APU Bleed Valve Inoperative Closed
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative deactivated in the closed position provided: 1. APU bleed switch is selected OFF, and 2. An alternate air source is available for engine starts. Note: APU can be used for electrical power. Note: Two air carts required for engine start, unless a single cart is high volume output.

name: APU Bleed Valve Inoperative Open
rem: (O) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS), may be inoperative open provided APU is not used in flight. Theater Restrictions May Apply

name: APU Bleed Valve Inoperative Open
rem: (O) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS) beyond 120 minutes, may be inoperative open provided: 1. APU is not used in flight, and 2. Repairs are made within 4 flight-legs. Theater Restrictions May Apply
oproc: 1. Set APU BLEED pb switch to OFF. 2. Reference Operations Manual Volume 1 Supplementary Procedures section for APU Bleed Inoperative Engine Start procedures. If an APU Takeoff is specified on the WDR, reference Operations Manual, Volume 1, Packs Off for Takeoff. 3. On the APU SD page, disregard the position of the APU bleed air valve. 36-12-02B, 36-12-02C 1. APU bleed air can be used for Main Engine Start (MES) or air conditioning only on GROUND. 2. Do not use APU in flight. 3. To prevent the ECAM alert AIR APU BLEED FAULT from triggering, the APU BLEED pb-sw must be selected in accordance with the APU state: A. When APU is running: i. APU BLEED pb-sw – ON B. When APU is shutdown: i. APU BLEED pb-sw – OFF CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: APU Bleed Check Valve Inoperative Closed

name: APU Bleed Check Valve Inoperative Open
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative open and APU used provided: 1. Airplane remains at or below FL200, 2. X BLEED selector is selected SHUT and ENG 1 bleed is selected OFF, and 3. Upon initial MEL application, MCC shall review the 7 day history, including Post Flight Report, of the operative pack and pneumatic bleed system associated with the operative pack to ensure no known conditions exist that could prevent an adequate supply of bleed air for cabin pressurization. Note: In this configuration, the number 2 engine bleed air will supply the right pneumatic manifold. Note: APU fuel burn will be 175 lbs per hr.

name: APU Bleed Check Valve Inoperative Open

oproc: 1. Set APU BLEED pb switch to OFF. 2. Reference Operations Manual Volume 1 Supplementary Procedures section for APU Bleed Inoperative Engine Start procedures. If an APU Takeoff is specified on the WDR, reference Operations Manual, Volume 1, Packs Off for Takeoff. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Cross Bleed Valve – Automatic Control
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided manual control operates normally.

name: Cross Bleed Valve - Manual Control (For A319, A320, and
rem: (O) (M) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS) beyond 120 minutes, may be inoperative provided: 1. Automatic control is verified to operate normally PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, 2. APU bleed is operative for engine start, 3. Aircraft is not operated in known or forecast icing conditions, 4. Both bleed air supply systems operate normally, and 5. Both air conditioning packs operate normally. Note: This item is AD related. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: During flight deck preparation: 1. X BLEED selector –OPEN. (Check on ECAM BLEED page that the crossbleed valve position indication shows open). 2. X BLEED selector –SHUT. (Check on ECAM BLEED page that the crossbleed valve position indication shows closed). 3. X BLEED selector –AUTO. 4. Prior to starting No. 2 engine, X BLEED – OPEN. 5. After No. 2 engine start, X BLEED – AS REQUIRED. 36-12-04B During flight deck preparation: 1. X-BLEED selector – AUTO
mproc: PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE Check the 1. Check that the APU is started. 2. APU BLEED p.b.-sw – OFF A. On the BLEED SD page, check that the cross bleed valve is indicated close. 3. APU BLEED p.b.-sw – ON A. On the BLEED SD page, check that the cross bleed valve is indicated open

name: Pylon Leak Detection Loops
rem: (M) One may be inoperative provided: 1. Associated pylon leak detection loop is deactivated, 2. Upon initial MEL application, MCC shall review the 7 day history, including Post Flight reports, of the operative pack and pneumatic bleed system associated with the operative pack to ensure no known conditions exist that could affect proper operation of the pressurization system, 3. If displayed during previous flight, AIR ENG 1(2) BLEED LEAK caution is confirmed to be false by troubleshooting, and 4. Affected bleed air supply system is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 36-11-01A. Note: For A321NEO use MEL 36-00-00F. Note: If speedbrakes inoperative airplane remains below FL310.

name: AIR APU BLEED LEAK Caution on ECAM EWD
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. AIR APU BLEED LEAK caution is confirmed to be false by troubleshooting, 2. APU leak detection loop is deactivated, 3. APU check valve is removed and replaced by a blanking cap, 4. APU air supply system is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 36-12-01, and 5. BMC 1 is operative.
oproc: 1. For all A319/A320 aircraft and A321CEO ships 3001-3018 after application of the (M) procedure: A. Disregard the AIR BLEED MAINTENANCE message displayed on the EWD, only if the message is due to the deactivation of the APU leak detection loop. 2. For A321CEO ships 3019-3098, 1001-1029 and all A321NEO aircraft after application of the (M) procedure: A. Disregard the AIR APU LEAK DET FAULT alert displayed on the EWD.
mproc: 1. Remove the APU check valve and install blanking cap: A. For the A319, A320, and A321CEO: i. Perform AMM Task 36-12-00-040-801-A B. For the A321NEO: i. Perform AMM Task 36-12-00-040-802-A. 2. Deactivate the APU Leak Detection Loop: A. For the A319, A320, and A321CEO: i. Perform AMM Task 36-12-00-040-808-A B. For the A321NEO: i. Perform AMM Task 36-12-00-040-820-A

name: Potable Water System Compressor
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided the compressor is deactivated. Note: Water system pressure may be supplied on the ground by operating the APU or running an engine.

name: Potable Water System Quantity Indication
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Affected quantity indication is deactivated or isolated, 2. System is verified not to have leaks, and 3. If at a station with potable water servicing capabilities, service the potable water tank PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Potable Water System Individual Components (ALL EXCEPT Lavatory Sink Drain or Faucet Fill Valve and/or Overflow Valve and Galley Drains)
rem: (M) Individual components may be inoperative provided: 1. Associated components are deactivated or isolated, and 2. Associated system components are verified not to have leaks. Note: Any portion of system which operates normally may be used. Note: Leaking lines or tank renders system inoperative.

name: Potable Water System Individual Components (Lavatory Sink Drain or Faucet)
rem: (O) (M) Lavatory Sink Drain(s) or Faucet(s) may be inoperative provided: 1. Associated components are deactivated or isolated, and 2. Associated system components are verified not to have leaks.

name: Potable Water System Fill Valve and/or Overflow Valve
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Associated system components are verified not to have leaks, and 2. The overflow valve is verified closed AFTER EACH WATER SERVICING (Log Book Entry Required). Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Potable Water System Galley Drains
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided associated galley drain is blocked.

name: Potable Water System
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Performance corrections are applied, 2. System is drained, 3. System is not serviced, and 4. Repairs are made within one flight day. PAGE

name: Potable Water System Contaminated
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Each galley potable water shut–off valve is turned to OFF, and 2. Each lavatory sink potable water is deactivated.
oproc: 1. For the A321CEO and the A321NEO, pour a small bottle/cup of water down each lavatory toilet hourly to prevent clogging. 38-10-01E PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, verify logbook entry has been made by maintenance for MEL/CDL REQUIRED FOLLOW UP ACTION as stated on the (yellow) MEL Placard placed in the logbook. Note: The above logbook entry is only required after each water servicing. 38-10-01F Captain to brief Lead Flight Attendant/Purser which galley drains are blocked. 38-10-01G Captain to brief Lead Flight Attendant/Purser: 1. To pour a small bottle/cup of water down each lavatory toilet hourly to prevent clogging. 2. Galley water and coffee makers cannot be used. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Lavatory Waste Systems (Including Wheelchair Accessible Lavatories)
rem: (O) (M) Associated lavatory system(s) may be inoperative provided: 1. Associated components are deactivated or isolated to prevent leaks, 2. The Captain will determine if flight duration is acceptable with a forward lavatory unusable, and 3. The lavatory door is locked closed and placarded ”UNSERVICEABLE–DO NOT USE.” Note: It is acceptable for other lavatories (in lieu of the forward) to be used during flight. Note: These provisions are not intended to prohibit lavatory inspections by crewmembers. Ensure lavatory door is locked closed after crewmember inspection. Note: Affected lavatory is usable by crewmembers provided the vacuum waste system is operative. Ensure lavatory door is locked closed after crewmember use.

name: Lavatory Waste Systems (Including Wheelchair Accessible Lavatories) Waste Quantity Indication System
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Indication system is deactivated or isolated, 2. Indication system components are verified not to have leaks, and 3. Lavatory waste tank is serviced PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE. Note: LIQUID LEVEL SENSOR 41MG is not deferrable. This MEL cannot be used for LIQUID LVL SNSR Faults/Failures. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Lavatory Waste Systems (Including Wheelchair Accessible Lavatories) Individual Components
rem: (M) Individual components may be inoperative provided: 1. Associated components are deactivated or isolated, 2. Associated system components are verified not to have leaks. Note: Any portion of system which operates normally may be used. PAGE

name: Lavatory Waste Systems (Including Wheelchair Accessible Lavatories) Vacuum Generator (Toilet)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Vacuum generator is deactivated, and 2. Procedures are used to only allow use of lavatories at or above 16,000 ft MSL. Note: Toilets will not flush on ground or altitudes below 16,000 ft MSL. Toilets will flush normally above 16,000 ft MSL (by differential pressure).
oproc: Captain is to ensure the lavatory door is locked closed if crewmember inspection has been performed or if the lavatory is used by a crewmember. 38-30-01D Captain to brief Lead Flight Attendant/Purser to lock lavatories below 16,000 ft and unlock lavatories above 16,000 ft.
mproc: 1. Close and lock the Lavatory Door as follows: A. Lift the LAVATORY sign on the outer face of the lavatory door. B. Move the latch under the sign until the indicator shows the door is locked (OCCUPIED). C. Check that the door will not open. 2. Place a strip of Red colored OM2 “UNSERVICEABLE – DO NOT USE” tape (or equivalent) across the lavatory door and across the toilet lid to prevent use. Note: An acceptable alternate to the OM2 tape is a section of 2 inch wide masking tape with the words “UNSERVICEABLE – DO NOT USE” printed legibly in 1 inch black letters. 38-30-01B Note: LIQUID LEVEL SENSOR 41MG is not deferrable. This MEL cannot be used for LIQUID LVL SNSR Faults/Failures. Indication system is deactivated or isolated. 1. Remove power for the liquid level transmitter (40MG) by pulling the associated circuit breaker: 2. Pull and tag the following circuit breakers: A. On A/C 3209–3250: On C/B panel 2001 VU: pull and tag circuit breaker VACU TOIL SYS–VACU–SYS location B06. B. On A/C 3251–3278 and A321 aircraft: On C/B panel 2001 VU: pull and tag circuit breaker VACU TOIL SYS–VACU–SYS location A06. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Electronic Flight Bag (EFB) Systems – Power Supply / Power Connection
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided alternate procedures are used.

name: Electronic Flight Bag (EFB) Systems - Mounting Device (Fixed Mount)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Associated EFB and hardware is stowed, secured by an alternate means, or removed from the aircraft, and 2. Alternate procedures are used.

name: Electronic Flight Bag (EFB) Systems - Mounting Device (Fixed Mount)
placard: EFB POWER CONNECTOR INOP ____ EFB MOUNT INOP ____ EFB MOUNT INOP
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Associated EFB and hardware is stowed, secured by an alternate means, or removed from the aircraft, and 2. Procedures do not require its use.
oproc: Use the EFB Power Kit to power EFB's during flight or refer to FOM Chapter 16 Section 1 for EFB battery and backup battery requirements. 46-20-01B Utilize removable PED Mount
mproc: Deactivate EFB Power Supply Connections by: 1. On the 121VU panel pull and secure PILOTS OUTLETS/115V 60HZ circuit breaker located at K23. Note: Both EFB power connections will be inoperative after pulling circuit breaker. 46-20-01B 1. Ensure that inoperative mount(s) is secured or removed from the aircraft. 2. Install removable PED mount on affected side. 46-20-01C 1. Ensure that inoperative mount(s) is secured or removed from the aircraft.
ptext: EFB POWER CONNECTOR INOP ____ EFB MOUNT INOP ____ EFB MOUNT INOP

name: Air Traffic Service Unit
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. Alternate procedures are used, 2. RNAV operations do not require ATSU use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements, and 3. Refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for PBCS Equipment Requirements. Note: ACARS and Datalink Functions will be inoperative. Note: Any ATSU function or mode which operates normally may be used. Note: If an Engine Performance Report Form is not available and MCC determines that there is no requirement for ENCON readings during that flight segment, the Engine Performance Report Form may be boarded at the destination. It is desired to have the ENCON data available once per flight day. Note: Refer to Airway Manual, Communications, Dispatch Communication, Flight Watch Requirements, ACARS Inoperative Reporting Procedures and Mandatory Company Reports. Note: For 3209-3270, refer to MEL item 23-24-01A.

name: ATSU Chime (All A319 and A321 A/C)
rem: (O) May be inoperative. Note: For 3209-3270, refer to MEL item 23-24-01B

name: ATSU Message Storage (All A319 and A321 A/C)
rem: (O) May be inoperative. Note: For 3209-3270, refer to MEL item 23-24-01C

name: ATSU UTC Time Reset (All A319 and A321 A/C)
rem: (O) May be inoperative. Note: For 3209-3270, refer to MEL item 23-24-01D

name: ATSU Out, Off, On, In, Times Function (All A319 and A321 A/C)
rem: (O) May be inoperative. Note: For 3209-3270, refer to MEL item 23-24-01E PAGE

name: ATSU Weight Data Page (All A319 and A321 A/C)
rem: (O) May be inoperative. Note: For 3209-3270, refer to MEL item 23-24-01A
oproc: 1. Prior to departure, advise departing station that ATSU is inoperative. 2. Ensure receipt of a paper FSR or copy of the EFSR prior to pushback. Reference FOM - Flight Ops Manual 14.4.1 Fuel Service Record (FSR)/Electronic Fuel Service Record (EFSR). 3. Crews should obtain a hard copy of the WDR prior to pushback. 4. Report Out, Off, On, and In times to station by radio communication. 5. If takeoff data is required after pushback, contact the dispatcher using the procedures in FOM - Flight Ops Manual 14.7.4 WDR Delivery Off the Gate with Network Outage/ACARS on MEL. 6. Operational information is to be communicated by voice radio. 7. Make enroute company reports as required. Utilize SELCAL by tuning VHF 2(3) to the appropriate Delta radio network frequency. If SELCAL is not available, continuously monitor appropriate company voice radio frequencies. Note: Company frequency must be monitored rather than 121.5. Note: Monitoring company frequency is not required on a PRM approach. A. Refer to Airway Manual 6.1.2 Communication Equipment Requirements Chart for ACARS inoperative requirements and company radio monitoring and company position reporting procedures. B. FANS (CPDLC/ADS-C) may be inoperative not allowing PBCS. 8. Engine performance data must be manually entered onto Engine Performance Report Forms. Refer to Volume 1 A320 5.5.8 Engine Condition Monitoring (ECM) Recording. 9. Ensure that a minimum of ten (10) Engine Performance Report Forms (OM-851) are installed in the aircraft logbook. If not, contact MCC. (forms can be obtained from the TechOps Home Page) Note: If an Engine Performance Report Form is not available and MCC determines that there is no requirement for ENCON readings during that flight segment, the Engine Performance Report Form may be boarded at the destination. It is desired to have the ENCON data available once per flight day. 10. Flight Duty Period (FDP) Limit: A. Utilize LATT Worksheet or Contact Flight Control to determine LATT/FDP Limit and if FDP extension is required to complete to the flight. Pilots must monitor FDP Limit prior to OFF event. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Data-Link Control Display Units (DCDU)
rem: One may be inoperative.

name: Data-Link Control Display Units (DCDU)
rem: (O) Both may be inoperative provided: 1. CPDLC is not required for operation, 2. Use voice radio procedures for position reporting, 3. Voice radio procedures are used to request departure clearance, 4. RNAV operations do not require DCDU use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements, and 5. Refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for PBCS Equipment Requirements.
oproc: 1. CPDLC Operations – Not Authorized. 2. Utilize HF, or VHF Voice Communication. 3. ADS-C and CPDLC are not available. ATC position reporting needs to be accomplished via voice radio. Departure clearances can be obtained via ACARS PDC or radio.

name: ATC Msg. Pb
rem: One may be inoperative.

name: ATC Msg Pb
rem: (O) Both may be inoperative provided: 1. CPDLC is not required for operation, 2. Use voice radio procedures for position reporting, 3. Voice radio procedures are used to request departure clearance, 4. RNAV operations do not require its use,refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements,. and 5. Refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for PBCS Equipment Requirements.

name: ATC MSG Lights
placard: ____ ATC MSG PB INOP BOTH ATC MSG PB INOP ____ ATC MSG PB INOP
rem: One or both may be inoperative.
oproc: 1. CPDLC Operations – Not Authorized. 2. Utilize HF, or VHF Voice Communication. 3. ADS-C and CPDLC are not available. Position reporting and departure clearances need to be accomplished via voice radio.
ptext: ____ ATC MSG PB INOP BOTH ATC MSG PB INOP ____ ATC MSG PB INOP

name: ATC Datalink (A321CEO and A321NEO)
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. CPDLC is not required for operation, 2. Use voice radio procedures for position reporting, 3. Voice radio procedures are used to request departure clearance, 4. RNAV operations do not require ATC Datalink use, refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for RNAV Minimum Requirements, and 5. Refer to Navigation Equipment Tab (this manual) for PBCS Equipment Requirements. Note: Any ATC Datalink function or mode which operates normally may be used.
oproc: 1. CPDLC Operations – Not Authorized. 2. Utilize HF, or VHF Voice Communication. 3. ADS-C and CPDLC are not available. Position reporting and departure clearances need to be accomplished via voice radio. 4. For more information on aircraft capability on ATC datalink communication, refer to Airway Manual, 6.3, ATC Communications.

name: COMPANY Datalink
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided alternate procedures in Airway Manual, Dispatch Communication are used. Note: Any COMPANY Datalink function or mode which operates normally may be used.
oproc: Use procedures in Airway Manual, 6.4.2.1, Dispatch Communications.

name: Flight Operations and Maintenance Exchanger
rem: May be inoperative provided procedures do not require its use.

name: PBCS Monitored Performance
rem: Aircraft is not PBCS approved due to poor monitored performance. Do not file flight plan using P2 or SUR/RSP180 ICAO codes. FANS (CPDLC/ ADS-C) functionality is normal and can be used.

name: Fault(s) Indicated by FUEL
rem: May be displayed provided repairs are made within twenty flight days. Note: Dispatch with associated MAINT STS message displayed on ECAM is permitted without CFDS this interrogation.

name: Fuel Tank Inerting System
rem: May be inoperative provided repairs are made within twenty flight days.

name: Fault(s) Indicated by APU
rem: Note: Dispatch with this maintenance status message displayed on ECAM is permitted without CFDS interrogation.

name: APU System
rem: (O) (M) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS), may be inoperative provided the APU is not used. Note: Two air carts required for engine start, unless a single cart is high volume output. Theater Restrictions May Apply

name: APU System
rem: (O) (M) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS) beyond 120 minutes, may be inoperative for four flights.

name: APU System Pneumatic
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. APU BLEED switch remains OFF, and 2. An alternate air source is available for engine starts. Note: APU can be used for electrical power. Note: Two air carts required for engine start, unless a single cart is high volume output.

name: APU System – Cold Soak Not Verified
rem: (O) Inflight verification of cold soak start capability has not been accomplished. Extended Operations (ETOPS) beyond 120 minutes Not Authorized: 1. APU remains OFF after takeoff until cold soak start is attempted, 2. Cold soak inflight start of APU may be accomplished after at least two hours at or above FL 270, and 3. Verification has been accomplished with in 6 landings. Note: APU is run for a minimum of five minutes. Fuel burn is negligible.

name: APU System – Cold Soak Not Verified
rem: (O) (M) Inflight verification of cold soak start capability has not been accomplished. Extended Operations (ETOPS) Authorized: 1. The APU oil quantity is checked, 2. APU is started on the ground, 3. APU remains operating until 2 hours prior to top of descent, 4. A cold soak APU start will be accomplished, 5. Authorization limited to one flight, and 6. Performance corrections are applied. PAGE

name: APU Air Intake Flap (Secured Open)
rem: (M) May be inoperative secured open and APU used provided repairs are made within 10 flight days.

name: APU Air Intake Flap (Closed or partially closed)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative closed or partially closed provided APU is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 49-10-01A or 49-10-01B as applicable. Note: Two air carts required for engine start, unless a single cart is high volume output. Theater Restrictions May Apply
oproc: 1. Do not use APU. 2. APU MASTER switch – OFF. 3. External electrical and pneumatic power are required for normal starting. 4. Reference Operations Manual Volume 1 Supplementary Section for APU Inoperative (Bleed and Generator) Engine Start procedures. If an APU Takeoff is specified on the WDR, reference Operations Manual, Volume 1, Packs Off for Takeoff.
mproc: 1. To deactivate APU Air Intake Flap in OPEN position: A. For A320 aircraft with the 36-300 APU perform AMM Task 49-16-00-040-001-A. B. For all A319 aircraft, all A321 aircraft, and A320 aircraft with the 131-9A APU perform AMM Task 49-16-00-040-003. Learning Video 49-10-02B 1. Defer the APU per MEL 49-10-01A or 49-10-01B as applicable.

name: APU Fuel Pump
rem: (O) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS), may be inoperative provided both engine driven generators are operative. Note: APU may be started using any AC boost pump feeding the left fuel manifold. Note: AC power must be available on the airplane to operate the boost pump(s).

name: APU Fuel Pump
rem: (O) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS) beyond 120 minutes, may be inoperative provided repairs are made within four flights. Note: APU may be started using A.C. boost pump feeding the left fuel manifold.
oproc: Pressurize Left Fuel Manifold with at least one fuel tank pump prior to APU start.

name: APU LP Valve
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative secured closed provided APU is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 49-10-01A or 49-10-01B as applicable. Note: Two air carts required for engine start, unless a single cart is high volume output. Theater Restrictions May Apply

name: APU LP Valve
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative in closed position provided: 1. Valve is indicated closed on ECAM, and 2. APU is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 49-10-01A or 49-10-01B as applicable. Note: Two air carts required for engine start, unless a single cart is high volume output. Theater Restrictions May Apply
oproc: 1. Do not use APU. 2. APU MASTER switch – OFF. 3. External electrical and pneumatic power are required for normal starting. 4. Reference Operations Manual Volume 1 Supplementary Section for APU Inoperative (Bleed and Generator) Engine Start procedures 5. If an APU takeoff is specified on the WDR: A. For takeoff – switch off both packs. B. At the THR RED altitude, place PACK 1 pb to ON. C. When the flaps are selected up, place PACK 2 pb to ON Note: Expect to receive PACK 1(2) OFF ECAM message after T.O. INHIBIT is removed at 1,500 feet AFE. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: MASTER Switch ON Light
rem: May be inoperative.

name: MASTER Switch FAULT Light
rem: May be inoperative provided N and EGT indications are available on ECAM APU Page

name: START ON Light
rem: May be inoperative.

name: START/AVAIL Light
rem: May be inoperative provided N indication is available on ECAM APU Page.

name: APU Indications
rem: May be inoperative provided START/AVAIL Light and MASTER Switch FAULT Light are operative.

name: APU GEN Parameters
rem: Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS), may be inoperative provided both Engine Driven Generators are operative.

name: LOW OIL LEVEL
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Oil level is verified BEFORE EACH REFUELING (Log Book Entry Required), and 2. There is no evidence of abnormal consumption or leakage. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
mproc: 1. BEFORE EACH REFUELING: A. Oil level is verified before each refueling per the following: i. Config 1 – (APU 36–300) a. Task # 49–90–00–600–001 ii. Config 2 – (APU 131–9A) a. Task # 49–90–00–600–007

name: Emergency Operation Cylinder Damper Functions (Damper Functions Only)
rem: Any or all may be inoperative.

name: Cargo Door Actuators
rem: (M) One per door may be inoperative provided: 1. Integrity of Yellow hydraulic system is not affected, 2. Wind velocity does not exceed 30 knots, and 3. Operation of door with a single actuator is limited to 75 landings.

name: Cargo Door Actuators
rem: (M) One per door may be inoperative provided: 1. Affected actuator is deactivated, 2. Integrity of Yellow hydraulic system is not affected, 3. Wind velocity does not exceed 30 knots, and 4. Operation of door with a single actuator is limited to 75 landings.

name: Cargo Door Actuators
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Integrity of Yellow hydraulic system is not affected, and 2. Door(s) is manually closed and locked.
mproc: Leak Check of the Door Hydraulic System: 1. Turn the selector of the service panel to the OPEN position and hold it for three seconds to bleed the door actuator. 2. Remove the support struts from the cargo door structure and the door frame of the fuselage. 3. Turn the selector of the service panel to the CLOSE position and hold it until the cargo door moves down. 4. Open and close the cargo door three times. Make sure that the cargo door is fully opened. 5. Do a visual inspection of the door actuators, the pipes, the hoses and the related valves. Make sure that there are no leaks. Note: If necessary, disconnect the hydraulic pipes from the defective components and attach correct pressure plugs to the open ends. 6. Examine the Yellow reservoir level on the HYD page of the lower display unit of the ECAM system. 7. If necessary, fill the Yellow reservoir to its normal fill level 52-30-01B, 52-30-01C 1. Perform AMM Task 52-36-00-040-001-A.

name: Hand Pump
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided the integrity of Yellow hydraulic System is not affected.
mproc: Check the Hand Pump for leaks. If leaking perform the following procedure. If no leaks found apply MEL, no further action required: 1. Open the access panel 198CB to get access to the ground service panel of the Yellow hydraulic system. 2. Remove the access panel 197AB to get access to the hydraulic connections of the hand pump. 3. Pull and tag the following circuit breakers: 4. On A/C 3209–3238 ● On C/B panel 123VU: pull and tag circuit breaker Y HYD/ELEC PUMP/NORM location AB03. 5. On A/C 3239–3278, all A319 aircraft, all A321 aircraft ● On C/B panel 123VU: pull and tag circuit breaker Y HYD/ELEC/ELEC PUMP/NORM location AB03. 6. On ALL A/C ● On C/B panel 123VU: pull and tag circuit breaker Y HYD/ELEC/PUMP location AB06. ● On C/B panel 121VU: pull and tag circuit breaker HYDRAULIC/HYD POWER/Y location N30. ● On C/B panel 122VU: pull and tag circuit breaker DOORS/CARGO location T12. 7. Depressurize the Yellow hydraulic system. 8. Depressurize the reservoir of the Yellow hydraulic system. 9. Place a Warning Tag on the cockpit overhead panel 40VU to prevent operation of the Yellow hydraulic system. 10. Place a Warning Tag on the Ground Service Panel to prevent the operation of the yellow hydraulic system. 11. Set the PTU P/B SW on the overhead panel 40VU to prevent unwanted operation of the power transfer unit. 12. Put a spillage container below the hydraulic connections of the hand pump to collect the hydraulic fluid 13. Disconnect the pressure pipe from the port A of the hand pump and install a pressure plug on the open end of the pipe. 14. Disconnect the suction pipe from the port C of the hand pump and install a pressure plug on the open end of the pipe. 15. Install blanking plugs on the ports A and C of the hand pump. 16. Energize the aircraft electrical circuits. 17. Remove tags and close the following circuit breakers: 18. On A/C 3209–3238 ● On C/B panel 123VU: remove tag and close circuit breaker Y HYD/ELEC PUMP/NORM location AB03. 19. On A/C 3239–3278, all A319 aircraft, all A321 aircraft ● On C/B panel 123VU: remove tag and close circuit breaker Y HYD/ELEC/ELEC PUMP/NORM location AB03. 20. On ALL A/C ● On C/B panel 123VU: remove tag and close circuit breaker Y HYD/ELEC/PUMP location AB06. ● On C/B panel 121VU: remove tag and close circuit breaker HYDRAULIC/HYD POWER/Y location N30. ● On C/B panel 122VU: remove tag and close circuit breaker DOORS/CARGO location T12. 21. Remove warning tags from the overhead panel and the ground service panel. 22. Pressurize the reservoir of the Yellow hydraulic system. 23. Pressurize the Yellow hydraulic system. 24. Set the ECAM system to ON and push the HYD p.b. sw. on the ECAM control panel so that the ECAM lower display unit shows the HYD page. 25. Check the hydraulic system of the FWD or AFT cargo door for leaks: A. Open and close the FWD or AFT cargo door three times to bleed the hydraulic system. B. Make sure that there are no leaks at the suction and pressure pipe ends (visual inspection). C. Examine the Yellow reservoir level on the HYD page of the ECAM lower display unit. If necessary, fill the Yellow reservoir to its normal level. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Cargo Door Opening Electrical Control System
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided the affected cargo door(s) is verified closed on ECAM door page PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE. Note: Hand pump may be used to operate cargo door(s). Ensure cargo door(s) are fully open during loading and unloading. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Cargo Door Opening Electrical Control System
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Hand pump operates normally, and 2. Cargo doors are fully open during loading and unloading. Note: Two people will be required to operate cargo doors. Note: Any cargo door control function that operates normally may be used.

name: Cargo Door Opening Electrical Control System Open and Locked (Green) Lights
placard: OPENING ELECTRICAL CONTROL SYSTEM INOP OPENING ELECTRICAL CONTROL SYSTEM INOP OPEN AND LOCK GREEN LIGHT INOP
rem: (O) May be inoperative and associated cargo compartment used provided cargo door is fully open during loading and unloading.
oproc: 1. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE, Captain is to ensure that affected cargo door(s) is verified closed on the ECAM door page. 2. When cargo door operation is required, the yellow hydraulic system may be pressurized from the cockpit using the following procedure: A. On the 122VU panel remove the collar and reset the DOORS/CARGO circuit breaker located at T12. B. PTU p.b.-sw – OFF. C. Yellow Hyd Pump – ON. D. Operate Door E. Yellow Hyd Pump – OFF. F. PTU p.b.-sw – AUTO. G. On the 122VU panel, pull and secure the DOORS/CARGO circuit breaker located at T12. 52-30-04C When a cargo door Open and Locked (Green) Light is inoperative, it is necessary to inform ground personnel: 1. To visually check that the affected cargo door is in the fully open position, with no downward movement after releasing the lever of the manual selector valve. 2. To visually check that the lever of the manual selector valve stays in the neutral position CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE
ptext: OPENING ELECTRICAL CONTROL SYSTEM INOP OPENING ELECTRICAL CONTROL SYSTEM INOP OPEN AND LOCK GREEN LIGHT INOP

name: Cargo Door Drift Pin
rem: May be inoperative provided repairs are made within 1 flight.

name: Cockpit Door Locking System
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Automatic locking system is deactivated, 2. Door dead bolt operates normally and is used to lock the door, 3. Alternate procedures are used for locking and unlocking the door using the dead bolt, and 4. Repair are made within two flight days.

name: Cockpit Door Toggle Switch (UNLOCK Function)
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided alternate procedures are used.

name: Cockpit Door Toggle Switch (LOCK Function)
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. The cockpit door locking system keypad is deactivated, and 2. Alternate procedures are used.

name: OPEN Light
rem: May be inoperative.

name: FAULT Light
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided all LEDs on CKPT DOOR CONT Panel are operative.

name: Buzzer
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. The cockpit door locking system keypad is deactivated, and 2. Alternate procedures are used.

name: Keypad
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. The cockpit door locking system keypad is deactivated, and 2. Alternate procedures are used.

name: Keypad Green and Red LED’s
rem: (O) One or both may be inoperative provided alternate procedures are used.

name: Pressure Rate Sensors
rem: One may be inoperative.

name: Pressure Rate Sensors
rem: Both may be inoperative provided repairs are made within two flight days.

name: Door Release Strike (Catch Spring, Solenoid, Bolt)
rem: One may be inoperative provided the associated door release strike is failed in the open/unlock position. Note: Disregard the associated OPEN light if illuminated.

name: Door Release Strike (Catch Spring, Solenoid, Bolt)
rem: (M) One may be inoperative provided associated door release strike is deactivated or removed. Note: If center strike is affected, the OPEN Light will be Inoperative.

name: Control Unit LED’s
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided associated FAULT light is verified to operate normally. PAGE

name: Cockpit Door Secondary Locking System (Deadbolt)
rem: May be inoperative provided automatic lock controls operate normally.

name: Installed Physical Secondary Barrier (IPSB)
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. IPSB is secured in the fully stowed position in accordance with standard operating procedures, and 2. Alternate procedures are used.

name: Installed Physical Secondary Barrier (IPSB)
placard: INOP AND STOWED OPEN IPSB INOP AND REMOVED
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. IPSB is removed, and 2. Alternate procedures are used.
oproc: Captain will brief Lead Flight Attendant / Purser to utilize "Opening Flight Deck door without a Barrier" procedure. Refer to On Board Manual.
mproc: Remove the IPSB per the following procedure (if needed, reference CMM 25-26-01, Disassembly): Note: The disassembly procedure assumes the IPSB is in the stowage position. 1. Detach Door Leaves A. Release the stowage latch. B. Release the Emergency Release Mechanism from the upper hinge fitting i. Remove the cover via the snap lock. ii. Push out the cover on the opposite side. iii. Turn the finger wheel counterclockwise. iv. Move the pin 90° and the pin push down. v. Disengage the Emergency Release Mechanism from the upper hinge fitting. C. Disengage the IPSB from the lower hinge fitting i. Lift the door assembly upwards to disengage from the lower hinge fitting D. Remove the IPSB.
ptext: INOP AND STOWED OPEN IPSB INOP AND REMOVED

name: Passenger Doors/Cabin Emergency Exit - Open Indication
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE a visual check is made to ensure that the affected door(s) is closed and locked, and 2. White SLIDE ARMED light on the affected door does not illuminate when the door is closed and locked and slide armed. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Passenger Doors/Cabin Emergency Exit - Open Indication
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE a visual check is made to ensure that the affected door(s) is closed and locked, and 2. White SLIDE ARMED light on the affected door remains illuminated when the door is closed and locked and slide armed. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Passenger Doors/Cabin Emergency Exit - Open Indication
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. Aircraft is limited to Day operations only, and 2. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE a visual check is made ensure that the affected door(s) is closed and locked. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Passenger Doors/Cabin Emergency Exit - Closed Indication
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. Aircraft is limited to Day operations only, and 2. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE a visual check is made ensure that the affected door(s) is closed and locked. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Overwing Emergency Exits - Open Indication
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE a visual check is made and the door is pushed against to ensure that the affected overwing emergency exit is closed and locked, and 2. SLIDE Indication on ECAM DOOR page illuminates white when the overwing emergency exits are closed and locked. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard) PAGE

name: Overwing Emergency Exits - Open Indication
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Aircraft is limited to Day operations only, and 2. A visual check is made and the door is pushed against PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE to ensure that the affected overwing emergency exit is closed and locked. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Overwing Emergency Exits - Closed Indication
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Aircraft is limited to Day operations only, and 2. A visual check is made and the door is pushed against PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE to ensure that the affected overwing emergency exit is closed and locked. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: FWD Cargo Door Without an LGCIU fault and associated Thrust Reverser message
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided a visual check is made PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE to confirm that affected door is closed and locked. Note: Out, off, and in times must be reported using free text downlinks or by radio communication. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: FWD Cargo Door Accompanied by LGCIU 1 Fault and associated Thrust Reverser message
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. A visual check is made PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE to confirm that affected door is closed and locked, 2. GPWS is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 34-48-01A, 3. Associated Thrust Reverser is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 78-30-01A, or 78-30-01B as applicable, 4. KJAC (Jackson Hole Airport) operations – Authorized on dry runways only, and 5. Repairs are made within one flight day. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: FWD Cargo Door accompanied by LGCIU 2 Fault and associated Thrust Reverser message
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. A visual check is made PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE to confirm that affected door is closed and locked, 2. KJAC (Jackson Hole Airport) operations – Authorized on dry runways only, 3. Associated Thrust Reverser is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 78-30-01A, or 78-30-01B as applicable, and 4. Repairs are made within one flight day. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard) PAGE

name: AFT Cargo Door Without an LGCIU fault and associated Thrust Reverser message
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. A visual check is made PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE to confirm that affected door is closed and locked, 2. Aft cargo compartment heating system is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 21-43-02A or 21-43-02B as applicable, and 3. No live animals or temperature sensitive cargo are carried in the AFT Cargo Compartment. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: AFT Cargo Door accompanied by LGCIU Fault and associated Thrust Reverser message
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. A visual check is made PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE to confirm that affected door is closed and locked, 2. Aft cargo compartment heating system is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 21-43-02A or 21-43-02B as applicable, 3. No live animals or temperature sensitive cargo are carried in the AFT Cargo Compartment, 4. GPWS is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 34-48-01A, 5. KJAC (Jackson Hole Airport) operations – Authorized on dry runways only, 6. Associated Thrust Reverser is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 78-30-01A, or 78-30-01B as applicable, and 7. Repairs are made within one flight day. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: AFT Cargo Door accompanied by LGCIU 2 Fault and associated Thrust Reverser message
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. A visual check is made PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE to confirm that affected door is closed and locked, 2. Aft cargo compartment heating system is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 21-43-02A or 21-43-02B, 3. No live animals or temperature sensitive cargo are carried in the AFT Cargo Compartment, 4. KJAC (Jackson Hole Airport) operations – Authorized on dry runways only, 5. Associated Thrust Reverser is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 78-30-01A, or 78-30-01B as applicable, and 6. Repairs are made within one flight day. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard) PAGE

name: Avionics Compartment Access Door
rem: (O) (M) Indications may be inoperative provided a visual check is made PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE to confirm that doors are closed and locked, to include pushing against inward opening access doors. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Bulk Cargo Compartment Access Door
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. A visual check is made and door is pushed against PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE to confirm that affected door is closed and locked, 2. Aft cargo compartment heating system is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 21-43-02A or 21-43-02B as applicable, and 3. No live animals or temperature sensitive cargo are carried in the AFT Cargo Compartment. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: 1. PRIOR TO ENGINE START, ensure that the associated door/exit is closed and locked using the following procedure: A. Make sure that door LOCKED indicator is displayed on the affected door/exit. B. Make sure the SLIDE ARMED light on the affected door/exit does not illuminate when the door is closed and locked and slide armed. 2. After engine start, clear the door/exit by depressing the EMER CANC pb. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: CABIN PRESSURE Light (On Doors)
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided absence of differential pressure is confirmed before opening of associated door.
oproc: 1. Ensure the agent does not open the door prior to receipt of the Flight Attendant signal. 2. Captain to brief Lead Flight Attendant/Purser to: A. Check with flight deck crew to ensure cabin is unpressurized before a passenger door is opened. OR B. Flight Attendant may check another door to ensure cabin is depressurized prior to opening the affected door(s).

name: Door Residual Differential Pressure Detection
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided: 1. All CABIN PRESSURE lights on cabin doors are placarded inoperative, and 2. Absence of differential pressure is confirmed before opening of any cabin door.
oproc: 1. Ensure the agent does not open the door prior to receipt of the Flight Attendant signal. 2. Captain to brief Lead Flight Attendant/Purser to check with flight deck crew to ensure cabin is unpressurized before a passenger door is opened.

name: RVSM Status
rem: (M) Authorized by MCC when: 1. RVSM operations cannot be used, 2. No specific MEL item applies, and 3. The aircraft must remain at or below FL280. Note: This SYS/SEQ number is to be used for deferring the RVSM Status of the aircraft. This number also requires Maintenance Control’s authorization prior to its usage or removal. RVSM Not Authorized
mproc: 1. Create a new log page in the aircraft logbook using the appropriate MEL Item Number (contact MCC for instructions). 2. The deferral code VSM must also be entered on the log page and the placard when deferring this item. 3. MCC authorization is required prior to its usage or removal. 4. RVSM operations – Not Authorized. Limit flight altitude to FL 280 or below.

name: Front Windshields (PPG Windshields and PAL Windshields) (PPG Windshields and PAL Windshields) (Only P/N PAL 06282 & 06283 and SPS Windshields except P/N STA320–1–3–1 and STA320–2–3–1)
rem: (O) (M) One front windshield outer ply may be cracked provided: 1. Windshield crack must be within the limits described in AMM Task 56-11-11-200-001, 2. Aircraft is not operated in known or forecast structural icing conditions, 3. Other front windshield is not damaged, 4. Affected windshield heating system is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 30-42-03, and 5. Repairs are made within ten landings.

name: Front Windshields (SPS Windshields) (P/N STA320–1–3–1 and
rem: (O) (M) One front windshield outer ply may be cracked provided: 1. Windshield crack must be within the limits described in AMM Task 56-11-11-200-001, 2. Aircraft is not operated in known or forecast structural icing conditions, 3. Other front windshield is not damaged, 4. Affected windshield heating system is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 30-42-03, and 5. Repairs are made within one landing, and
oproc: 1. Ensure procedures for MEL 30-42-03 are followed. Note: Delamination and/or shattering of the outer layer ply does not affect structural integrity of the window
mproc: 1. Perform AMM Task 56-10-00-040-001. 2. Defer the affected windshield heat system per MEL 30-42-03. 3. If the windshield outer ply has cracks or if the windshield has burn spots that are equal to or more than the acceptable limit, deactivate the anti-ice of the defective windshield. To deactivate the anti-ice of the defective windshield open and collar the following circuit breakers: For the Left Side ● On C/B panel 123VU: circuit breaker ANTI ICE/L/WHSLD location AF10. For the Right Side ● On C/B panel 123VU: circuit breaker ANTI ICE/R/WHSLD location AF03.

name: Fixed Lateral Windows (Aircraft with Glass Windows) (SPS Supplier P/N SPS... and PPG Supplier P/N NP...)
rem: (M) One fixed lateral window outer ply may be cracked provided: 1. Windshield crack must be within the limits described in AMM Task 56-11-12-200-001, 2. Other fixed lateral window is not damaged, 3. Affected window heating system is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 30-42-02, and 4. Repairs are made within one landing.

name: Fixed Lateral Windows (Aircraft with Acrylic windows) (LUCAS/ACT Supplier P/N
rem: (M) May be damaged within the limits described in AMM Task 56-11-12-200-001.
mproc: 1. Perform AMM Task 56-10-00-040-002. 2. Defer the window heat system per MEL 30-42-02. 3. f the fixed window has burn spots that are equal to or more than the acceptable limit, deactivate the anti-ice of the defective window. To deactivate the anti-ice of the defective window pull and secure the following circuit breaker: A. For the Left Side: i. On C/B panel 123VU: circuit breaker ANTI ICE/WINDOWS/L location X14. B. For the Right Side: i. On C /B panel 123VU: circuit breaker ANTI ICE/WINDOWS/R location W14. 56-11-02B 1. Perform AMM Task 56-10-00-040-002 2. If the fixed window has burn spots that are equal to or more than the acceptable limit, deactivate the anti-ice of the defective window. To deactivate the anti-ice of the defective window pull and secure the following circuit breaker: A. For the Left Side: i. On C/B panel 123VU: circuit breaker ANTI ICE/WINDOWS/L location X14. B. For the Right Side: i. On C /B panel 123VU: circuit breaker ANTI ICE/WINDOWS/R location W14.

name: Sliding Windows (Aircraft with Glass Windows) (SPS Supplier P/N SPS... and PPG Supplier P/N NP...)
rem: (M) One sliding window outer ply may be cracked provided: 1. Windshield crack must be within the limits described in AMM Task 56-12-11-200-001-A, 2. Other sliding window is not damaged, 3. Affected window heating system is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 30-42-02, and 4. Repairs are made within one landing.

name: Sliding Windows (Aircraft with Acrylic windows) (LUCAS/ACT Supplier P/N
rem: (M) May be damaged within the limits described in AMM Task 56-12-11-200-001-A.
mproc: 1. Perform AMM Task 56-10-00-040-002. 2. For MEL 56-12-01A, defer the window heat system per MEL 30-42-02. 3. If the fixed window has burn spots that are equal to or more than the acceptable limit, deactivate the anti-ice of the defective window. To deactivate the anti-ice of the defective window pull and secure the following circuit breaker: For the Left Side ● On C/B panel 123VU: circuit breaker ANTI ICE/WINDOWS/L location X14. For the Right Side ● On C/B panel 123VU: circuit breaker ANTI ICE/WINDOWS/R location W14.

rem: May be inoperative provided repairs are made within 10 days. Note: Dispatch with maintenance status message displayed on ECAM is permitted without CFDS interrogation.

name: ENG 1(2) FAN COWL NOT CLSD Alert
placard: FAN COWL NOT CLSD ALERT INOP
rem: (O) May be inoperative provided associated fan cowl door latches are checked closed PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE. Note: This item is AD related. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE 1. Ensure associated engine fan cowl latches are closed.
ptext: FAN COWL NOT CLSD ALERT INOP

name: Fan Cowl Latch Lock Keys
rem: (M) Item may be used to track replacement of keys for up to 10 calendar days. Note: This item is AD related.
mproc: With one or both Fan Cowl Latch Keys missing: 1. Dispatcher must be contacted. 2. MCC must create a CAR to set up replacement of key assembly at the next maintenance opportunity. 3. The missing key(s) shall be replaced within 10 (ten) calendar days, and this limit cannot be extended (Reference AD 2018-05-04).

name: Fuel Return-to-Tank Valves
rem: One may be inoperative in closed position provided APU generator is operative.

name: Fuel Recirculation System Valves (Closed)
rem: One may be inoperative in the closed position on one engine provided the APU generator is operative. Note: For A319 and A320 aircraft with the CFM 56-5A engine use MEL 71-00-00A. Note: AMM Task 73-11-50-360-002-A can be used to determine valve position if needed.

name: Fuel Recirculation System Valves (Open)
rem: One or both valves may be inoperative in the open position provided that the four wing tank pumps are operative. Note: For A319 and A320 aircraft with the CFM 56-5A engine use MEL 71-00-00A. Note: AMM Task 73-11-50-360-002-A can be used to determine valve position if needed.

rem: (O) May be inoperative provided performance corrections are applied.

rem: (O) May be inoperative provided performance corrections are applied.

rem: (O) May be inoperative provided performance corrections are applied.

name: Flex Temp Function
rem: One or both may be inoperative provided takeoff is performed in TOGA Mode.

name: Engine Overthrust Protection System
rem: One may be inoperative for six flights.

name: Fuel Used Indications
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Associated tank fuel quantity indication system operates normally, and 2. Fuel–On–Board indication operates normally.

name: Fuel Used Indications
placard: FUEL USED INDICATION INOP FUEL USED INDICATION INOP
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. Associated tank fuel quantity indication system operates normally, 2. Fuel–On–Board indication operates normally, and 3. FUEL USED 1+2 indication is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 28-41-06S.
mproc: 1. Gain access to the SYSTEM REPORT/TEST MENU on the Multipurpose Control Display Unit (MCDU). 2. Push the Line Select Keys adjacent to the following menu items on the MCDU screen: A. FUEL B. SYSTEM STATUS C. CH 1 FQIS STATUS 3. ’NO FAULTS’ must appear for each of the above selections on the MCDU. 4. For A321 aircraft defer FUEL USED 1+2 per MEL 28-41-06S.
ptext: FUEL USED INDICATION INOP FUEL USED INDICATION INOP

name: Fuel Filter Clog Cautions on
rem: (M) One may be inoperative provided associated filter is changed before the next flight and then ONCE EACH FLIGHT DAY (Log Book Entry Required). Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Fuel Filter Clog Cautions on
placard: FILTER CLOG CAUTION INOP FILTER CLOG CAUTION INOP
rem: (M) One may be inoperative provided: 1. Associated filter is changed before the next flight and then EVERY 50 FLIGHT HOURS (Log Book Entry Required), 2. ENG 1(2) FUEL FILTER PARTLY CLOGGED caution is not displayed on the EWD for the opposite engine, and 3. ENG 1(2) FUEL SENSOR FAULT caution is not displayed on the EWD for the opposite engine. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
mproc: 1. Replace the associated fuel filter per AMM Task 73-30-00-040-043. 2. ONCE EACH FLIGHT DAY: Replace the associated fuel filter per AMM Task 73-30-00-040-043. Figure 73-1: A321CEO Fuel Filter CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

rem: (M) One may be inoperative provided: 1. Associated filter is replaced before the next flight and then EVERY 50 FLIGHT HOURS (Log Book Entry Required), 2. ENG 1(2) FUEL FILTER CLOG caution is not displayed on the EWD for the opposite engine, and 3. ENG 1(2) FUEL SENSOR FAULT caution is not displayed on the EWD for the opposite engine.
