rem: (M) One may be inoperative provided: 1. Associated FUEL FILTER SENSOR subtitle is displayed on the EWD, and 2. Associated filter is replaced before the next flight and then EVERY 50 FLIGHT HOURS (Log Book Entry Required), Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: FUEL SENSOR FAULT Cautions on ECAM EWD
rem: One may be inoperative provided: 1. Associated FUEL FILTER SENSOR subtitle is displayed on EWD, 2. ENG 1(2) FUEL FILTER PARTLY CLOGGED caution is not displayed on EWD for opposite engine, and 3. Repairs are made within 8 flights or 16 flight- hours, whichever occurs first.

name: FUEL SENSOR FAULT Cautions on ECAM EWD
placard: SENSOR CAUTION ON ECAM INOP SENSOR CAUTION ON ECAM INOP SENSOR CAUTION ON ECAM INOP
rem: May be inoperative provided associated IDG FOC SENSOR subtitle is displayed on EWD.
mproc: 1. Replace the associated fuel filter per AMM Task 73-34-00-040-802-A. 2. REPLACE ASSOCIATED FUEL FILTER EVERY 50 HOURS: A. Replace the associated fuel filter per AMM Task 73-34-00-040-802-A. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE
ptext: SENSOR CAUTION ON ECAM INOP SENSOR CAUTION ON ECAM INOP SENSOR CAUTION ON ECAM INOP

name: HEAT SYS DEGRAD Cautions on ECAM EWD
rem: May be inoperative for two flights provided APU and APU generator are operative.

name: HEAT SYS DEGRAD Cautions on ECAM EWD
rem: (M) One may be inoperative provided: 1. APU and APU generator are operative, and 2. CFDS does not report an ENGXD-0720-BDCV TEST ABORT COUNT message.

name: HEAT SYS DEGRAD Cautions on ECAM EWD
rem: (O) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS), may be inoperative provided APU and APU generator are operative.
oproc: Extended Operations (ETOPS) not authorized.
mproc: Check that CFDS does not report an ENGXD-0720-BDCV TEST ABORT COUNT message.

name: System A
rem: NO RELIEF APPROVED. Note: A and B output (or input) leads from the exciter boxes may be swapped in order to restore System A operative. Reference MEL item 74–31–01B for possible relief.

name: System B
rem: (O) (M) May be inoperative provided: 1. System A is operative, and 2. Associated start valve system operates normally.

name: System A
rem: (O) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS), may be inoperative on one engine only.

name: System B
rem: (O) May be inoperative on one or both engines provided System A operates normally.

name: System A and B
rem: (O) Except for Extended Operations (ETOPS), two ignitors may be inoperative provided they are not on the same engine. Note: On PW1100 engines, system A must be considered inoperative on both engines if the common power supply line from 401XP 115VAC ESS BUS is inoperative as a result of either a loss of electrical continuity or a short circuit (C/B Engine/1 AND 2 IGN/SYS A on the 49VU panel located at A03) is tripped).
oproc: 1. Ensure the following has been accomplished for the associated engine: ● For Engine 1: on C/B panel 121VU: pull and secure circuit breaker ENGINE/IGN/ENG1/SYS B location P41. ● For Engine 2: on C/B panel 121VU: pull and secure circuit breaker ENGINE/IGN/ENG2/SYS B location P42. 2. Start the affected engine using the procedure in Volume 1 A320 5.7.1 Engine Start - Manual 74-31-01C, 74-31-01E 1. Extended Operations (ETOPS) is not authorized. 2. Start the affected engine using the procedure in Volume 1 A320 5.7.1 Engine Start - Manual CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: Engine 1 Selected Igniter
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided IGN FAULT warning is verified to operate normally on ECAM.

name: Engine 2 Selected Igniter
placard: IGNITER INDICATION ON LOWER DU INOP IGNITER INDICATION ON LOWER DU INOP
rem: (M) May be inoperative provided IGN FAULT warning is verified to operate normally on ECAM.
mproc: To verify IGN FAULT warning is operating normally: 1. Start the affected engine(s): A. If engine 1 is affected, pull the following circuit breakers: i. On C/B panel 49VU: pull circuit breaker ENGINE/1 AND 2/IGN/SYS A location A03. ii. For all aircraft other than the A321NEO, on C/B panel 121VU: pull circuit breaker ENGINE/IGN/ENG1/SYS A BAT location P39. iii. On C/B panel 121VU: pull circuit breaker ENGINE/IGN/ENG1/SYS B location P41. iv. Check on ECAM upper display that IGN FAULT warning is triggered. v. On C/B panel 49VU: close circuit breaker ENGINE/1 AND 2/IGN/SYS A location A03. vi. For all aircraft other than the A321NEO, on C/B panel 121VU: close circuit breaker ENGINE/IGN/ ENG1/SYS A BAT location P39. vii. On C/B panel 121VU: close circuit breaker ENGINE/IGN/ENG1/SYS B location P41. B. If engine 2 is affected, pull the following circuit breakers: i. On C/B panel 49VU: pull circuit breaker ENGINE/1 AND 2/IGN/SYS A location A03. ii. For all aircraft other than the A321NEO, on C/B panel 121VU: pull circuit breaker ENGINE/IGN/ENG2/SYS A BAT location P40. iii. On C/B panel 121VU: pull circuit breaker ENGINE/IGN/ENG2/SYS B location P42. iv. Check on ECAM upper display that IGN FAULT warning is triggered. v. On C/B panel 49VU: close circuit breaker ENGINE/1 AND 2/IGN/SYS A location A03. vi. For all aircraft other than the A321NEO, on C/B panel 121VU: close circuit breaker ENGINE/IGN/ ENG2/SYS A BAT location P40. vii. On C/B panel 121VU: close circuit breaker ENGINE/IGN/ENG2/SYS B location P42.
ptext: IGNITER INDICATION ON LOWER DU INOP IGNITER INDICATION ON LOWER DU INOP

name: IGN FAULT Caution on ECAM
rem: (M) One may be inoperative provided associated ignition system is verified to operate normally ONCE EACH FLIGHT DAY (Log Book Entry Required). Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: IGN FAULT Caution on ECAM
rem: (M) Two may be inoperative provided associated ignition system is verified to operate normally PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required). Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: IGN FAULT Caution on ECAM
placard: IGN FAULT CAUTIONS INOP IGN FAULT CAUTIONS INOP IGN FAULT CAUTIONS INOP
rem: One or two may be inoperative.
mproc: ONCE EACH FLIGHT DAY: 1. Verify the associated ignition system operates normally by performing AMM Task 74-31-00-040-043 on the A319/ A320 aircraft or AMM Task 74-31-00-040-001 for the A321 aircraft. 74-31-03B PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE 1. Verify the associated ignition system operates normally by performing AMM Task 74-31-00-040-043 on the A319/ A320 aircraft or 74-31-00-040-001 for the A321 aircraft.

name: Buffer Air Check Valve
rem: One may be inoperative in open position. Note: ENG 1(2) AIR MINOR FAULT alert is triggered when the Buffer Air Check Valve is failed in the open position.

name: TCC/TACC Valve
rem: (O) (M) One may be inoperative in closed position provided the affected engine takeoff EGT margin is greater than 16°C.
oproc: 1. Monitor EGT on affected engine during all phases of flight. 75-25-01 TCC/TACC VALVE EFFECTIVITY A321NEO FLIGHT PLANNING Increase BLOCK FUEL by 2.4%. REQUIREMENTS NOTES None AWABS Performance corrections have been applied. TAKEOFF TAKEOFF PERFORMANCE None WORKSHEET LANDING None
mproc: 1. For an inoperative ACC/TACC valve in the CLOSED position, ensure that the affected engines takeoff EGT margin is greater than 16º C. A. Use the link to the engine condition report or contact the MCC for assistance. Note: A new EGT margin assessment must be performed in the case of engine malfunction or engine related external event such as a bird strike.

name: Thrust Lever Position Sensor (A319 aircraft) (A320 aircraft) (A321CEO aircraft)
rem: One may be inoperative on one engine provided: 1. The A/THR is operative, 2. Both LGCIU’s are operative, and 3. Repairs are made within 3 flight days.

name: T/R Tertiary Lock Valve
rem: One or more may be failed open.

name: Thrust Reverser Systems (A319 aircraft) (A320 aircraft) (A321CEO aircraft)
rem: (O) (M) One may be inoperative provided: 1. Inoperative reverser is deactivated and secured in the stowed position and no operations or procedures require its use, 2. All stow and deploy switches on the inoperative reverser operate normally, 3. ENG 1 (2) REV PRESSURIZED caution is not present on ECAM after deactivation, 4. Wheel brake tachometers operate normally, 5. Main wheel braking system operates normally, and 6. For wet runway operation, applicable performance corrections for Thrust Reverser Inoperative are applied for takeoff. Note: This item is AD related. KJAC (Jackson Hole Airport) operations Authorized on dry runways only. KEYW (Key West) operations Not Authorized

name: Thrust Reverser Systems
rem: (O) (M) One may be inoperative provided: 1. Inoperative reverser is deactivated and secured in the stowed position and no operations or procedures require its use, 2. ENG 1(2) REV INHIBITED caution is displayed on ECAM EWD after deactivation, 3. ENG 1(2) REV UNLOCKED caution is not present on ECAM EWD after deactivation, 4. ENG 1(2) REV PRESSURIZED caution is not present on ECAM EWD after deactivation, 5. Wheel brake tachometers operate normally, 6. Main wheel braking system operates normally, 7. For wet runway operation, applicable performance corrections for Thrust Reverser Inoperative are applied for takeoff. Note: This item is AD related. KJAC (Jackson Hole Airport) operations Authorized on dry runways only. KEYW (Key West) operations Not Authorized

name: Thrust Reversers Inhibition Relay Contacts
rem: (O) (M) Two contacts may be inoperative provided: 1. They are on the same engine, and 2. The associated reverser is deactivated and deferred per MEL 78-30-01A
oproc: 1. Comply with MEL Item 78-30-01 (O) procedure.
mproc: 1. Defer associated Thrust Reverser inoperative per MEL Item 78-30-01A.

name: T/R Indications
rem: (O) (M) One may be inoperative provided the associated T/R is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 78-30-01A, or 78-30-01B as applicable.
oproc: 1. Comply with MEL 78-30-01A, or 78-30-01B (O) procedures as applicable.
mproc: 1. Defer associated Thrust Reverser inoperative per MEL 78-30-01A, or 78-30-01B as applicable.

name: REVERSER CTL FAULT Caution on ECAM EWD
rem: (O) One may be inoperative provided: 1. Wheel brake tachometers operate normally, 2. Main wheel braking system operates normally, 3. Associated thrust reverser system is considered inoperative and deferred per MEL 78-30-01B, and 4. Performance corrections are applied. Note: This item is AD related.
oproc: 1. For a landing conducted with REVERSER CTL FAULT on one side, ensure that both engine thrust levers are retarded to the IDLE detent for the flare and the touchdown. Select both thrust levers to reverse when applying reverse thrust. 2. Comply with MEL 78-30-01B (O) procedure. 78-31-01 REVERSER CTL FAULT CAUTION ON ECAM EWD EFFECTIVITY A321NEO FLIGHT PLANNING None REQUIREMENTS NOTES 1. AWABS Takeoff data is provided for DRY or WET runway conditions only. Takeoffs using ICY, QCTR or HCTR data are not authorized. AWABS Performance corrections have been applied. TAKEOFF Manual WDR not permitted with MEL 78-31-01. PERFORMANCE WORKSHEET

name: REV MINOR FAULT Caution on ECAM EWD
rem: One or both may be inoperative:

name: Oil Quantity Indications
rem: (M) One may be inoperative provided: 1. Associated oil quantity is verified PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE (Log Book Entry Required), 2. There is no evidence of abnormal consumption or leakage, and 3. Associated ENG OIL PRESS, OIL TEMP indications operate normally. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
mproc: 1. Inspect engine exterior for evidence of oil leakage. 2. Check the aircraft log for evidence of normal oil pressure, oil temperature and oil pressure warning system operation for the affected engine. 3. Contact Maintenance Control to verify that engine oil consumption is not excessive. 4. PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE: A. Verify that affected engine oil tank is properly serviced with approved lubricating oil. See AMM Ref. 12–13–79, 300 series pgs. Figure 79-1: CFM56 Oil Tank Sight Gage CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: OIL LO PR Warning on ECAM
rem: (O) One may be inoperative. Note: Simultaneous illumination of the BLUE ELEC PUMP pb-sw FAULT light and the ENG 1(2) PUMP pb-sw FAULT light with engines off may be due to a failed engine oil low pressure switch. In this case, the ENG 1(2) OIL LO PR alert is inoperative, not displayed and dispatch is not possible.

name: OIL LO PR Warning on ECAM
rem: (O) Both may be inoperative provided RCDR GND CTL is verified ON AFTER EACH ENGINE START. Note: Simultaneous illumination of the BLUE ELEC PUMP pb-sw FAULT light and the ENG 1(2) PUMP pb-sw FAULT light with engines off may be due to a failed engine oil low pressure switch. In this case, the ENG 1(2) OIL LO PR alert is inoperative, not displayed and dispatch is not possible. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: 1. Periodically monitor oil pressure indication on ECAM ENG page. 2. Disregard any aural warning, red flashing MASTER WARN light, or warning message associated with the inoperative ENG OIL LO PR ECAM indication. 79-33-02B 1. Periodically monitor oil pressure indication(s) on ECAM ENG page. 2. Disregard any aural warning, red flashing MASTER WARN light, or warning message associated with the inoperative ENG OIL LO PR ECAM indication. 3. AFTER EACH ENGINE START: A. RCDR GND CTL – CHECK ON.

name: OIL FILTER CLOG Indications on ECAM Engine System Page
rem: (M) One may be inoperative provided a check for filter clogging is made ONCE EACH FLIGHT DAY (Log Book Entry Required). Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: OIL FILTER CLOG Indications on ECAM Engine System Page
placard: FILTER CLOG INDICATION INOP FILTER CLOG INDICATION INOP
rem: One may be inoperative.
mproc: 1. ONCE EACH FLIGHT DAY: A. CHECK OF OIL SCAVENGE FILTER CLOGGING INDICATOR. i. On panel 115VU, install a warning notice to tell persons not to start the engine 1 (2). ii. Make sure that the engine 1 (2) has been shut down for at least 5 minutes. iii. On panel 50VU, make sure that the ON legend of the ENG/FADEC GND PWR 1 (2) pushbutton switch is OFF, and install a warning notice. iv. On the A319/A320 aircraft, open left fan cowl door. v. On the A321CEO aircraft, open the oil access door. vi. Check that pops out is not visible on the oil scavenge filter. vii. Close left fan cowl door or oil access door as applicable. viii. Remove warning notices from the panel 115VU and 50VU. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE
ptext: FILTER CLOG INDICATION INOP FILTER CLOG INDICATION INOP

name: OIL FILTER CLOG Caution on
rem: (M) One may be inoperative provided: 1. Associated filter is replaced ONCE EACH FLIGHT DAY (Log Book Entry Required), and 2. Chip detectors are inspected when the filter is replaced and do not reveal the presence of metal chips or other contaminants. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: OIL FILTER CLOG Caution on
rem: (M) One may be inoperative provided: 1. Associated filter is replaced ONCE EACH FLIGHT DAY (Log Book Entry Required), and 2. Visual indicator (pop-out) on the electrical master chip detector is inspected when the filter is replaced and does not reveal the presence of metal chips or other contaminates. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: OIL FILTER CLOG Caution on
rem: (M) One may be inoperative provided: 1. Associated filter is replaced before the next flight and then every 150 flight hours (Log Book Entry Required), and 2. Associated magnetic chip detectors are inspected when the filter is replaced and do not reveal the presence of chips. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: OIL FILTER CLOG Caution on
rem: (M) One may be inoperative provided: 1. Associated filter is replaced before the next flight and then every 150 flight hours (Log Book Entry Required), 2. Associated Oil Debris Monitoring System is verified operative when the filter is replaced, and 3. Associated ENG 1(2) OIL CHIP DETECT was not displayed on the EWD during the previous flight. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: OIL FILTER CLOG Caution on
rem: (M) One may be inoperative provided: 1. Associated ENG 1(2) OIL FILTER PARTLY CLOGGED caution was not displayed on the EWD during the previous flight, 2. Associated magnetic chip detectors are inspected and do not reveal the presence of chips, and 3. Repairs are made within 3 calendar-days or 30 flight-hours, whichever occurs first. PAGE

name: OIL FILTER CLOG Caution on

rem: (M) One may be inoperative provided: 1. Associated ENG 1(2) OIL FILTER PARTLY CLOGGED caution was not displayed on the EWD during the previous flight, 2. Associated ENG 1(2) OIL CHIP DETECTED caution was not displayed on the EWD during the previous flight, 3. Associated Oil Debris Monitoring System is verified operative, and 4. Repairs are made within 3 consecutive calendar- days or 30 flight-hours, whichever occurs first.
mproc: 1. ONCE EACH FLIGHT DAY: A. Change the associated filter and do a check of the Magnetic Chip Detectors ONCE EACH FLIGHT DAY (reference AMM TASK 79–35–00–040–043). 79-35-02B 1. ONCE EACH FLIGHT DAY: A. Replace the main oil supply filter and inspect the visual indicator on the electrical master chip detector by performing AMM Task 79-35-00-040-042 79-35-02C 1. Replace associated oil filter and inspect associated chip detectors per AMM Task 79-35-00-040-802-A. 2. EVERY 150 HOURS: A. Replace associated oil filter and inspect associated chip detectors per AMM Task 79-35-00-040-802-A. 79-35-02D 1. Replace associated oil filter per AMM Task 79-21-06-960-801. 2. Verify associated Oil Debris Monitoring System is operative per AMM Task 79-35-00-040-805-A 3. EVERY 150 HOURS: A. Replace associated oil filter per AMM Task 79-21-06-960-801. 79-35-02E 1. Inspect the associated magnetic chip detectors per AMM Task 79-35-00-040-804-A. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

Associated magnetic chip detectors are inspected when the filter is replaced and do not reveal the presence of chips. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: OIL SENSOR FAULT Caution on ECAM EWD
rem: One may be displayed provided: 1. Associated ENG 1(2) OIL FILTER PARTLY CLOGGED caution was not displayed during previous flight on EWD, and 2. Repairs are made within 3 consecutive calendar- days or 30 flight-hours, whichever occurs first.

name: OIL SENSOR FAULT Caution on ECAM EWD
rem: (M) One may be displayed provided: 1. It is verified before next flight that CFDS does not report any fault related to ODM or PHMU electrical failure, 2. Associated filter is replaced before the next flight and then every 150 flight hours (Log Book Entry Required), and 3. Associated magnetic chip detectors are inspected when filter is replaced and are confirmed to be within limits defined in AMM. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: OIL SENSOR FAULT Caution on ECAM EWD
rem: (M) One may be displayed provided: 1. It is verified before next flight that CFDS does not report any fault related to ODM or PHMU electrical failure, 2. Associated filter is replaced before the next flight and then every 150 flight hours (Log Book Entry Required), 3. Associated ENG 1(2) OIL CHIP DETECTED caution was not displayed on EWD during previous flight. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

Associated magnetic chip detectors are inspected before next flight and then every 75 flight-hours and are confirmed to be within limits defined in AMM (Log Book Entry Required). Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: OIL CHIP DETECTED Caution on ECAM EWD
rem: (M) One may be inoperative on EWD for a maximum of five flights or 10 flight hours, whichever occurs first.
mproc: 1. Perform AMM Task 79-00-00-281-802-A.

name: Valves
rem: (O) (M) One may be inoperative provided: 1. AFTER EACH ENGINE START, valve is manually closed, and 2. Associated igniter system operates normally. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: Valves
rem: (O) (M) One may be inoperative provided: 1. Required shutdown time is checked before starting affected engine, and 2. Valve is manually closed AFTER EACH ENGINE START. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
oproc: 1. Accomplish external operation of the engine start valve procedure as follows: A. Establish communications with mechanic Pushback coordinator. B. Accomplish pushback procedure and checklist. C. Beacon – ON D. ENG MODE selector – IGN/START E. ENG MASTER switch – ON F. When ground crew member is ready: i. Announce to ground crew: OPEN START VALVE AND KEEP OPEN. ii. Monitor engine indications. iii. When N2 is at 50%, announce to ground crew: CLOSE START VALVE. G. Continue with normal procedures. Note: Starter assisted engine relight should be avoided. 80-11-01B BEFORE ENGINE START 1. Before the manual override of the start valve, respect the below minimum time after last engine shutdown: a AIRPORT ELEVATION MINIMUM ENGINE SHUTDOWN TIME (h) (ft above Sea Level) 4,000 and below 9 4,001 to 5,000 9.5 5,001 to 10,000 10 Above 10,000 10.5 CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE

name: ENG MAN START Controls
rem: One or both may be inoperative. Note: Autostarts must be conducted for all engine starts including first flights.

name: FAULT Light on ENGINE MASTER Panel (On Pedistal)
rem: One or both may be inoperative.

name: ECAM Start Valve Position Indicators (Lower Display)
rem: (M) One or both may be inoperative provided AFTER ENGINE START, valve is verified closed. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)

name: ECAM Start Valve Position Indicators (Lower Display)
rem: (M) One or both may be inoperative provided start valve is verified closed AFTER ENGINE START. Repetitive Check Required (Yellow Placard)
mproc: PRIOR TO EACH DEPARTURE: 1. Open access door on right fan cowl. 2. AFTER ENGINE START, ensure that manual handle is in CLOSED position. 3. Close access door. CONTINUED ON NEXT PAGE
